title: wonderful
text: A wonderful hotel for a quick last minute trip. We arrived in the evening to a warm, friendly welcome. The room was clean, spacious and warm. We ate in the bistro. The steak dinner was absolutely sublime-cooked to perfection. It’s not particularly modern or swanky, but it’s clean, comfortable and friendly. Couldn’t ask for anything more fir a very reasonable price. Thank you !

title: We arrived late but we’re made welcome and as always found the accommodation just right.
text: We were warmly welcomed to the hotel by Soli, who quickly found our room and welcomed us back as returning guests

title: Last minute booking - warm welcome
text: My team and I was provided with very good customer service by Parv and Soli

title: The worst hotel I have ever stayed in over 40 countries/hundreds of hotels ,Check ALL the reviews not just the top rated
text:
Where do i start! People can't have stayed at the same hotel as us! Arrived 3.30pm (8C outside so cold). Given a key, corridors stank of sewage, room cold as heating not on, toilet bubbles and emits sewer gasses when room above and to sides flush the toilet. Paper thin walls and ceilings - we could here people farting, adjusting position in bed, TV's, footsteps upstairs (or maybe it was a herd of zebras running around for hours?). Asked reception for help e.g. change of room in a near empty hotel but apparently "there is nothing we can do...". Went into the bar then went back to our room to get jackets as it was so cold (this was 4 hours after we were told heating was going on). Went into restaurant and still needed coats - it had wall to wall radiators that were all switched off and they were relying on what looked like 4 or 5 small portable electric heaters (the type my Gran would use to keep her feet warm) to heat a LARGE area. We got a taxi to eat elsewhere and told the staff why. 2 other guests were also in the bar area with coats on and we did not see anyone eat at the hotel - they all went out! After an awful night listening to other people's bodily functions, sewer smells wafting up from the toilet on each flush we left early the next morning (we had paid for 2 nights but there was no way that was going to happen!!) and told reception exactly what we thought. It is NOT the staff's fault, but the owners or managers need to spend a night in their own caravan like rooms with the heating off and see what its like for paying  guests. This property used to be 5 stars, now its 3 stars and I think 2 of those stars were stolen! The rooms and the toilet operations need to be inspected by environmental health - So glad we didn't eat at the hotel as I wonder what other shortcuts they have been taking!
Do not bother with "dear guest we are sorry to hear that..." rubbish. Fix it for others, we were in room 112. You could, however, refund us for the night we didn't stay!

title: Couple getaway
text:
Amazing. Nice country area. As soon as we checked it. Warmest welcome. Warm lobby.  Very easy check in.  Brilliant value.
Warm rooms. Very spacious. Love the tables and chairs so eating in the room was easy and better. Very big desk area in the room, so my partner can work on the laptop and lots of space all around.
We had breakfast in the morning lots of choice. And dinner too. Food has been fabulous.  Partner got the fish and chips. 
Fish was perfect. As not lots and lots of batter on just a perfect amount. I got steak. I asked med to med well. Sadly got it rare. The lovely waitress got it sorted brilliantly for me.  The staff was so friendly and made sure we were happy.  Definitely coming back 100 %

title: Poor experience. Customer service poor.
text:
Car park on arrival dark, reception was manned. Room tv didnt work first off as being wiped clear of channels(fixed later) ..ladybird  in room kept on appearing. Mentioned and said they had a problem upstairs.
 Sewage smell from toilet that was glugging bubblesall night. Poor breakfast and very expensive. 
The door connecting another room was thin and you could hear what was being said. The light from it also was very bright, they stayed up late.
In all the worst stay at this hotel we have had.
We did mention all this to the staff ...
Would i stay again... NO
Would i recomend..... NO

Have I now sent 5 emils to the hotel and two to their head office. No reply so far.

Breakfast for one contrntal and one cooked was £28. The toast was served 20 mins after I had eaten the food. 
I have even rung them up.. but nothing.

All food was odrdered in advance.  No food appeared until around 8.20.  Mains around 9.00.  This was disruptive for some older folk whose sleep was affected by eating late. There was only ONE waiter.
And some of the food was poor.  Eg Melon boat, my grandsons' starter, was a pile of little melon chunks that I could hardly get my fork into.  And their "pizza" was very disapppointing.  It looked like four triangles of toast with something wierd on top.  Not suitable for a 6 and a 9 yr old.

title: Do not hire function room
text:
Recently used this hotel for a 50th birthday function, however the experience deserves a 1 star review 

The DJ had not turned up, when we questioned the managers they kept saying he will be here in 15 minutes. He arrived 1 hour late with minimal equipment and continued to play unsuitable music, when we spoke to the DJ who appeared quite frustrated informed us he had been booked last minutes and had no idea who or what he was catering for ! Suggesting the hotel had forgotten to arrange a DJ in advance . 
To make matters worse only 1 member of staff serving drinks all evening this was clearly too much for 1 person to deal with. 

We tried to raise these concerns with the on site managers who were very dismissive and showed no empathy or or interest in resolving the issues! 

Overall the event was poorly organised and handled unprofessionally I would not recommend this hotel for hosting any functions.

title: Loved it
text:
I wonder if I stayed in the same hotel as some of the other reviewers. I stayed for 4 nights and was delighted with the service I received. Upon pulling into the large carpark I was greeted by a charming receptionist. Booking in was simple and off I trotted to room 105. Upon entry I was greeted with a nice bathroom with shower over bath and plenty of bright lighting. I do hate a dull bathroom. I moved into the room which is like any other room in a hotel, tv/kettle etc. The bed was a king size, great since it was only me and I had a nice table and chairs to sit at. The bed was absolutely fine but the carpet did look abit old but there were no holes or tears in it. I certainly preferred this room to the travel lodge I’d just stay at in Guildford (never again will I use that brand).

I had paid for breakfast and it was lovely. On week days it is cooked to order, you just stipulate what you want and the quantity of each item. I had 3 sausages and 6 rashers of bacon with lots of mushrooms but a person next to me had smoked salmon. At the week end it was buffet style just like TL & PI and again was fresh and plentiful. I didn’t have the evening menu as was eating out each night but I did notice the restaurant was closed Sunday nights.

I’d certainly recommend this hotel to everyone

title: This hotel / hostel should be condemned
text: I had 1 night here and never again. I came here on a business trip. The first room was awful and had to move rooms because the plug sockets did not work, so they stated they will upgrade me to ab executive room.....!!! Exactly the same type of room, not an upgrade at all. The rooms are dirty and very dated and unsafe. The kettles are rusty inside, so I did not drink from them. The sink in the (executive room, joke) did not have any form of socket or plug / grid, so you could quite easily loose jewellery done it. The smell of the rooms is awful. Dinner again, awful, I had the chicken burger, and the chicken tasted off.  The banging and noise above you does not help when trying to sleep. You only have 2 plug sockets in the room which are the other side of the room, so no good for business use. I think HSE would have a field day at this hotel. Like I said the hotel should be closed down and condemned l. On a positive note, the reception staff were lovely, but you can see they were embarrassed.  The owners should be ashamed with themselves to allow a hotel / hostel get this bad. I would rather sleep in a tent.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Passenger654

Thank you for your feedback! 
We appreciate your kind words about our staff. 
Please email me on khalid@unohotels.com at your convenience, as I'd like to understand more about your experience.

Kindest Regards 
Khalid Sioud

title: Accident waiting to happen
text:
Having stayed here before I was looking forward to staying again but what a mistake

I have seen reviews for the same period (11-14th September) regarding the food I was also refused a refund despite only eating one of the breakfasts paid for due to the quality of the food - inedible. Clearly a theme

The fire evacuation in the middle of the night was chaotic and we were directed to a fire exit that wasn’t the closest and didn’t align to the written fire safety instructions. Also the door we were guided to was locked!

No one outside to co-ordinate where we should go, no one taking names to make sure everyone was outside, and we were then told to re-enter the building before the fire service had even arrived let alone check the building was safe

Again these concerns were raised and dismissed by Souliman the following day despite having experienced everything I have stated above and the number of fire safety breaches they had made. 

I was given the details of Khalid the director (which I will happily share should anyone want them) who has chosen not to acknowledge the concerns raised or the ropey nature of his food

I will be making sure that as many of my equestrian contacts - and there are many - are aware of this in the spirit of duty of care to people I know

ownerResponse.text:

Thank you for sharing your feedback on here and for reaching out directly to me yesterday. 
I truly apologise for the challenges you faced during your stay. We take your concerns very seriously, especially regarding the breakfast quality and fire safety procedures.

I'm glad to have had the opportunity to discuss a resolution with you on my e-mail response yesterday, and I appreciate your patience as we work to rectify these issues. 
With Regards to your your experience during the fire evacuation, I've already engaged our team to review protocols in place and discussed your feedback.

On a positive note, we have had a visit from the local fire brigade,  who were comfortable with our internal procedures and provided us with valuable feedback to enhance our safety measures. We are committed to making necessary improvements for the comfort and safety of our guests and teams.

Thank you once again for your feedback, and I hope we can have the chance to provide you with a much better experience in the future.

Kindest Regards

title: Avoid!!!
text:
I don’t know where to start!

Room itself was clean but I shared my bathroom for the duration of my stay with hair from what I can only imagine to be multiple other people (yes I reported this and yes the room was “cleaned” during my stay). 

The hotel broke more HSE regulations than I thought possible following an early morning fire alarm. All of which have been reported to the local fire authority as the hotel were not interested in discussing sensibly with us. 

Last but not least….breakfast! Wow! What an absolute disaster! I don’t even know where to start. Apart from advising you not to start yours! For your own health! It was revolting. Pretty sure the scrambled egg had been scraped off the car park, the bacon had been cooking longer than my entire stay and as for the black pudding…you could play ice hockey with it!

The hotel management were originally unavailable for discussion, then available, then laughed at us and are now avoiding us and our booking agents. 

What that says about this establishment speaks volumes. 

Avoid!!!

title: Crimes against the Full English
text: Only been here for one night so far but felt I had to share. The plus side , the staff are absolutely lovely , its a bit dated  although very clean , bring your own USB chargers as there none . The down sides are the beds are very hard and pretty uncomfortable but the true crime against food is in the breakfast . The coffee was cold, I asked if we could have some fresh which was duly replaced with the same , my disappointment was rising and then it appeared . Honestly my 7yr old granddaughters would have done better . The mushrooms were deep fried to the point of being brown bullets , the hash browns greasy and horrible and the black pudding couldn't be cut with a knife and the scrambled egg was dripping in grease  . Who ever is in that kitchen should be ashamed of themselves . There is no excuse to dish up such abysmal fayre ,

title: Awful breakfast
text: Hotel looks tired from the outside but friendly and helpful lady in reception offered a nice welcome. Room was clean and comfortable. However, breakfast was the worst I have experienced from a hotel. At £13 each we expected the full English to be freshly cooked. When the plates arrived the food had obviously been on a hot plate for some time. Bacon was like rubber. One small, tasteless, wrinkled sausage. Beans were dried out as if they had been reheated from day before. Worst of all was my wife's scrambled egg which was greasy, undercooked and had a strange smell. It was so bad it totally put her off the rest of her breakfast. Despite the restaurant being virtually empty we were not asked if everything was OK until the plates were cleared. I complained and the manager appeared a short time later. He was full of apologies and offered a free "late check out". We declined this and I asked for a refund of the price of one breakfast but this was refused due to "not our policy!!" Digusting. We won't be back.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear JukeboxSurrey

Thank you for taking the time to share your constructive feedback with us. We greatly appreciate your kind words about our receptionist and the cleanliness and comfort of your room.

However, we sincerely apologise for your experience with the breakfast. We aim to provide high-quality meals, and it is clear we fell short on this occasion. Your comments on the food quality and service will be addressed with the kitchen brigade to ensure improvements are made.

We understand your disappointment regarding the refused refund and appreciate your feedback on our policies. Your insights are invaluable, and we will review them closely.

Should you consider staying with us again, we would love the opportunity to provide you with a much-improved experience.

Thank you once again for your feedback, and look forward to welcoming you back.

Kindest Regards

title: Desperate, dirty, dilapidated and depressing
text:
This is a desperate place. There are staff and management that clearly couldn’t care less about this hotel. The approach is riddled with long grass and weeds everywhere and the front of the hotel is unkempt again with weeds. It’s clear no one thinks that it’s worth making an effort to tidy this place up, this is England after all where we are proud of our gardens and outside areas but clearly not here. 

Inside has no atmosphere - some rooms including meeting room stank of disinfectant or bug spray and on occasions so strong I had to leave the room. 

The young female receptionist was as helpful as she could be but woefully disorganised even when she tried hard. 

Give this hotel a wide berth and look for somewhere else anywhere.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Peter B

Thank you for your feedback. We take all comments seriously and regret to hear that your experience did not meet your expectations.

We are continuously working on improving our facility and the overall guest experience, and your views about the upkeep and atmosphere are valuable to us. We appreciate your recognition of our receptionist's efforts, and we will address the areas you've mentioned to enhance our service.

We hope you might consider giving us another chance in the future to show you our improvements.

Kindest Regards

title: Middle of the road hotel
text:
Stayed at the Hickstead for a few nights while in the area. The hotel is fine, its basic but clean and comfortable for a short stay. The shower was never able to quite get beyond moderately warm - fine for the summer, less so for winter.

Staff were very friendly and helpful throughout the stay.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear KS0197

Hi there,

Thank you for taking the time to share your feedback regarding your recent stay at our hotel. We're pleased to hear that you found the hotel clean and comfortable, and that our staff provided friendly service during your visit.

We appreciate your comments about the shower temperature, as we are always looking for ways to improve our guests' experience. Your input is valuable to us, and we hope to welcome you back in the future for an even more enjoyable stay.

Best regards,

title: Friendly, helpful, very good prices on food and drinks
text: Super friendly guys that work there will do anything you ask, food spot on, beer is tasty and its realistically priced rooms are clean what more could you ask for ! And in a nice and quiet location nice little get away not far from burgesshill if you fancy a meal out or more drinks 😜

We appreciate your feedback about our location being a nice getaway, and we look forward to welcoming you back soon for another enjoyable experience!

Kindest Regards

title: A fab birthday celebration
text: I liaised with Soli for a few weeks organising a 3 course meal for 35 family & friends to celebrate a birthday. He was very helpful & all the arrangements were easy to co-ordinate. Everybody has told me that they thoroughly enjoyed the food & the service was efficient & friendly. I would definitely recommend this venue if you are thinking of organising something similar. They even gave us a special room rate for those of us that stayed overnight.

Thank you so much for your wonderful email! Soli and I are thrilled to hear your positive feedback, and it truly means a lot to us.

title: Worst Hotel i’ve ever stayed in in UK
text: bad experience. We had raw sewerage come up the pipes and into our toilet in the morning. We couldn’t shower as the bath would not drain or use the toilet. As it was the weekend we were told they couldn’t provide a refund but would get back to us. We haven’t heard anything. We paid 313 pound for 1 night .

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Pioneer61876286305

Thank you for your patience as we investigated your concerns. I want to assure you that the issue with the plumbing was a minor one, and we're taking steps to prevent any recurrence.

I sincerely apologise for the inconvenience you faced during your stay. please do drop me an e-mail should you wish to visit again and will ensure all aspects of your stay are looked after.

Kindest Regards

title: Give it a miss !!
text: The first impression gained from a car park and entrance full of 3 months growth of weeds and a “lawn” that was probably last cut in Cromwells time sets the tone for this hotel. Note to the owners, If you’re intent on ripping people off by charging 4 times the normal room rate for Hickstead week at least try and provide a service and experience worthy of that rip off price. No tonic at the bar and complete apathy and disinterest from a night manager with the people skills of a hungry alligator, breakfast that was best avoided after the first mornings experience and the previous occupants medication left in the drawer in the room. It was Fawlty Towers without the benefit of the humour !!! Avoid unless you’re absolutely STUCK or happy to stay in a below par establishment that has zero interest in its customers.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear 779brendanI.

Thank you for your review.

I would be pleased if you would contact me directly on khalid@unohotels.com to allow me to discuss the matter directly with you.

Kindest Regards

title: A very poor experience….
text: Decided to eat here as it was close to Hickstead Showground. Alarm bells should have rung when we arrived and the car park/outdoor areas were overgrown and generally unkempt. After waiting at the bar for 5 minutes with no one appearing to serve we asked at reception if we could have a table for dinner, we were seated at a dirty table in a very dated, empty dining room. Ordered bottle of pinot Grigio, were then given wine glasses so dirty you couldn’t see through them. Wrong wine was brought to the table, then to be told they had ran out of Pinot Grigio. We ordered gin  and tonic instead, waitress came back to the table embarrassed and explained they had run out of tonic! On the menu the nachos were served with salsa, guacamole and sour cream. When they arrived we asked where the sour cream was and apparently they had run out and chef had replaced it with yoghurt! The cheese was so burnt that the nachos were almost inedible. Overall a terrible experience from start to finish.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Get_away_fr0m_it_all,

Thank you for your feedback regarding your recent experience. I'm really sorry to hear about the issues you faced during your visit. We take such matters seriously and would love to make it right. If you’re willing to give us another chance, we’d be delighted to invite you back for a much-improved experience.

Kindest Regards
Khalid

title: Not the best place to stay
text: Booking in was terrible, only one person booked on reception so it took ages and then there is confusion from booking.com as they seem to say they have taken the payment but they have not. Also just display the WiFi code somewhere. It’s Hickstead in case you can’t find it.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Steven D.

Thank you for your review.

I would be pleased if you would contact me directly on khalid@unohotels.com to allow me to discuss the matter directly with you.

Kindest Regards

title: A wonderfully warm welcome
text:
A wonderfully warmth welcome to a charming hotel hidden down a lane! Booking in we were allocated a room on the first floor, as I have mobility problems I asked if a ground floor room could be arranged. No problem, all done with a smile. The room was spacious and comfortable, not too warm as the window had been opened. We visited the bar, served by the receptionist who appeared to be the only member of staff on duty who apologised that there was no choice of draught beer as there had been a wedding reception the previous day ( obviously drunk the bar dry of beer !) No deliveries on Sunday but the only beer on draught proved to be acceptable. My wine was certainly fine and the prices were reasonable.
There was an exceptionally good breakfast on offer in very pleasant surroundings. The waiter was attentive and prompt in his service. The only quibble would be the offering of marmalade which was the usual little packet of something more like a jelly! At a cost of £12.00 per person it was quite expensive but given the quality and quantity of items on offer well worth it.
This was a very pleasant overnight stay and would certainly visit again

ownerResponse.text:
Dear LindaH157

Thank you very much for sharing your lovely experience at the Hickstead Hotel! We’re thrilled to hear that you received a warm welcome and that we were able to accommodate your request for a ground floor room. It’s great to know that you found the room spacious and comfortable.

We appreciate your understanding regarding the limited draught beer selection due to the wedding reception. We always strive to provide our guests with a variety of options, and we're glad the beer and wine you had met your expectations.

We're particularly pleased that you enjoyed the breakfast and found the service attentive. Your feedback about the marmalade is noted, and we will certainly look into offering an alternative that better meets our guests' preferences.

Thank you again for your kind words and for choosing to stay with us. We look forward to welcoming you back for another pleasant stay in the future!

Kindest Regards
Khalid Sioud

title: An unexpected delight
text:
We booked the  hotel simply because we needed a dog friendly, budget option that was convenient for an event, but it far exceeded our expectations. Rooms are spacious and bathrooms sparkling clean. We had asked for adjacent rooms  and  were given two  interconnecting rooms which was even better. 

The two standouts though were the staff and the food.

We arrived late so ate in the Oak Bistro simply for convenience. The menu was uninspiring so the delicious steaks that arrived  were a wonderful surprise .Breakfast was also far better than anticipated - and at least as good ifnot better, than at some hotels five times the price.

What really sets the hotel apart though are the staff. Everyone we came into contact with was charming and helpful. The nothing is too much trouble approach creates a really welcoming atmosphere.

As expected at this price point, not everything was perfect. The free wifi frequently dropped, the mattresses are a bit unforgiving and there are no sockets by the bed. These small negatives were easily outweighed by all the other things about the hotel that are great.

We stayed out at The Hickstead out of necessity, but would happily return by choice.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Emma W.

I would like to thank you for writing such a great review of your time here at the Hickstead House Hotel.
I was thrilled to rad this fantastic feedback and glad you enjoyed the food and found our staff to be excellent.

We look forward to welcome you back hopefully very soon.

Kindest Regards

title: The Hickstead hotel, not as good as at first looks
text:
We arrived a bit before check in time which is 3 pm however we had to wait till 4:30 before our room was ready for us. The room itself was very tired and in need of a makeover, the bed was very hard could have done with a soft mattress topper or ideally a new mattress. The wifi was terrible couldn’t get more than ten mins of wifi before it logged off again.

On a positive note the staff were very friendly and the meals in the restaurant were very good.

Not sure I could recommend this hotel or would stay again shame because it promised so much.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Val B.

Thank you very much for your feedback and score; I am really sorry about the delayed check-in and issues with the room - that's definitely not the experience we want for our guests.

You will be pleased to hear that we have upgraded our WIFI facilities so to exceed the evolving needs of our guests.

We will all agree that our staff make all the difference and I will definitely pass along your praise.

Thank you and please do stay again.

Kindest Regards

title: Satisfied
text:
Vacation greenway Paris London by bike with friends. Stop at the Hickstead Hotel. Very warm welcome from the manager. Placement of bikes in a safe place and very friendly discussion with the manager who speaks French. Very good quality rooms and catering.
We will keep a very good memory and recommend the stopover.

ownerResponse.text:
Merci beaucoup pour votre commentaire sur notre hôtel V1751JDmartineg. 
Je suis heureux d'apprendre que vous avez apprécié votre séjour parmi nous. C'est une chose dont notre personnel, notamment the Gerant est très fier, c'est pourquoi nous apprécions vos commentaires. 
Nous espérons que vous reviendrez nous rendre visite. 
Je vous prie d'agréer, Madame, Monsieur, l'expression de mes salutations distinguées

title: A discovery...
text:
We were invited to a party  at the burgiss hill football ground. Hickstead was the ferfect place to stay. very friendly staff notably the lebanese manager, so helpful.
Nice big bedroom, very quiet. Lovely breakfast. No teaspoons on the table to strir our tea or coffee??? The menu in the restaurant could be updated to some more gourmet food, was a bit food from the seventies. I believe that the restaurant could be the place to go by even a  good chef, cos the location is great.

We look forward to welcoming you back soon!

Warmest regards,

title: Great south coast stay
text: Arrived late in the evening and was kindly great by the night reception start, big room and well stocked bar! Will be staying again. Big car park with quick access to  the motor way, London, Brighton and Gatwick airport. Reasonably priced too

title: Staff and food are outstanding
text:
This is the third time I have stayed here and I have to say, whatever they have changed in the kitchen has worked. Without doubt one of the most flavoursome delicious meals I have had in a long time. Value for money is beyond contestation. 

The staff are outstanding and will always try and do their best for you, a credit to the hotel. 

The rooms are always clean and tidy. Its not modern or designer but it's great for a couple of nights. The food and staff make up for it. Well worth staying here.

Kellie Mason 
General Manager

title: Overnight stay
text:
Just returned from an overnight stay at The Hickstead. We requested a quiet room and given a room overlooking the car park, which was quite busy as they had a function on. 

We booked Dinner in the Oak tree bistro which has not changed since we were there 8 years ago. 

Dinner was superb so compliments to the chef couldn’t fault it at all, but my fault is in the restaurant, bright lighting no atmosphere or candles on the table, which would create a better ambience and possibly attract more guests to eat in there. 
No upselling to encourage guests to spend more or encourage them to have a nightcap in the bar. 

It has now become a dog friendly hotel which I don’t have an issue with but it appears to be the main focus of the hotel! 

The rooms were very hot yet the restaurant was chilly.

 Kellie Mason - General Manager

title: Dog happy, guests happy !
text: This was our 4th stay as it is very handily located for visiting family. Our stay there was the best so far and that was due to the very helpful and friendly staff. It has always been a good stay but the staff were noticeably a few notches above those on our previous stays. They are very dog friendly and even have a dog VIP photo wall. Room was clean and comfortable however our only feedback is that although we prefer to be at the back of the hotel the gravel staff / delivery car park makes for quite a noisy time of it when vehicles do park there or leave although this was not often but for some reason this seemed to occur between 11pm and 12pm when trying to get to sleep. Not a big complaint, just an observation. We had breakfast twice which was good and the service excellent, the dog even got a sausage! The ground are also excellent for giving the dog a walk, nice and secure away from main roads. Thank you, we will be back soon.

We look forward to welcoming you and your dog back to the hotel very soon.
Kind regards,

title: Comfortable stay
text:
I stayed for just one night at the Hickstead Hotel, the staff were very welcoming on arrival, I was a little disappointed you could not get a snack in the bar in the afternoon, but they did arrange a sandwich for me.

Allocated a nice room on ground floor, well furnished with good desk area and reasonable WIFI, bathroom facilities were good, comfy bed with spotless linen and towels. Got a good night’s sleep.

The TV in my room was not working properly with “poor signal” appearing, while trying to watch football ☹

Never used the restaurant in the evening as was out but breakfast was good being cooked to order.

All in all a very nice stay for a 3 star hotel, well done!

Kellie Mason - General Manager

title: Second stay there and I will return
text:
My stay at the Hickshead hotel has been excellent in many respects, especially the helpfulness of the staff and their proactive attitudes. The Hotel is very convenient, the food is good and as I say the Staff make this place somewhere I will return. 
Unfortunately if you have a room facing the carpark not only do you get woken up at silly o'clock by patrons chatting in the carpark (not the Hotel's fault at all) but also by the smell of cigarette smoke coming in through the window, as patrons that are staying in the Hotel sit on the picnic tables to smoke.  Yes I did have to get up and close the window but then the room is too warm, catch 22. Other than that I would recommend staying there as it was very chilled and accommodating. Thank you Hickstead hotel.

Some of the decor is a little dated but everything is in good order / super clean & you can’t fault the location for anyone competing at Hickstead!

title: Fawlty Towers is better
text: First the staff - they are not at fault and are helpful nice people. The hotel...where do you start, old, tired, carpets scruffy & stained, bedding feels stale, cant open the windows (Health & safety) so stuffy and airless, towels the same as the carpets. Decent shower to be fair and parking is great but burgers like ice hockey pucks, salt overload and a chef that went home before we could order dessert at 9.30! Breakfast average and for the price this isnt worth the money. Only stayed there because I couldnt get in my usual hotel and it was a great reminder of why we stopped using it.

title: Not good….avoid.
text:
Used this hotel on business pre pandemic and it has gone downhill.
Rooms are dated. Beds very hard. Bathrooms tired.
Restaurant…….not good at all. We were a group of 8 with only 5 other people in the restaurant. Food took ages to arrive. Burgers were like ice hockey pucks. Fish and chips dry. The worst thing was everything seemed be cover in salt….not nice at all. Then at 9.20 pm we asked for desert and were told that the chef had shut the kitchen…..unbelievable!
The only good thing about the hotel is the plentiful free parking when you stay there.

title: A fabulous stay at a first class hotel
text: We stayed at the hotel, as we were attending a wedding nearby. Well, I have to say that the hotel was absolutely first class. The staff were fabulous and super friendly and helpful. The rooms are very well appointed and included a trouser press. There was even a shoe cleaning set. I would recommend this hotel to anyone.

title: Business
text:
I booked this room specifically as their website said they did 24 hour room service and I knew I wouldn't get to eat until around 2300 due to working.
Got back at 2300 and was told the kitchen closed at 2100 and they didn't do 24 hour room service, showed the manager that it was on their site. Was told that the bar was available 24 hours and this is classed as room service!
Was also told that if I knew I was going to be late I could have told them and they would have had something there, why would I do this when it said 24 hour room service.
When I asked for Head Office details, was told by the manager all complaints come to me.
Went to bed very hungry, will not be returning and I do a lot of visits to Hickstead as work in the Horse world.

title: Wedding
text: I would just like to thank all the staff for thier wonderful service on our wedding day. Everything was so perfect and all the guests were very happy .The food was amazing and the rooms were very clean and comfortable.

title: Room filthy
text: The staff fabulous couldn't fault them, BUT the room omg it stunk so fusty to the point it made us feel sick the bedding & the decorative pillows & bed throw stunk so bad 👎 like it had just been fetched from a charity shop...the curtains when opened stains all up the back of the lining the windows & window sills was filthy,  i just wanted to leave so bad, tv didn't work no aircon or fan the room was so hot didn't sleep most of the night. the fusty smell was even worse from the umidity..would i stay here again Absolutely NOT could not wait for morning so we could leave? Left 2 hours early couldn't even eat the breakfast because could smell the fusty stinking room on our clothes the smell was awful 🤢 room needs a good clean and bedding washing & everything disinfecting

title: lovely staff poor food and rooms in need of update
text: The staff were all cheerful and helpful, however, our room was dark and airless, the bedding was low quality, foam pillows and cheap duvet. On our second day, our room was not cleaned and tea making items not replaced, loo paper had run out , bin not emptied and bed unmade and towels not replaced. The over bath shower had a cheap curtain that needed replacing, it didn't reach far enough down, to stop water running onto the bathroom floor. A staircase ran over our room so we were kept awake at night by constant running up and down. The food was dissapointing. I had chicken pie with mash and vegetables which arrived barely warm. I returned it and asked for a salad sandwich, which I was told, they did not do. I had an egg sandwich which was made with the cheapest supermarket, thin white bread, which I didn't eat. The breakfast bread was at least wholemeal, but again, the thin, cheap supermarket type. The scrambled egg that my husband had,was tough and unpleasant. No chef worth the name would ever produce such poor quality food so I assume they don't have one. Whilst I accept that Covid has hit hard in many places, this low level of experience really is not acceptable . We were expecting at the very least, decent quality food and a comfortable bed to sleep in. The hairdyer provided was fixed at the back of a drawer and required the on switch to be held down to keep working and the flex was much too short, making the whole experience very difficult. There were only very shallow and narrow drawers in the room, only big enough for socks and other very small items of clothing and the hanging space in the wardrobe was too short for dresses, meaning that the bottom 6 inches of my dresses were crumpled. Fine for men who just have trousers and shirts, but not good for us ladies whose dresses need more space to hang.All in all, not something we would want to repeat.The online pictures of this hotel show a large, impressive Edwardian property, but the rooms are in a tatty annex next to that. We were expecting big airy rooms, what we got was a million miles away from that, low ceilinged, gloomy and airless.

ownerResponse.text:
Hello slittlejohnglass, 
Thank you for making this recent review of your stay here with us.
We appreciate your comments and will be looking to take corrective action on the points made for future bookings.

title: Nice place, with a few pennies needed to be spent perhaps.
text:
Fantastically located for commuting to all of West and East Sussex. Lovely building with huge grounds and ample parking. Very safe for the kids and very close to Burgess hill and Hayward’s heath if to live need a supermarket or somewhere different to eat.

The staff are really lovely and made our stay very comfortable. The receptionist upon check-in was very helpful arranging a return taxi for us all as we were in the area for a family wedding. We didn’t make use of the restaurant but the menu looked fresh with a few good options.

2 night stay was reasonable in terms of cost for a “room only” status.

A couple of small issues we needed to bring to the attention but we’re dealt with during and after our stay with some really good post communication.

With a little more investment perhaps in the rooms, bringing them up to date, this place will be a lovely place to return too.

Thanks

ownerResponse.text:
Hello Smt2479,

title: Great location for Hickstead showjumping ground
text: Our granddaughter and her horse had qualified in our area for the national schools  competition to show jump in the final at The All England Jumping Course at Hickstead. As we live nearly 300 miles away we wanted to stay local to the show ground and required a hotel the was dog friendly. The Hickstead hotel fitted that requirement being just 5 minutes from the venue. All the staff we encountered were friendly and helpful from reception to restaurant. The bedroom was on the ground floor and was a good size with tea and coffee making facilities. The room was starting to look tired and the room was not well insulated as we heard guests upstairs walking about above us and also in the corridor. The price for B & B and the dog was good value and the cooked breakfast was plentiful. The hotel is set away from the main road so you are not disturbed by passing traffic and is surrounded by large gardens ideal for walking the dog. There are plenty of parking spaces around the hotel

title: Anti social guests
text: Very noisy anti social guests in room next to me who told me to f off you f...ing bitch when I asked them to be quiet at 2.30am. Poor quality sound insulation, banging doors, tired decor. Staff pleasant but ineffectual in managing situation. I won't be returning.

title: In need of and update.
text:
This was my third of fourth stay here and while it was ok, it really was just OK. £140 B and B was average price. 
It really could be a nice hotel with just a few updates. My room was well sized, but dated, with a tv and remote with the back missing, threadbare carpet and hairs in the bath, showing it hadn’t been thoroughly cleaned. 
Having said that the room was clean. 
Breakfast which costs £15. (Part of the £140) was not as good as I have experienced there before. 
No one to greet and find a table for you, tea and coffee now served in pump urns, which didn’t work properly. Toast and full English was ok, sausages were a highlight! And Heinz ketchup was on offer. 
All in all ok… but I think if Premier Inn had rooms, that would be my choice over here.

title: Wedding at Fawlty Towers
text: We booked our wedding for June 2021 but had to move it due to covid. The hotel were very helpful and initially in touch to discuss all the arrangements but it went downhill after our Co-Ordinator went on maternity leave.  We had to chase relentlessly and whilst Karen was an improvement on her predecessor there were things that were completely missed in the set up.   The room I stayed in the night prior to the wedding left a lot to be desired with ripped bedding and torn net curtains. The day of the wedding we were served an inedible breakfast that had been under a heat lamp but to be fair there was only one lady serving who was also covering reception.  A friend of mine had to move all the cleaning equipment away from the front of the hotel as it was such an eyesore. The wedding went smoothly and the dinner was good but there was items that they got wrong on the menu which was disappointing.  There was also a dreadful loud buzzing coming from a fridge from the bar at the back of the room.    In the evening the bar in the function room was not used as promised and everyone had to use the bar in the lounge which was tiny.  There was no explanation to this and we had been promised that both bars would be open and there  would be no queuing for drinks which did not happen.  The staff became incredibly rude at the end of  the night - it felt like they had had our money and we were no longer important to them.  We were told that the bar would be open to all hotel guests but this was shut at 100am by a very grumpy member of staff who said that he would need a water cannon to make the guests leave and go to bed!  Absolutely appalling!    Obviously the wedding rates there are cheaper than other hotels but there is a good reason for this as the hotel is dilapidated and needs an overhaul.  A huge Covid infection for a third of the guests was the cherry on the cake - not blaming the hotel for that but it didnt help having over 130 people crammed in their lounge bar!!!

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Saharaharahara,

Thank you for your review unfortunately we did have a slight change of personel, i am sorry to hear that you had found that difficult in regards to having to chase up.

I appreciate your comments and shall look into why the service was not up to our usual service standards. I am pleased to hear though that your meal was good even if it was slightly different than expected.

Unfortunately on the night we did have issues with some of our electrical equiptment, which hampered our service standards due to no fault of our own.

As for the comment made by the staff member I shall have to investigate this further, as such comments are completely unacceptable.

I am sorry to hear about some of your guests. We do provide hand sanitiser stations throughout and masks should guests require them. As well as keep high volume areas ventilated.

Thank you again for the feedback,
Alex Karollus

title: Faulty Towers
text: Recently went to a wedding. (Half the wedding contracted covid), although this was no doubt carried in by a guest the rooms were tropical with heating on full whack all day and night/stifling. The rooms are quite tired. Ours had ripped net curtains and the inter joining room had rips in the duvet covers. We ordered champagne the morning of wedding to brides room which arrived after some time but with no glasses or the orange juice we had asked for with it, these turned up 30 mins later. The breakfast (bearing in mind there were 4 of us and one couple and a single man dining) had been left to sit under a heat lamp for ages so everything was dry to the point the beans had a thick skin on them. The toast we ordered with breakfast turned up 30 mins after we’d finished and the butter another 20 mins after that. I think there were literally 2 staff members doing everything. They were pleasant but when you are charging money for a wedding at your establishment you need to have more staff. They looked knackered and obviously were working far too long hours. There were rubbish bags and mops and buckets from the day before in the front porch if hotel that one of us ended up clearing away so the guests would nit be greeted with it when they arrived. The bar in the room of wedding was supposed to be open but was not. All in all it was all a bit like faulty towers. Place needs a lick of paint, sprucing up  and more staff.

ownerResponse.text:

Thank you for bringing the previous items to my attention in order for me to investigate.

I do appologise that on this occasion we had not met our usual standards.

title: Avoid
text:
Short review: Wish we'd booked the Travelodge on the roundabout - £80 cheaper for 2 nights including breakfast.
Long review:
FOOD:  no idea.  At check-in we were informed the kitchen was closed for the duration and breakfast (rate was B & B) would be delivered in a bag containing room-temperature orange juice, instant porridge, toffee yoghurt (for breakfast?), a yoghurt covered cereal bard and a croissant, plastic cutlery and no plate.  Yum!
ROOM:
Choice of views consists of car park or the Bolney Industrial Estate, which is great if listening to revving engines all day and evening (until 10:30pm) on a Saturday is your thing.  Hardly "Set in peaceful Sussex countryside nestling beneath the Downs" advertised on website.
Room was spacious... but decor reminiscent of 1980's mid-range hotel.  Carpet stained.
Bathroom: Shower had good water pressure and a 'quiet close' lid on toilet seat.  Wish the doors had been 'quiet close'... they slam... extremely loudly
TV - small and located as far away from bed as possible.  Freeview but would only access ITV channels and E4 (aside from shopping channels), which is great if Midsomer Murder & Friends repeats are all you want.
Tea/Coffee:  only decaf in room.
No room service.
Bed:  Uncomfortable - mattress with collapsed at edges.
COVID procedures:  Signage goes to great lengths to insist that guests wear face coverings over "Nose & Mouth", but this policy evidently doesn't apply to staff.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear EJPKB,
 
Thank you for taking the time to leave a review of your recent stay at The Hickstead Hotel.
 
I am sorry to hear that you were not entirely happy and It is disappointing to read your comments as during your stay nothing was brought to our attention where I am sure we could have tried to rectify your concerns where possible.
 
We have currently made the decision to keep the restaurant closed which is why we are only selling room only or an option with a breakfast bag delivered to your room. We are also calling and emailing all guests prior to arrival to advise accordingly. 

Since the pandemic began the team at the hotel have always followed Covid rules & advice as required and I know they take great pride in ensuring they are compliant. 

We do value all our customer's feedback which I can assure you I will be reviewing with the team whilst also implementing any corrective actions as appropriate

I do hope we get the opportunity to welcome you back again to the hotel in the future where I am sure you will experience us in a better light.

Kind Regards
Paul

title: This Hotel needs attention !!!
text: Stayed here for a single night, have been a regular on and off over the years, and while the staff and food were good, this hotel badly needs some proper investment and TLC. It’s in a perfect location close to Hickstead Show Jumping Arena, up the road from the new expansion in Burgess Hill, it could be a premier location for many local businesses and location, but sadly it’s very tired. It needs investment in just about every area. From the reception areas (the always prevalent faint sewage whiff), to the carpets fixtures and fittings bedrooms, bathrooms and water pressure issues (which we were told about on check out - when shower flow was  complained about - very handy!) the food in the restaurant was good, as was the service and staff very polite. Just someone needs to take this hotel, spend some serious money...to make some serious money from local business, weddings and tourists. Why do I say this? The highest price local hotel in Burgess Hill is a Premier Inn (c £150 per night) and it’s always booked up, this hotel isn’t and that says it all.

title: Check before you book!
text:
We had been given an afternoon tea gift and chose this hotel, the food was just beautiful, afternoon tea, dinner and breakfast were all delicious. The staff are some of the frienliest I have ever encountered at a hotel, that is why it is so sad that we had a very disappointing stay!
 We unfortunately stayed when there was a wedding! HUGE MISTAKE! We weren't told at the time of booking or later when actually speaking to a receptionist about a ground floor room that there would be a wedding on that day. OMG I have never experienced such noise at a hotel. Kids were being allowed by stupid parents to play up and down the corridor alongside the ground floor rooms. We did phone and ask reception if this was normal and the lovely lady that came go our door apologised and ask them to stop, which they did for a while but it happened again later as well. My advice to anyone booking is check when you book to see if they have a wedding booked! Although the staff were all lovely and helpful and so friendly our Weekend was spoilt by the REALLY RUDE INCONSIDERATE GUESTS AT THIS WEDDING. We could not really get out of there quick eenough on the Sunday morning. So unfotunately we would not come again. Sad really because it is a lovely hotel.

I am pleased however to hear that you did enjoy the food and found the staff friendly. I do hope that you change your mind and come visit us again in the future and if you wish to do so please contact Kellie at the hotel who will assist with your reservation and advise if any event is taking place.

The room itself, whilst a little tired, had everything we needed, and they even provided a fan as the weather was extremely hot. We had fresh toiletries provided throughout our stay, and the room was clean, fresh and aired upon our return (thank you Rosie).
Breakfast was absolutely brilliant. A choice of cereals, fruit,yoghurt, croissants, and a choice of hot foods from the menu. We had full English, and it was delicious. The waiting staff did an amazing job of keeping tables cleared and waiting times for food to a minimum. This was especially noticeable on the Sunday, when everyone seemed to arrive at the same time. title: Simply lovely! We'll definitely be going back.
text:
Oh what a joy to stay at a hotel where the staff are fabulous and the hotel really gets ALL the basics absolutely right. 

I booked online and two weeks before our stay I received a phone call from the receptionist asking me if we would like to book a table in the restaurant for our evening meal as it was the Brighton Marathon that weekend, and the restaurant was getting booked up. Nice touch - we didn't know about the marathon - we were just visiting relatives, and we gratefully accepted her offer.

I had also requested a mattress topper when I booked, because I need a soft bed due to a back condition, and the housekeeper ensured that one was put on the bed.

We were received a very friendly welcome from Sharon, the receptionist, and found our room to be large, comfortable and immaculately clean. There was a bowl of fresh fruit and glass bottles of both sparkling and still water. No plastic bottles - great! 

There was a sewing kit and shoe shiner in the drawer, as well as information about laundry and dry cleaning services. There were tea and coffee making facilities, plenty of lights, a trouser press and lots of sockets - important for me, as I have to plug in a piece of electrical medical equipment near the head of the bed. There was a hair drier (attached) inside a drawer, and the cable was a bit short really, but there was a comfy chair and a mirror right next to it, and it reached OK if I sat there.

There wasn't a full-length mirror, which would have been useful or mini bar (which didn't bother me a bit) and there wasn't a safe either, which considering lots of people now travel with laptops, ipads etc, was a bit of a surprise. I think they ought to consider adding those.

The bathroom was big enough, and had some nice touches such as tissues, cotton wool balls in a jar and toiletries provided. There was a good make-up mirror, although the lights were almost too bright and they dazzle a bit. The bathroom was very clean and the bath and shower were a joy.... none of those dratted dials to do battle with... a simple hot tap and cold tap on the bath, and a shower with two controls that said "Hot / Cold" and "On / Off"... how delightfully sensible, simple and uncomplicated! There was no complicated air con, but a window that opened, a thermostatic valve on the radiator, and an electric fan, so the temperature was very controllable and comfortable.

There were extra pillows and towels in the wardrobe (and the receptionist told us that on arrival), and we didn't need them, so they didn't need to be washed unnecessarily... another tick for their green credentials.

We went down for our evening meal, and were extremely impressed by both the food and the staff. The restaurant was fairly busy, and only three staff were on duty: Justine, (the restaurant supervisor, who smiles ALL the time, and creates a great atmosphere), and Kelly and Cammy who were helpful, friendly and efficient. The staff are really attentive - you only have to look at them and change your expression, and they pick up on it straight away and come and ask what you would like. As well as being quick and efficient, they all make you feel as if they have lots of time for you - I really don't know how they manage that - it's a real skill. I've been in many restaurants where the staff look, but don't see - the staff here really DO see, and that's a skill, too.

The food was lovely. The pate was smooth and creamy, but served with only three quarters of a slice of toast (why do restaurants always do that? The toast is the cheap ingredient!), but Justine was wise to this, warned us it didn't come with much toast, and asked us when we placed the order if we'd like extra, which we gratefully accepted. She then came to see if we would like more part way through our starter, and we said we would - so she organised that for us. The chutney was very tasty, too.

We ordered the chicken supreme, and asked for the asparagus to be substituted with savoy cabbage, which was done with no fuss at all. There was a generous portion of lovely tender meat, and the fondant potato and savoy cabbage were perfectly cooked.

I was disappointed that there wasn't a really chocolatey dessert on offer, so Justine fetched me the lunch menu, which had profiteroles on it. I actually settled for the sticky toffee pudding, which was gorgeous.

I then asked if I could have some decaffeinated teabags and a jug of milk to take to our room which again they provided immediately.

Breakfast was busy, probably due to it being marathon weekend, but again the service was good. Some of the food ran out and took a while to replenish, and the scrambled egg was a bit lumpy, but everything else was good, and when I was looking through the yoghurts, Justine asked me if I was OK, and I said I was looking to see if there was a strawberry one, and as there wasn't one out, she went and fetched some straight away. The morning staff were Justine and Kelly, who looked as fresh as they had the night before and Holly who was also lovely.

If you like a hotel with all the extras - mini bars, turndown service etc, then this might not be the place for you, but if you want good food, good value (£147 a night for a double room, bed and breakfast), a clean and comfy room, and fabulous staff who seem to love their jobs and really want to help you, then Hickstead Hotel will meet all your needs. Best hotel we've stayed in for ages, and we'll definitely be back.

We had hoped the dessert would redeem the meal but again we were disappointed. We all came away hungry!! And I was thankful I had bought a pack of crisps in my  suitcase. We were disappointed to read other reviews of people complaining about the rubber duck, yet they continued to serve it. I would not recommend eating here. We paid a substantial amount each for a night stay so would have at least expected something better than chewy and dry food. To top it off there is a nasty smell floating around the foyer which isn't particularly pleasant.

title: Nothing special
text:
I had organised for my works Christmas party to be at the Hickstead Hotel. 
Dealing with the events co-ordinator was very easy and both ladies I dealt with were very helpful and accommodating especially when one of our party had to pull out about a week before the event offering their full refund. 
We arrived at the venue, there was no one at reception so we made our way to the bar. There was a tray with a couple of glasses of Buck’s Fizz on the bar but no one there to hand them out so I had to ask if we were to help ourselves. 
We were ushered to the events room and took our seats. 
my starter came out, although a little sparse it was tasty. I had the turkey for my main. It did look a bit like a stuffed turkey roll you would get from Iceland but the turkey was flavoursome. The potatoes unfortunately were very soggy and looked like they could have stayed in the oven a bit longer. The carrots and parsnips were not what I expected either. They looked like chips and they too were soggy. The sprouts in my opinion was the tastiest item on my plate. And yes I do love sprouts. 
If I’m honest it was a bit bland, nothing special, but I did clear my plate. The serving staff were great and the disco was enjoyable. The price was a bit high for the quality of food you get so I don’t think I will be booking again next year. 
I did get a phone call from the events coordinator after the event asking how our evening went as one person in our party had expressed their disappointment with their meal. They had the duck as their main course and I must admit, I did try it and it was very dry. Glad I didn’t have the duck! I had not planned on making any complaint as the evening with friends/work colleagues was really enjoyable and wasn’t going to let a soggy potato get between that.

title: Not bad, but used to be better.
text:
Stayed here this week for the first time in about a decade. It's not blatantly obvious, but I could sense that things weren't quite as they used to be.

When we checked in, I think the grown-ups were having the night off and we got the work experience shift. The young chap on reception was pleasant enough, but looked really scruffy - shirt hanging out of his trousers, tie undone, long hair falling about. Not a great first impression.

It's a rare hotel these days which doesn't have at least a 5ft bed in its double rooms - but the Hickstead still only has 4'6" doubles, even though there's plenty of space in the rooms for something larger.

Cleanliness was fine, but the hotel is just beginning to look a tiny bit tired - it's getting to the point where a good spruce-up would reap benefits.

Walls, doors and floors/ceilings could benefit from increased soundproofing. Every step taken by whoever was in the room above us resonated into our room, and the noise in the corridor on our second morning was very disturbing.

Nice breakfasts, served by very pleasant and welcoming staff. (But see the accompanying restaurant review for my views concerning two-person and four-person tables being far too close to each other - we spend most of our time at breakfast the first morning listening in detail to the phone conversation that the businesswoman on the next table was having with her office.)

Price-wise - a room here isn't cheap, but that seems to be the norm for Sussex these days (pretty much impossible to get anything decent for less than £100 a night).

Thank you for your recent review. I am sorry that you felt things weren't quite as they used to be.

I am pleased you enjoyed our breakfasts.
I do hope we get the opportunity to welcome you back to the hotel again in future, where I am sure you will experience us in a better light.

title: A truly quintessential experience
text:
To celebrate "Fathers Day", my daughter presented me with an E voucher through 'buyagift' to experience "Afternoon tea for two at Classic Lodges Hotels UK wide". This meant we could chose any one of a number of hotels within that Chain across the country to enjoy this "experience".

We chose to visit the Hickstead Hotel, West Sussex.It was a simple matter of calling up the hotel and arranging a date and time.

The hotel itself is just off the A23 at Jobs Lane, Bolney, West Sussex and the sat nav took us to the front entrance.

As we drove through the impressive entrance and down the lengthy driveway, we were surrounded by countryside. First  sightings of the hotel itself presented a sense of grandeur and spaciousness.There is ample parking and the reception area is but a few steps from the car park.

Upon entering the hotel we were immediately met by a delightful receptionist who instantly made us feel very welcome.Having confirmed the boking, we were then handed over to one of the waiters who looked after us for the whole visit. He was both pleasant, very smart, attentive and inter reacted extremely well.

The menu for the actual "tea" was extensive with  a good selection of different sandwiches and fillings. Having made our decision as to what we would have in terms of sandwiches,we were given our pots of tea. Before getting through half the first cup, the  food arrived, beautifully presented. It was very apparent, all was freshly made and looked delicious!

We then settled down to a lovely relaxed afternoon enjoying the fayre. The sandwiches contained ample filling  and tasted divine. Accompanying the sandwiches were a large selection of assorted  dainty cakes and  a banoffee sweet, set in a shot glass. Exquisite!

Despite our best efforts, we were unable to consume all the goodies placed before us whilst at the table. When requested, our waiter  duly removed the remaining sandwiches and cakes and presented them to us in a purpose made box to take with us. They were subsequently enjoyed later at home with our friends.

The hotel itself has some 52 bedrooms and  suites  and being only  20 minutes from Gatwick Airport I could see this as an ideal stop over when using the airport.

The afternoon tea? A truly quintessential experience.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Cotehele

Thank you for taking the time to review your recent Afternoon Tea. I am delighted to hear that you enjoyed your experience with us and I will ensure to share your kind comments with the team at our daily meeting. I hope we get the opportunity to welcome you back soon.

Kind Regards
Paul

title: Comfortable, Clean and Freindly
text: Booked in to attend a friends wedding,  stayed a few nights longer.  The rooms was comfortable and clean,  staff were very friendly and attentive.   A little noise from ongoing events and people moving about on the floor above/ door slamming but no more than you would get living in a family home or flat! All in all an extremely pleasant stay!

title: Could do better
text:
Stayed for a couple of nights as friends were having their wedding here. Car park was very spacious and outside of hotel looked today. Booking in at reception was swift with pleasant staff. Bedroom was reasonably well equipped but looking a little tired. Carpet was stained and a bit worn. Bathroom wasn't too good with a terrible shower curtain, main mirror with bits missing and the bottom corner of the bathroom door rotted away. Shower was good pressure and temperature. Bed was comfortable but pillows were very uncomfortable. During the evenings in the bar area there was a distinct lack of staff and we had to go looking for someone nearly every time we wanted a drink. Very disappointed at cleanliness of bar area with noticeable amounts of dust on glass shelves and displays. 
Dining area was pleasant and food was generally of a good standard. Breakfast one day was cooked to order which I prefer and quality of produce used was very good. Wedding ran very smoothly with good food which was commented on by most guests. To round it up. Good and bad points. Need to spruce things up especially in bedrooms and hallways and pay a bit more attention to cleanliness and employ more staff (those staff that are there were very friendly and professional) An average 3 star.

title: Booked an afternoon tea with a voucher from Yeo Valley.
text: Booked an afternoon tea with a voucher from Yeo Valley. As per their menu, we chose 2 types of sandwiches each (Coronation Chicken, Roast Beef with Horseradish, Cheese with a Red Onion Chutney and Egg Mayo with Chives). We have also been served Fruit Scones, Mini Pear Tarts, Strawberry Cheesecake, Summer Berry Meringue and Sherry Trifle. Our initial reaction was OK, they were looking just OK. Once we started eating and we realised that something was not quite right therefore we have left most of the sandwiches and the Strawberry Cheesecake on the table. As the time was passing by our gut feeling was telling us to stop as something was not right. Luckily we did stop. As soon after we left their premises our stomachs were starting to react. We end up with very disturbed stomach problems for the whole afternoon and evening.  Do AVOID THIS PLACE FOR AFTERNOON TEA unless you want stomach problems.

title: Great location, service & food shame about the rest
text:
I was given a Red Letter Day Tea for Two and thought this looked a great place. We arrived and thought it in a very lovely location, easy to park the car. Entering there was no one at reception, we walked around and found a dining room (looks as if it is in a time warp - as does the rest of the hotel we walked through - 70s shades of brown!!), the female member of staff could not have been more welcoming as she showed us to our table, for a hotel in such a great location it is a shame that all the windows of this room look onto the car park.

The tea was lovely, the sandwiches were made to our requirements (we were both impressed by this), everything was tasty and could not be faulted. Well done to the kitchen team for such a lovely tea. The room as said is very 70s and lacks any sort of atmosphere but, with such a sad view from the windows they would be hard put to give the place a good feel - I have ideas if they are interested.

When we left the lovely female staff member (the only member of staff we saw!!) was  on reception, she is a great asset and a credit to this establishment GIVE HER A PAY RISE this place needs her more than she needs it.

Location GREAT
Service GREAT
Food GREAT
Decor POOR
View POOR

This visit was saved by the service and food, thank you

title: Quality and Convenience
text:
My wife and I stayed at The Hickstead on a Saturday night - just B&B at the hotel, with family parties in Burgess Hill on Saturday night and Sunday lunchtime, to celebrate a landmark birthday for my brother.
For a 3 star hotel in its price bracket, we thought The Hickstead was excellent. The staff were helpful, friendly and efficient, breakfast on Sunday morning was fine, and our room was well furnished and fitted, and very comfortable.
We had asked for a quiet room, and the room we got on the ground floor at the back was very peaceful. And although there was a wedding reception in the hotel which included music and dancing in the evening, we heard absolutely nothing of it after we turned in.
I rarely enter a comment on Tripadvisor, but I confess that when I booked the hotel I was a bit concerned at a couple of other customers recent comments, and having been very happy with our stay I felt I should share our very positive experience!

title: Mistake
text:
We booked this at short notice as we needed to be in Brighton.
The lady who checked us in was very surly and we didn't feel very welcome. 
The room was OK. Dated but clean. The bed was a disappointment. Very uncomfortable.
NO AIR CONDITIONING. This was the biggest  problem as it was 30 degrees. We were given a small fan, but it too had seen better days and was very noisy. 
We went out for dinner and arrived back around 9pm. We walked through the front door towards Reception and a different lady was manning it. She never even glanced up and we didn't  the get the friendly 'good evening' which we have come to expect from the usual hotels we stay in. 
Sleeping was very difficult due to the heat and the bed.

Breakfast seemed rather chaotic. Tables were waiting to be cleared but the young girl who was serving  was run off her feet. 
The food was OK. Nothing stood out as warranting a mention. 
An elderly couple  came in who appeared to have been staying a week or so, and she demanded the restaurant supervisor sit down with her while she slated almost every meal she had eaten.  She was demanding a refund on her bill. 
At this point,  We were feeling very uncomfortable so got up and left. 
I'm afraid we wouldn't  return, though the hotel is In a  lovely  setting.

title: Sad demise of a "Classic".
text:
We have stayed at the Hickstead two or three times a year for the last 5 years and have, unhappily, seen it go down and down.  It has never been top notch but it has so much potential that we just kept hoping that the owners would decide to invest in this property.  Clearly this isn't about to happen and it's unlikely we'll stay there again.

The hotel could be really lovely and to see the neglect is very sad.  The location is fabulous, the staff have always been excellent and very professional although, sadly, many have now left or are leaving.  The restaurant is, still, surprisingly good for an out of town 3* hotel.

However, the rooms!  They are in urgent need of renovation, especially the bathrooms where black mould is now the norm.  That said, they are clean, just very scruffy and unloved.

Yes, they apparently do a good trade in conferences etc. and this must be how they stay afloat but the hotel guests seem to be incidental and, allowing the bedrooms to become so run down, just shows disrespect for Classic Lodge's loyal clients, like us.

I'd love to read a glowing review this time next year to the effect that the place has been smartened up and looking great.

ownerResponse.text:

Thank you for your review and I am sorry to hear that you have been disappointed with your recent visits to the hotel. I can assure you that the new management team on board are fully focused on creating a great experience for all our customer's and that we have already put in place a pro active maintenance programme. I do hope that you give us another opportunity in the future where I am sure you will experience us in a better light.
kind Regards

title: Lovely setting but in need of update
text:
Firstly I have to say the hotel setting is lovely. Peacefully set  in the countryside with great surroundings. I don’t like to be negative but feel I have to comment. Firstly reception staff should be warm and welcoming. Didn’t feel this at all. 
The bathroom in our room smelt of urine , I went to mention it at reception but unfortunately another room wasn’t available , however we were offered a discount off our evening meal. I was surprised the lady in reception didn’t come to the room to smell for herself, it was as if she already knew!!
If you are vegetarian the menu for dinner only offers one dish. I’m afraid to say this tasted like a ready meal! The wine list could also do with updating. 
The staff at breakfast were lovely but appeared rather stressed. 
This hotel would be lovely if it were to be updated and customer care was a priority. The setting is fantastic. However I don’t feel enough is being done to maximise would could be a first class hotel.

title: A lovely setting and a warm welcome.
text:
We were in the area as my wife was on a business course nearby.

The Hickstead was a good choice for a one night stay.

Set in a quiet rural area with plenty of free on site parking.

Spacious, well appointed room, which was Spotlessly clean. So a big thumb's up to the housekeeping staff for that.

Friendly staff at check in which was without delay.

We ordered evening meals from the room service menu and were very impressed with the food quality, quantity and the speed of service.  All faultless.

In the morning, breakfast was a relaxed affair with a buffet of hot cooked items and a range of juices and continental pastries, cereals etc.

Check out was set for 11am but as I wasn't feeling well I asked for a later checkout and 1 o'clock was agreed.  This was done with no extra charge, so a big thanks for that.

We will be back in the area in a few weeks time for another course for my wife and would not hesitate in choosing The Hickstead again.

The picture is an interior view of our room but the hotel does present itself well as you approach up the drive.

A good choice.  We'll be back.

title: Rather unloved
text:
Stayed March 2018, one night. £90 room only

When you first approach this property, you have this feeling that you're arriving somewhere really rather special. The entrance and driveway are almost grand and beautifully laid out and then you reach the front door to discover everything else is a bit of a let-down.

I'm going to overlook the fact that the hotel separately tried to overcharge me, first for the pre-paid room on arrival and then for dinner the next day. Perhaps these things happen and they did remedy the bill without fuss.

What surprises me is that the published photos do seem to be quite genuine and yet do so little to convey the tired state of this place. They claim the rooms are decorated in a contemporary style but the brochure must have been written c. 1975. And there seems to have been very little updating or maintenance since. Therefore, and especially given other local hotels in which I've stayed for quite a bit less (and including breakfast,) I thought the accommodation really poor value overall. Worn and dirty carpeting, mouldy shower seals and decaying furniture are just not acceptable.

Not wishing to be totally negative, it is certainly quiet which is a blessing and I did sleep well. I also can't fault the breakfast, that was a very pleasant surprise - plentiful and well-priced (on its own.)

If the price point were rather lower, I suppose I'd consider staying here again. But with so many more appealing and cheaper options locally, I doubt it.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Worldwiser

Thank you for your recent review and I am sorry to hear that you were disappointed with your room. We do fully appreciate all our customer's feedback and I will be reviewing your comments with the team in order to improve our service delivery and product further. I do hope we get the opportunity to welcome you back again in the future.
Kind Regards
Paul

title: Awful
text: I review hotels frequently and don’t like to give bad reviews but I am always very honest and unfortunately this hotel was pretty dreadful! I cannot believe the good reviews and ratings it has....they sound like a different hotel! When you arrive at the hotel it looks nice from the outside and is off the main road so in a quiet place. When we arrived there was a wedding ongoing. We checked in and found the lady on reception to be very unwelcoming and unfriendly. We thought she must have been busy or stressed but she was still like that later on. Our room was average, it was clean however the net at the window had rust staining along the bottom. The bathroom tiles had been broken and looked like they had just been put back on with blu tack! The rooms were not sound proof at all. In the morning we awoke to the sound of someone having a wee and breaking wind in the room above us!! We went out to nearby Burgess Hill for the evening and returned around 11pm. We ordered sandwiches from the 24hr menu to be told that that wasn’t actually 24hrs as there was no chef!! After complaining we were eventually given sandwiches. The one positive thing about the hotel was the lady serving at breakfast - she was very friendly and talkative. Unfortunately the breakfast itself wasn’t great and one of my friends was served a very under cooked poached egg! All in all we won’t be returning - we paid £137 for a night and I don’t think it was worth even half of that unfortunately.

I would like to personally apologies for the obvious failings you experienced during your visit and I can assure you I will be meeting with the relevant head of departments to investigate further whilst also to implementing corrective action training as appropriate, I do hope you reconsider your decision not to visit us again and give us another chance where I am sure you will experience us in a better light.

title: Lovely hotel in a quiet area but near to plenty!
text: We stayed here for a two night break, visiting Brighton and Arundel. The hotel was easy to find, had ample parking and was pleasant on approach. The hotel was clean and comfortable, staff friendly and our room spacious and more than adequate. Nice toiletries and plenty of tea and coffee available, as well as some biscuits!! Bed was comfortable and we had two good nights sleep. There was a wedding on the Friday night, although we didn't hear any noise from this and even this morning, the wedding party was kept separate from the other guests. Only grumble would be the, although speedy, English breakfast, which for our taste was rather undercooked (prefer a little colour on both bacon and sausage and tomatoes were not softened at all). Saying this, we would stay again. Have posted reviews on sea life centre, Brighton pavilion, cafe Rocco and the Brighton museum (Brighton appx 15min drive, reccomend the park n ride) and Arundel castle (appx 45 min drive).

title: Brilliant wedding venue and top quality staff
