The toilet took about an hour to refill so that it could be flushed again, and the handle looked and felt like it would break any minute.  Afternoon tea and breakfast were both good.

title: Birthday Party
text:
Check in was easy and fast. Our room on first floor was large, clean and very comfortable. Free wi-Fi was easy to use in room, and flat screen TVs had good choice of stations. Tea and coffee provided with extra bonus of trouser press.comfortable beds to sleep in and bathroom had all usual extras, shower gel etc.
Attended a Birthday Party in the Function Room, which was large and spacious. Only downside was there was no dance floor, and we had to dance to the band on carpet. Bar was set up on a table which was not a problem and prices are reasonable for a hotel.

Breakfast was a highlight with a great selection of hot and cold food buffet style. Four choices of fruit juices and everything was fresh. Tea and coffee on request.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Brightscot

Thank you for feedback, I am delighted to read you had a good time. I am sorry for the delayed response here on TA, I have only recently joined the hotel. 
We do have a dancefloor and are happy to provide it free of charge, possibly it was the organisers request on this occasion not to have one. 
I hope we can welcome you back to the Hicktead Hotel soon for more great functions. 

Kind Regards
Zoltan Szentpeteri
Hotel Manager

title: Ammazing, relaxing and truely perfect.
text:
First time here for new years eve, stayed here on three previous occasions.
What can we say about this Hotel. From the moment we got there till the time we left, the staff were were some of the best we ever had, nothing was to much trouble and considering the number of people staying that was a credit to the Manager and his team.
The food for New years eve was as always FIRST CLASS especially the number catered for.
We will be back during the year, also returning for New Year.
Thank you all for a very special night.

ownerResponse.text:
many thanks for taking the time to review your stay.
I look forward to seeing you again soon

Kevin

title: Pre-wedding stay
text:
We stayed here prior to and just after a nephew's wedding in Brighton.

We cannot find fault in the manner in which the team run the business. The reception personnel were extremely helpful and courteous, providing us with a room which was not within earshot of a wedding function that night. The manner in which Kevin conducts proceedings at breakfast in the restaurant warrants special mention. The food was spot on, tasty and prepared to order.

We note that  a few negative comments have been left by folk. Hmmm... they are obviously welcome to share their views. However, if abject  luxury is what they are looking for, then for heaven's sake, search for luxury hotels in the first place, dig deeper in their pockets and pay luxury money.

For our family members, all was well. Thank you for looking after us.

ownerResponse.text:
it was a pleasure to look after you and I hope we shall see you again soon

Kevin

title: Family Christmas gathering
text: I would like to thank all the very friendly helpful team working on the 28th Dec 2017. We really enjoyed spending our family Christmas with you all, many thanks for everything. Lovely welcome, hot dinner and excellent organisation from the hotel. Many thanks again and happy new year to all of you.

ownerResponse.text:
a pleasure and I hope to see you again next year

Kevin

title: Afternoon tea
text: Visited for afternoon Christmas tea. Excellent with a good choice of sandwiches and cakes together with attentive service in elegant surroundings and lovely views. Great value, particularly as I also used my Yeo Valley Yeokens to get two for one.

ownerResponse.text:
really lovely to read your comments, many thanks for taking the time to do so, they are appreciated by all the team.
I hope to see you return soon

Kevin

title: Great value stay
text: Second time we've stayed here on the classic lodges package. Good room and a free upgrade (fruit bowl, newspapers and slippers) as I have their privilege card, substantial afternoon tea with good pastries. Inclusive dinner was Pub style grub (fish and chips, burgers, steak etc) but deserts were beautifully presented. Breakfast buffet was adequate and there was a good choice of cooked options. Mine, smoked haddock and poached eggs was a mixed bag; fish very badly dried out but eggs were perfect and wouldn't be out of place on Masterchef professionals. All this said it was a good value package and we probably would use the hotel again.

ownerResponse.text:
thank you for the review, I am sorry the haddock was poor and I will discuss with the chef on duty.

i hope to see you again soon

Kevin

title: Awful hotel
text:
I can’t believe the reviews for this hotel. It has to be one of the worse places I have ever stayed. 
The rooms are large but the beds are so uncomfortable. The walls are paper thin and you can hear everything the person in the room next door is doing. Along with all of their conversation.
Breakfast is very basic and the pastry were stale. 
I will never be staying here again and cannot understand how is Has the reviews it does.

ownerResponse.text:
I would welcome the opportunity to discuss your recent stay with you.
the firmness of mattress will always be an individual choice, so I am sorry if the room you had was too firm with the mattress.
I was particularly surprised regarding the breakfast comments, as well as eggs cooked to your liking (poached, fried, scrambled, boiled) a full breakfast contains sausage, bacon, hash brown, black pudding, tomato, beans and mushrooms. guest can, if they prefer have smoked salmon (with or without eggs), eggs benedict or kippers, all at no extra cost.
the cold offering includes meats, cheese, fresh fruit (cut and uncut), the usual selection of cereals with an option of porridge. the danish pastries and croissants are cooked fresh in our own kitchen each morning. dependant on what time you came down and how busy breakfast was the may no longer have been warm but they would have been freshly baked that day.
something obviously went wrong and I would like to discuss with you directly, i am interested as to whether you feel that the breakfast I have described is a 'basic' breakfast or, for some reason, wasn't available to you.

Kindest regards

Kevin Berry
Hotel Manager

title: Stayed Here Many Times - Not Exciting
text: Wish I could get excited. Rooms are perfectly good. Location is brilliant for A23. Just feel it lacks atmosphere. The bar feels more like a hall than a pub lounge. The beer is poor in selection. Not had a memorable night here.

ownerResponse.text:
thanks for leaving a review.
when you are next here please let me know, I would like to have a chat with you about some thoughts I have to try and improve the atmosphere and offering in the bar area.
as you may be aware I have been here a few weeks and so it has been very much observing and tweaking at this stage but i do hope to be able to improve what we are doing, albeit within the physical confines of the building itself.
I look forward to speaking with you soon

Kevin

title: Lovely stay with friendly and professional staff
text:
Absolutely lovely hotel, the staff were very professional and kind. 
Slept really well and ate a good breakfast. Would definitively recommend this place. 
Location is lovely, makes for a really relaxing and enjoyable stay. 
Marie, Haywards Heath.

title: Afternoon tea
text: Went for afternoon tea with a friend on Monday 6th November. My 2nd visit but there won't be a 3rd! Both my friend and were very disappointed.  She likes Earl Grey tea which they had, I like Assam or Darjeeling which they didn't have, they could only offer fruit teas or English Breakfast!  For afternoon tea!  And it was all tea bags!  The dining room lacks any ambiance and one wilting flower detracted even further.  The milk was already on the table, which I thought unnecessary, milk should be brought with the tea. The plate of sandwiches was a strange number, 3 sets of 3, for 2 people if you divide equally you have to cut one in half.  The cakes were nice but the lemon drizzle was very dry. The scones were the best part, they were lovely.  The staff were friendly and polite but I feel that they are fighting a losing battle. Frankly I wouldn't like my wake to be held let alone a wedding! All in all a very disappointing afternoon.  They should take a look at the Ashdown Park Hotel, afternoon tea there is wonderful.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to review the Hickstead.
I was disappointed to read that you did not enjoy your latest visit for afternoon tea, we must have done something right the first time for there to be a second, and indeed I believe in general we do a good afternoon tea as most people tell me so and we did just over 350 last month.
all that said it doesn't mean we get it right every time and this would appear to be one of those few occasions we haven't, for this I apologise.
all the cakes are homemade so I was pleased you enjoyed those and the scones but it was unfortunate the lemon drizzle was dry.
i agree the milk should have been brought with the tea and this is the normal procedure I would expect.
i also noted that you felt the staff were friendly and polite so from what I understand the main issues appear to be:

dry lemon drizzle cake
no Assam or Darjeeling - though both are normally available.
a strange number of sandwiches
lack of atmosphere.

I would like the opportunity to discuss the latter with you. all the former I can resolve quickly and easily but I would value your thoughts on the atmosphere as I to feel we can uplift this experience, I am still relatively new having only been in situ 6 weeks.
if you would call me and discuss your thoughts on atmosphere, and in particular, what the difference between your first visit and second was, as I assume the atmosphere was more conducive on the first visit or there wouldn't have been a second.
I would be most grateful to hear from you

many thanks again

Kevin Berry

title: Ok if you don’t want anything special
text:
The positive is the location is great, just off the M23, close to Gatwick, close to Brighton and good location to travel around the area.

For me the hotel was very lacking in other areas considering the cost:
- Very hard mattress and hard pillows meant I hardly slept at all. If you like an old springy mattress you’re ok
- the bathroom, whilst clean, was again tired and in need of re-vamp
- General decor is tired and old needing a good facelift throughout 
- There seems to be a lingering smell around reception and the small bar area that was very off putting 

On a plus note, the breakfast (food and service) was very good - lacking a bit maybe in atmosphere. 

Wouldn’t stay here again, would book into the Horsham Travelodge or Premier Inn.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to review the Hickstead.
I am relatively new at the hotel, 6 weeks, and we are in the process of looking at a bathroom uplift programme.
additionally my maintenance manager started 1 week ago and so you will soon be able to see a general uplift in the hotel presentation.
when you are next in the area please let m know as I would like to gauge your thoughts on the general improvement in presentation, I was pleased you enjoyed the breakfast and service which is the general bedrock we are using to build upon.

many thanks again

Kevin Berry

title: Good location with lovely staff
text:
We stayed one night and it was very good, dinner, bed and breakfast special rate which was great value.  The reception staff and greeting we received when we arrived was exceptional. 
Nothing was too much trouble, and dog friendly too.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to review the Hickstead.
it is most appreciate and I look forward, hopefully, to welcoming you back on future occasions.

many thanks again

Kevin Berry

title: So much better than expected.
text:
I was looking for a room near to Brighton as our son was seriously ill in the Royal Sussex Hospital. I very nearly did not book this hotel based on some of the reviews. I’m glad I did.

The staff could not have been more helpful. They even helped me locate the local Tesco store to purchase items to take to the hospital. Despite a wedding they made us feel we were important.

Room 219 was clean & spacious. It had a view of the car park, which is better than it sounds!!

The room is classic and may appear dated to some, but we found it had everything we needed. It was well positioned away from the wedding party, giving us some much needed sleep after a 6+ hour drive.

Food was good, drinks were cold. Both we felt were reasonable (not that we were really tasting anything).

Breakfast was self service, plenty of choice in a nice restaurant area.

If I had to make some constructive criticisms they would be-

Double bed felt a little small (I guess most people have a King/Superking these days).
Toilet in room 219 took quite a while to fill once flushed.
We did feel as if we were intruding on the wedding party as we eat in the small bar area that evening, but did not want a restaurant meal.

But your staff on reception, in the bar and serving our meals made up for all that. I would stay again, and might indeed do so very soon.

ownerResponse.text:
thank you for taking the time to post your review, it is appreciated. I was sorry to read that it was sad circumstances that brought you to The Hickstead but hope more pleasant ones will bring you back.
thank you for your generous comments about our staff, it is appreciated and I will share with the team.
I hope your son has recovered or is well on the way to doing.

thank you again for taking the time.

Kevin Berry

title: Nice place
text:
Spent here one night when came to West Sussex for a 2-day training. Had a ground floor room overlooking car park. It was clean and cozy - that's what you need to rest after all day in meeting room. :)
Small bar with pilsner (staropramen), ale (?) and Guinness (can). Some spirits as well
Food - great tasting mushroom and sherry soup, decent  portion of fish and chips however not fully satisfied with the pudding. I have asked for poached pear in red wine sauce. Pear was little bit too tender - maybe needed few extra minutes in the pan or should've used more ripe pear? On the other hand - my colleagues got very nice golden syrup sponge with custard (instead of ice cream) and they loved it :)
Generally nice hotel to spend a night. Staff was very friendly and helpful. Would use it again :)

ownerResponse.text:
thank you for taking the time to post a review, it is most appreciated.
I was pleased that you enjoyed your brief stay with us and hope to be able to welcome you back on a future occasion.
to assist anyone reading this review the lager is Pravha which is from the same brewery as Staropramen, the ale is Wolf Rock, a craft beer and the Guinness is canned due to lack of volume sold, so that we can always ensure it is fresh.

thank you again

Kevin Berry

title: Did the job
text:
This hotel was OK did the job wanted to spend a few days in Brighton before flying from Gatwick. The lady that checked us in was not over friendly and she did not think much to the views which was strange it was in a lovely setting down a drive well away from the road so peaceful. The menu for the bar was  not bad price but a drink in the bar was expensive.
The shower did not work and I had to go to another room to take a shower, they did  fix it while we were out. The booking included afternoon tea which was great really enjoyed afternoon tea there was plenty to eat. The grounds were very nice, and they give us a packed breakfast as we left before the restaurant opened for our flight. I would stay here again.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to post a review, it is appreciated.
I was surprised that you found the drink expensive, at £4 for a pint of lager and £3.30 for a measure of vodka, gin etc. I though we were good value for money for the area.
I have looked into the shower problem and it required a replacement part, this has been done and thank you for highlighting the issue.
I was pleased to read that, overall, you were happy with your stay and would visit again and I hope to be able to welcome you back in the near future

Kevin Berry

title: Nice hotel
text: We attended our friends wedding this weekend and were very happy with the hotel. The staff were very nice and the food was lovely! Our only small issue was that in a room for 3 people there were only 2 cups and saucers, 2 sets of towels and 2 spare pillows. For one night it was ok. The shower was hot enough and easy to operate. All in all, no complaints, and we would stay there again.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Sue,

Firstly, many thanks for taking the time to post the review, it is appreciated, compliments are always nice and learning of areas where we could have performed better will help us to do so.
Overall I was pleased that you enjoyed your stay, I received several complimentary notes from others guests and will ensure that I pass these on to all the staff.
I was disappointed to learn that we failed on the finer details of increasing the basic offerings to take account of the fact there was 3 people staying, please accept my sincere apologises for this, I am relatively new at the hotel so I will be looking at how these things are communicated to ensure we pick up on this in the future.

Once again, thank you for taking the time to respond.

yours sincerely

Kevin Berry
Hotel Manager

title: Bed and Tea
text:
I arrived at 9pm to a very full car park, apparently driver awareness course being done in the conference room which made me giggle as I'd not been able to find a decent space to park as loads had parked on the wonk over 2 spaces. 

Just time for a cup of tea, brewed in a pot cause it was there, along with some terrible oat biscuit, before sleep. 

Bed comfy, alright pillows, good shower and then I was gone.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear 'JustDrivingJo,

thank you for taking the time to post a review.
it was a shame you didn't stay a little longer and enjoy your time at The Hickstead further, our breakfasts always get positive comments, I was pleased that you seem to enjoy the short time that you did spend with us.
I think the challenge with the car parking is that, over the years, the white line indicators have worn away a little, we shall be re-painting these in the near future so, hopefully when you return, the parking should be a little less erratic!
Again, thank you for taking the time to comment and I hope we shall see you again soon.

Kevin Berry

title: Overpriced with uncomfortable beds and cold water
text: Stayed overnight for a friends wedding. The double bed was more akin to sleeping on a concrete slab it was so hard and uncomfortable. In the morning the bath water was tepid and the shower was freezing and difficult to operate with a strange red button which you appear to have had to press in and hold to generate any type of warmth, pretty pathetic really. £102 for this standard of room is a rip off. However staff did come across as quite friendly and the wedding was well organised and everyone enjoyed themselves. Just a shame about the room.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Ian,
thank you for taking the time to post your feedback.
I had a look at your room a little earlier, I am very new at the property, and so still on a voyage of discovery, I will agree that the mattress was quite firm, in good repair but yes quite firm. I think a softening mattress topper would ease the situation and I am looking into this.
The red buttons on shower taps are pretty common and are there to prevent the user from inadvertently turning up the hot water and scalding themselves, though it would appear from your previous comment this was unlikely to happen yesterday morning. I will be discussing the lack of hot water with my maintenance manager, as this should not be an issue, the boilers are relatively new and should have spare capacity.
I was pleased you enjoyed the wedding, it echo's a number of other positive comments I received over the weekend.

Again, thank you for taking the time to respond
yours sincerely

Kevin Berry

title: One Nighter
text: We found all the staff to be friendly and welcoming and the service we received was excellent. The evening meal was superb. Plenty of choice and extremely well cooked and presented. It's a shame that the quality of the bedroom is not up to the same standard. Our room felt dated and the bed extremely hard. The bathroom was tired as well but all the water pressure etc was fine. By the time you have tucked into the great cooked breakfast, you can forgive the room.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to post your review.
I had a look at a couple of the rooms a little earlier, I am very new at the property, and so still on a voyage of discovery, I will agree that some of the mattress are quite firm, in good repair but yes quite firm. I think a softening mattress topper would ease the situation and I am looking into this.
I was unable to look at your specific room with regard to the comment on the bathroom and I couldn't see, from the posting, you name and therefore which room you had been allocated. I would be happy to comment more specifically should you wish to contact me directly.
I am delighted to read that the staff and dining experience were so much more positive.

Again, thank you for taking the time to post your review

yours sincerely

Kevin Berry

title: 'Dog Friendly' not so Friendly
text:
I booked this hotel on line as my wife and I were performing at Hickstead Showgrounds over a two day weekend period, and we would have our small Lhasa Apso dog, Max, with us. We have no qualms taking him anywhere because he is well-behaved and quiet and we love to have our pet with us. The Hotel web site stated it was dog friendly, but this turned out to not be the whole truth.  I had booked three nights B&B plus two evening meals. On arrival, the Hotel was impressive and the staff polite and cordial.  However, we were advised that our dog could not be with us when we ate, even in the Bar area (we didn't expect to be able to take him into the restaurant, but we are quite happy to eat in the bar).  This also meant unless we sat outside with him, we couldn't relax and socialise in the bar in the evening either, only in our room.  Their compromise was that we could have our meals delivered in our room, but at an additional cost of £2.50 'room service' each per meal. This is not 'dog friendly' as far as we are concerned.  We did have our breakfasts delivered to our room, but I cancelled our evening meals and we found several pubs in the area who were happy to have our custom with Max with us when we ate.
Our room was quite large and looked out over the large car park, but there was no air conditioning - and it turned out to be a rather warm weekend, so we had to make do with opening the large window, which, for security reasons was unable to be opened beyond a few inches. Bath, shower and toilet were very clean, and the towels changed daily. We liked the hotel and the staff, but feel they should amend their publicity regarding their dog policy, so people know in advance that their dog will not be allowed in the bar regardless of whether one chooses to eat or not. Had we known, we would probably have booked elsewhere.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear John,

thank you for taking the time to post a review.
Firstly. may I say that I am very new into the role and was not in situ' during your stay, I am therefore on a steep learning curve!
I take your point regarding being more specific on the website, as a dog owner myself I often face similar situation's and appreciate hat not every establishment has an eating venue suitable for dogs but I do feel that we can communicate this a little clearer.
We will continue to welcome dogs into bedrooms and as such I hope that we shall see you on future occasions.

yours sincerely

Kevin Berry

title: Regular stays
text:
Along with a number of work's colleagues we regularly stay at The Hickstead Hotel. The hotel is ideally located for our works and set in a decent grounds allowing you to enjoy the evening outside should you so wish.   

We have always been made welcome, and none of us have had cause to complain. 

The rooms are set over two floors, the first rooms facing over the car park and fields have the better views, but all are always clean and tidy. 

During a couple of the extremely hot nights, even with the windows open (how far is restricted by the security cable) it did get quite hot in the rooms, air conditioning would have been nice. 

We all have never been short of hot water for showers and baths, regardless of the time of day. 

As we are staying are often staying for a number of nights it would be good to have a small fridge.

ownerResponse.text:
Good afternoon,

thank you for taking the time to post and I can only apologise that we have not responded in a more timely manner.
I am very new to the hotel and so was not in situ' when you stayed. I would be very happy to have a conversation around how we can make your stay more comfortable, particularly in hotter weather, within the physical constraints of the hotel.

please do feel free to contact me directly prior to your next stay

yours sincerely

Kevin Berry

title: Extremely disappointing
text:
Stayed here for one night as a couple. The hotel room was dirty. Dirty bath room, grout and silicone round the bath was mouldy and shower curtain yellow stained. Net curtain in the room again yellow stained. Carpet didn't look like it had seen a Hoover in a while and holes in the plaster board. We ate in the hotel restaurant. Decor is dated however staff very polite. The food was extremely overpriced for the quality. We had calamari which has so flavour. Like eating elastic bands. Steak was ordered medium but came well done. Lacked flavour and very chewy. Bed was uncomfortable to sleep on, need replacing. It was hot so we had the window open and were woken several times through the night by roudy guests through the night which the hotel so nothing about. Was then woken again by a member of staff watering plants with a hose at half 3 in the morning right outside our room which is ridiculous! 
Don't waste your money, for the amount we played we could have just stayed in the middle of Brighton!

ownerResponse.text:
Please accept my apologies for any shortcomings during your stay . As I am unaware of any of these issues and cannot identify who you are , and as these issues were not raised to be dealt with at the time, our preferred method  , it is a little hard to be constructive in the feedback 

 . If you would like to contact myself directly at the hotel on my personnel email , then I would be happy to look into these issues more for you . Please email on gm.hickstead@classiclodges.co.uk

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Two night stay , whilst attending a Wedding.
text: Stayed at the Hotel for two nights whilst attending a Wedding locally .The Room was clean and tidy,breakfast was good and staff very attentive , pleasant and helpful. Convenient for M23 to Brighton or to Gatwick.

ownerResponse.text:

We hope to see you again when you visit the bride and groom

title: One night stay
text: Quite location, room clean with some toiletries products provided. Kettle with tea and coffee only one pack of biscuits for two people. Staff .The breakfast buffet was ample choice but as with most buffet the food was not piping hot.

title: Pride Weekend
text: My partner and I have stayed at this hotel many times before during the past 6 years. We stayed for 2 nights last weekend and pleased to say that the hotel is up to its usual standard.  We love it here as its an almost countryside location, but less than 20 minutes to Brighton by car or taxi.  Comfortable rooms with all facilities and very pleased to see the return of the buffet breakfast again! We dined in the Oaktree Bistro on one of the evenings and again enjoyed great food.  Staff all lovely too.  Thanks to all at The Hickstead - we will be back again soon.

title: Ideal for overnight stay
text:
I have used this hotel regular since 2013 for an overnight stay on business trips.
Ideal quiet location for a good nights sleep.
Rooms clean comfortable although in in need of refurbishment.
Staff polite friendly and very helpful.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your positive comments . This will be much appreciated by the team 

Kind Regards

General Manager

title: Afternoon tea gluten free?!
text: Disappointing!  3 of us went for afternoon tea for my daughter's birthday. I am gluten free  and so was pleased I could be accommodated. Booked and  Ordered in advance by phone. On arrival we were offered our tea( drink) when we asked what types were available we were begrudgingly brought the choices!  Which were extensive! . My gluten free tea came separately which was nice, but no scones as the others had! On asking I was told they didn't do glutenfree scones? So felt a bit cheated! Started on the sandwiches which where tasty until I got to the ham and English mustard. Any gluten free person would be sceptical about this condiment. When I asked and it was checked indeed my thoughts where correct! Mustard not gluten free!!  Having said all the above, remaining sandwiches nice, cakes delicious, but did worry a bit, but I've had no reprecussions so must assume rest of food was ok! And the issue was compensated!!

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback and sincere apologies that we did not handle the gluten free issues perhaps as well as we could and should have done . 

I ma glad that it was sorted for you and that all ended well 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Afternoon Tea
text: Great experience, we took our grand daughter out for a cream tea at the Hotel.  It had been a gift to us from our children...  The cream tea was lovely and the staff served hot chocolate and jam sandwiches for our 5 year old, that was what she asked for.  I was quite prepared to pay for her drink and sandwich but that was waived away by the staff, a real kindness I thought.  Beautiful part of Sussex, lovely Hotel and wonderful staff.  Highly recommend the Hotel and staff.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your lovely feedback . EWe hve a lovely set of staff at the hotel and pride ourselves on our service , so it is lovely when it is recognised 

Come and see us again soon 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Nice afternoon out
text:
We were given as a present an afternoon tea The staff were very friendly and welcoming 
The tea they served was first class and very nicely done and we even were given a doggy bag to take home what we hadn't  eaten so nothing went to waste

ownerResponse.text:
Really pleased you  hd a lovely afternoon tea experience with us

title: Mr and Mrs White
text:
We had our wedding here last weekend 8th July and I would highly recommend it. The venue and setting is just beautiful.
The staff are professional and nothing was too much trouble . We had an unexpected wedding guest attend and with only a couple of hours notice the manager had organised another table setting and she was served a three course vegan meal.
The food was delicious and plenty of it and the breakfast room was set up magnificently.
Many of our guests stayed the entire weekend with us and commented it was the "Best wedding ever!!!" We were asked why we chose a venue so far away and I would say it was worth it, it exceeded all of our expectations. My daughter was our bridesmaid and said when she marries she wants it there too!
I can honestly say I would not have changed a thing. We were guided through the whole process from the minute we were shown around the place last year and I'm so pleased we went with our instinct to book there and then.
A huge thank you to the manager Giles Paul and his entire team who created a magical wedding weekend for us !!

ownerResponse.text:
Good afternoon Mr and Mrs White 

Thank you for such a lovely review and the letter and gratuity you sent it . 

It gave the whole team a real boost to get both and they  were all really pleased to read this . 

For such a personal day , it is so satisfying , when the efforts are appreciated 

Many Thanks again and keep in touch

Giles Paul 
General Mannager

title: Lovely Afternoon Tea
text: We arrived to a warm welcome and were seated immediately. The staff were very friendly and attentive throughout out visit, the sandwiches were freshly prepared and the cakes were homemade and delicious. We relaxed as we enjoyed our tea in the lovely surroundings and atmosphere. Altogether a lovely experience.

ownerResponse.text:
Delighted to read that you had a lovely afternoon tea experience with us ,

General Manager

title: Don't waste your money
text:
Paid £100 to stay one night here and not worth the money at all. Rooms are joined together by a door where you can then hear people talking next door...nice bit of privacy! Broken window so unable to open it. Get moved to an exact same room before finally getting moved to a "normal" room. This was very hot in here, luckily the window opened!
Nice location but a lot of money for what you get.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback , 

I can confirm that the room has now had its window replaced and apologise that you were put into there during such hot weather . We also have an interconnecting door in this room and it appears from your comments  that the soundproofing in the door has worn out , something we are now attending to 

Thankfully this only  effects one set of rooms but as you were adversely affected please contact myself directly and I will be delighted to ensure  your  stay is more comfortable and at a special rate

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Friendly staff and a wonderful breakfast make the difference
text: 15 (ish) minutes from Brighton The Hickstead is a great place to stay if you don't want the hassle of car unfriendly Brighton. The hotel boasts ample parking and very friendly and welcoming staff which makes up for the fact that the internal decor is due an overhaul and is looking a tad sad. The rooms are good but soundproofing is poor and if you are a light sleeper be prepared. The staff make a difference and the breakfast is one of the best I've had you don't make your own toast and drinks are not from a machine and its all cooked fresh. Breakfast is a must if you stay here

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for the lovely feedback about the team and breakfast , two very important aspects of our business 

We hope to see you again soon if visiting the area 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Afternoon tea
text: Went to The Hickstead for an afternoon tea experience - lots of yummy cake and sandwiches! Staff were lovely, especially when we couldn't eat all the cake and made up a doggy bag for us. We weren't ready to leave after our tea so we took our drinks to the lounge area and played scrabble for a while. The staff we quite happy for us to stay even though we weren't guests in the hotel. Overall a lovely experience and would defifintely go there again.

ownerResponse.text:
So pleased that you had a lovely tea 

We do lovely breakfast and dinner too 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Not bad for the fee
text:
Stayed in June on business, friendly professional staff, and for the £85 fee the room was adequate at a 3 star, and food good.  Nice quiet location as well.

Only one slightly negative point to note, the downstairs corridor leading to rooms smelt really bad, like a urinal.  Seriously in need of some ventilation and/or air fresheners.  I was worried the room at the end of the corridor (112) was also going to smell but it was fine.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for the mainly positive feedback . You will be pleased to hear that we have repaired and sorted the leaking pipe causing the temporary odour issue 

we look forward to seeing you again on your next business visit

title: Not acceptable !!!
text:
I have had issues with this hotel on recent stays but last nights topped it all. I arrived late and checked in and when I got to my room the problems are as follows :
The door wouldn't close properly, a rancid smell hits you immediately, it was an adjoining room which was occupied would not lock . There was a large ,still wet stain on carpet next to bed. I refused to stay in it so was given a " off status " room but bathroom was unusable and they suggested I slept in there and used bathroom in the previous smelly room. 
I absolutely refuse to stay here again.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Mr Morgan

I must sincerely apologise for the way that you were treated last Monday night when you checked in with our night porter  into a room that had an open interconnecting door . This should have ben locked of course and the slight damp patch was a wine stain that we had been cleaning and as it was hot we had assumed it would have dried long before your arrival . It should have been checked as the alternative room which was off for maintenance repairs should also have been . 

This series of incompetencies is not our normal service level and before you went on trip advisor you also made us aware of your dissatisfaction through the booking agents who booked you in . They have been in touch and we have been trying to get in touch with you ever since to talk to you, since last Wednesday , 

We would prefer to have sorted this out properly without you using this medium , and have been trying to contact you to do just that . To this end I would appreciate if you would email me directly with a suitable time day or night when I can talk to you directly along with a contact number . My email is gm.hickstead@classiclodges.co.uk  

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Superb.
text:
My family and I spent a fantastic night at The Hickstead after enjoying Brighton beach recently. 

The hotel is in an odd area, kind of in the middle of nowhere, but that appealed to me. It means that you'll not be bothered by passers by. It's set in nice grounds, it's pretty, quaint, and only a short drive into Brighton centre. 

The staff are phenomenal, very welcoming and accommodating, even in the middle of the night. 

We only ate breakfast at the hotel, but it was very good quality. I think there could have been a slightly bigger selection of cereal and pastries, but I have no complaints at all. It was busy and sometimes a few items ran out but once staff were aware it was replaced very quickly. 

I will definitely be back soon.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your great feedback which I will be sure to share with the team 

Please come and see us again soon 

Giles Paul 
General Manger

title: Wedding stop over.
text: Stayed here after my sons wedding.  ( at hickstead showground ). Was quite disappointed on arrival,  check in was very small and not very welcoming. ( Although it was  midnight ). The guy gave us our key and pointed us in a direction of doors. The room was ok with tea/coffee making facilities.  Plenty of room. But the bed was really hard. Bathroom needs a bit of a makeover. They did keep the bar open for a while but was asked to keep the noise down. So after a poor nights sleep it was off to breakfast.  Very good selection of bacon,  eggs, beans etc. Even if you didn't want scrambled egg they would do eggs whatever way you wanted. Cereal limited as was the fruit. Tea already made in thermos tea pot. After a few minutes became stewed.  Toast already on table so it was cold. Was it value for money at £102. No.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your mixed feedback 

Having been on duty during the breakfast on sunday , whilst I am glad you enjoyed most of it , the comment about tea and toast , I found a little strange as I personally approached your table on several occasions to offer more of anything as toast will of cpurse get cold and we would hve been happy to make fresh tea . 

I am sorry that you were not welcomed better on arrival  and am aware that after complaints from other guests , we had to ask several guests to be a bit quieter , always a difficult balance for us . 

We have just replaced over half our mattresses  with brand new hypnos beds  which might explain the hard beds and bathrooms are being slowly refurbished   

We will of course learn from the feedback and feel sorry you feel it was not value for money but if you are back in the area visiting the Wedding couple , please  get in touch , so I can sort your arrangements out personally 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: The only good thing here is the staff...
text: I had stayed in this hotel before (2014) and I was not looking forward to another stay. I was correct. This hotel is stuck in the 1980s, the rooms, the food (and some of the staff) have not changed for 30 years or so. Uncomfortable beds, small and very uncomfortable showers, no extra facilities such as plugs near your bed..wifi that hardly works (speeds that I last saw in the 1990s...), and the most miserable decor all got a tick.  But the staff smile and really try...which is why it gets two stars.  The food generally is just edible, although I did have the sea bream two days which was ok (the fish and chips was just passabl, but the tuna was really horrible). Bring earplugs if you want to sleep as it is really noisy, but better still, find somewhere else.

ownerResponse.text:
Following your feedback  I would appreciate having the opportunity to talk to you to discuss it further , I am unaware of the specific issues and as a point of note we have just replaced over half our mattresses and are refurbishing bathrooms  on an ongoing basis

We have some excellent long serving staff - please see all the other feedback reviews  and improving wifi - about to connect up to fibre optic high speed . 

Food also generally gets good feedback  , so whilst I appreciate freedom of speech , I would really prefer to have a proper conversation  , to see what we can improve

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Wedding venue
text: From planning our wedding, the big day and the morning after made this a very smooth and happy event, not just for us, but also for our guests. We opted for their wedding package which included a taster evening, two rooms the night before, the wedding breakfast, with toastmaster, an evening reception, the bridal suite, and breakfast the next day. All the staff at the Hickstead excelled themselves, they knew exactly what to do and when to do it. The wedding breakfast turned out to be a fairly complicated menu which was delivered, beautifully cooked, to the correct guests. Without exception, the 50 guests thoroughly enjoyed the food and were full of praise of the staff, and our choice of venue. Overall, they made our "special day" very special.

ownerResponse.text:

text: We had a gift voucher through Red Letter Days and decided to use it at The Hickstead. We were warmly greeted on arrival and shown to our table overlooking the grounds. title: Excellent staff and room service
text:
I was on business and stayed one night in this hotel which lies in a very quiet rural location. I had failed to ask the hotel in advance for a ground floor room and did so when I arrived. Even though another room had been allocated, the staff could not have been more accommodating, helpful and pleasant and once they had checked that a ground floor room was ready, it was allocated.

I had a room service dinner which was excellent and arrived very speedily. I also had breakfast in my room which was delivered precisely on time. The accommodation is very good for the price and I enjoyed my stay enormously. There is a large amount of easily accessible car parking and free Wi-Fi which works well.

This is a very good hotel with the advantage of delightful staff which are much to the credit of the management. I would not hesitate to stay here again.

I recommend this hotel.

title: A Pleasant Stay
text:
Stayed here overnight as we were running Brighton marathon next day. Booked online a year ago. Requested a quiet room as had previous issues with loud guests in previous hotels before a big race (sleep is definitely needed)
Was pleased on arrival receptionist pointed out we had been allocated one of the quietest of the 52 rooms. We did not book dinner or breakfast here, went out locally for dinner and up too early and gone before breakfast. We did however use the bar and sit outside in the lovely sunshine on Saturday afternoon.
The room was comfortable, tidy and more than sufficient for a 1 night stop over, everything we needed. Soaps and bits provided in bathroom.  Bed was comfy, I slept well. If I was to say one negative thing it would be the bathroom. On my evening soak I looked more closely, someone else's stands of hair over tiles, and in the bath. The silicone around all the bath was quite mouldy, tiles a bit dated, tiling behind loo messy. Definitely bathroom needed some attention and a deeper daily clean, but on 1st glance and if I'd not laid in Bath probably wouldn't have noticed. 
Check in and out efficient, staff friendly. Would use again if in the area.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback following your visit over Marathon weekend . 

I am glad you had a restful and enjoyable stay , pity you left before breakfast as we opened at 630 and served porridge to about 30 people , something we always do marathon weekend along with high carb pasta dinner the night prior 

We are currently starting a refurbishment programme in our bathrooms as part of our ongoing investment 

We look forward to seeing you next year if not before 

Giles Paul
General Manager

We are keen that all our guests are comfortable in their surroundings

text:
We had booked the Hickstead due to its close proximity to the Bolney Estate vineyard which we were visiting for a pre-booked tour.

Our stay included a 3 course dinner with wine, bed and breakfast for £109 for two people.

We had a brilliant weekend and thoroughly enjoyed being spoilt by the fab staff. The food was faultless and the candy floss served with my dessert was a real treat!

title: Tiptop Tea!
text:
My husband and I were given a Red Letter Day voucher for Afternoon Tea as an anniversary present and chose Hickstead as the venue as it was the nearest place to our home. We had never been to the hotel before and were delighted to find the hotel just off the A23 set in the most delightful surroundings. A plethora of daffodils greeted us as we drove up the drive and parked the car in a car park overlooking the countryside without an inkling the major road to Brighton was just half a mile away.
We were greeted by a lovely receptionist who showed us to our table by the window overlooking the car park and grounds. From here we could see rabbits playing in the field and watch the horses playing in the field.
The waitress served us our tea and we were delighted with the selection of sandwiches provided and an excellent array of cakes for us to enjoy. All of the food had been made on the premises and it was superb. In particular, the presentation of the sandwiches and the scones were excellent. The staff ensured we had everything we needed and we had an anniversary to remember.
We have dined at some fine places including the Ritz, but we believe you will not get a better experience than Afternoon Tea at the Hickstead hotel. We will return!

ownerResponse.text:

The team were really pleased to read your review and comments 

We look forward to seeing you again 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul
General Manger

title: Afternoon Tea
text: We were given a voucher as a gift for Afternoon Tea, not being afternoon Tea people we were not sure what to expect. What a lovely surprise it turned out to be. The Tea was very large and really good, as we could not manage all our cakes a member of staff kindly packed them for us to take home. Would recommend the Hickstead for great Tea and very comfortable surroundings.

title: Smashing Weekend
text:
We first came here a few years ago as a present from our children and really enjoyed the stay. Last year, my wife found an offer for the hotel and as we enjoyed our first stay so much, we booked again. Sadly last time, due to the behaviour of a group of disruptive and rude guests, our stay was spoilt. 
We wanted to get rid of that memory and therefore booked again.
We went down last weekend (25-26/2/17), with our dog as well! We were greeted by staff and went to our allocated room. Our room was, as expected, very clean and comfortable with everything we could wish for. Tea and coffee, TV, fully en-suite with soap/shampoo etc. supplied along with towels. Extra pillows in the wardrobe and a Trouser press - fantastic.
We booked for dinner and when we walked into the restaurant, we were recognised by the waiter - Artur and welcomed with a handshake and a smile and a genuine, "So pleased to see you again!"
We were seated at our table and we decided what to eat. Once ordered, we were then given a taster appetiser that the chef was trying out (Gnocchi with black cabbage and a sauce). It was very nice and neither of us had tried that before and both really enjoyed it.
For starters, I had the Buffalo Mozzarella, whilst my wife had Chicken Satay. For mains, I had supreme of chicken and my wife had lamb shank and we both had a peanut slice with ice cream and brandy snap for dessert,
The food was very tasty, cooked just right and just the right amount. Full compliments to the chef and the kitchen staff!
Throughout the meal the waiting staff were very attentive to our requests and needs, always there when we wanted them but never in your face or pestering us. This is how I like staff to be when I am out with my wife.
The room we had was very quiet and peaceful and we both had a very restful night.
Breakfast in the morning, was again very filling and lovely. It set me up for the day and the journey back home to Essex.
The hotel was very comfortable and clean. The staff very professional, attentive and courteous. All in all we had a fantastic weekend.
The hotel, staff and food allowed us to have a peaceful weekend and 'recharge our batteries'. This is what any hotel should be like!
Would we return to the hotel again?  We discussed this on the way home and yes - we will!
Thank you.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your extensive and positive review after your most recent visit . 

I am delighted you accepted the invitation to return and hopefully banish the memories  caused by the misbehaviour of other guests on a previous visit . 

We look forward to seeing you again soon 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Tired
text: It's always good to be asked how your stay was when checking out. This didn't happen when I left The Hickstead. Maybe they didn't care? If asked I would have pointed out that my room was cold and I couldn't adjust the temperature and the bed felt old, lumpy and the mattress at least in need of replacing. Just not good enough at £75/night. The hotel is generally tired, run down and soulless.  An extra £10 for breakfast confirmed for me that on this occasion, I'd made a big mistake choosing this hotel. To it's credit though to be fair, the breakfast was very good!

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Sir/Madam 

Thank you for your feedback . I was very sorry to read that you were not asked about your stay with us when you departed , something we do as a matter of course and even more regrettable that you had a couple of room issues which we were never made aware of until this forum . 

I must apologise for this but we always prefer to try and sort out any issues at the time so that you could have enjoyed your stay . It sounds like your radiator may have needed some adjustment ,something we could have easily done had we known but we certainly should have enquired about your stay on checkout and normally do this out of courtesy .

text: Beware the paper thin walls in the modern bedroom block, particularly if you have a connecting door to the adjoining bedroom! Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Convenient off the A23
text:
Don't be discouraged by my 3-star rating. It actually is about 2 1/2 stars but perfectly clean and nice. The room was spacious, clean and welcoming. The ubiquitous "full English breakfast" was ample and good and the staff very friendly and accommodating our every wish. There is no elevator to the 1st (Am. 2nd) floor, but they kindly helped us lug our bags up. The setting in the English countryside is lovely and tranquil and access to the nearby dormitory town of Burgess Hill very convenient indeed. We shall stay here again, as we have family in BH and formerly stayed elsewhere.
Our only criticism is that the walls are thin and conversations of adjacent rooms can be overheard quite plainly.
Access to the hotel off the main London/Brighton road (A23/M23) very convenient - only 1 1/2 minutes! Access for Crawley and Gatwick Airport easy. Despite busy holiday traffic, we buzzed around via M25 to Heathrow without a hitch.
In short, don't overlook this place. It's not meant to be fancy but it is clean, warm and welcoming.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback following your stay over The Christmas Period 

We look forward to seeing you on a return visit 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Ok but a little tired
text:
One night stay as we were in the area for a party. Pleasant enough room but the bathroom was a bit of a let down - odd choice of floor covering (paving??) that makes it look like a dirty exterior floor.
Breakfast was ok but we waited ages for teas and coffees - the staff were very nice and the restaurant was busy but we did wait a long time.
All in all it was ok value and perfectly satisfactory for a one night stay and had no pretensions to be anything more than it is.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback following your recent visit 

I am sorry you waited a little longer than expected for hot drinks . As you say we were busy and always do our best to manage the situation on a Sunday morning when you all get up together , so whilst you I am glad you were looked after I am sorry you waited a little too long something we work hard at and will work harder . 

Incidentally we will be doing some bathroom refurbishment in the New Year as we continue to invest in the hotel 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: A nice hotel it's a shame the bar closed so early.
text:
A nice hotel and good value. We didn't check in until 10pm on Friday and where amazed that the bar was closed so early. We were told that as there were no customers for the bars so the guy went home. I would have thought that the receptionist could have managed to pour a couple of drinks but didn't offer.( Friday 2nd Dec if the management are bothered )
Now the breakfast. I have been fortunate enough to have stayed in some of the best five star hotels and the cooked breakfast served at the Hickstead would be a hard act to follower by any hotel. It was great.
The hotel is in lovely surroundings, open country with horses in an adjacent field.
My wife and I were in the area for a funeral but will be back on a happier occasion for sure.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback and glowing reports on our breakfast which I was delighted to read you enjoyed 

We were extremely quiet on Friday evening with most residents out but although the bar did close at about 10.20 you should haver been offered a drink by the late receptionist and I have spoken to the new young lady about those rarer occasions when that situation may occur 

We look forward to seeing you again 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Nice hotel but let down by some clientele
text:
We stayed here as a result of a deal sent to my wife as we had stayed there before for an Afternoon tea and Overnight stay with breakfast about 4 years ago. We really enjoyed that stay and so were looking forward to the Cream Tea, 3 course meal and breakfast.
As we have a dog, getting away for  weekend is often difficult and were pleased that dogs were welcome here.
One of the first things I noticed was the 3 parking spaces closest to the main door. The 2 outside bay were disabled bays. However, they were all normal sized and both had plant pots close to the front (one to keep a fire exit route clear!) which made it awkward to park.
We did arrive a tad early and there was no-one on the reception desk to greet us. However a receptionist was soon found and we were booked in.
We knew that by having a dog friendly room, we would not be getting the best rooms. Ours was tidy but tired and on the ground floor. However, I did notice that there were a couple of tains on the carpet and that there was some blackening of the sealant around the bath and the fittings.
Obviously the dog could not be left in the room whilst we were eating and so after putting him in the car, enjoyed our cream tea in the lounge. That was very nice fresh scones and hot tea/coffee. One scone each - which was ample, bearing in mind we also had a three course dinner later.
The grounds at the front are plentiful and ample to exercise our dog. Load of grass and plenty of trees (which obviously is helpful if you have a dog!).  My only issue here is that there are no bins for dog waste and as a result you have to give the bagged waste to the receptionist for disposal.
When we went down for our evening meal, this was when we had our problems. There was a group of 8 guests that met here for a meal and overnight stay. They had previously met on holiday and had a re-union there. This group were all extremely drunk, foul mouthed and abusive to not only the waiting staff but also to us. We had our starters before they came to their table which was next to ours. The starter was very nice. Before our main meal was served, this group sat down and were very loud and shouting, "F...ing this abbd F... that etc etc". One particular "lady" was very rude towards us and as a result our waiter - Artur - asked if we would like to move tables. We declined as it would look obvious as to why we moved.
The main course was served, which again was very nice, but sadly we felt we had to rush our food - just to get away from this group!
We opted to have our Desserts in the lounge due to thei behaviour, but even this was questioned by this "Lady"
We would have liked to stay and have another drink, but again because of their behaviour we felt disinclined to do so and therefore walked the dog and returned to our room.
There was another party going on in the function room, and although there were numerous guests there, we did not hear them at all.
In the Room, my wife had a shower which she found flowed hot and cold with no consistency.
Personally, I found that the bed was extremely hard and as I am a larger build person, found it uncomfortable as I did not seem to make a dent and I ended up with painful hips. Previously the bed was very comfortable.
All was reasonably quiet and I managed to fall asleep, but then at about 1am, I woke up and then I heard the noise, which was only in the background in the first instance. However, this got louder and louder and I could clearly hear the "Lady" shouting the group singing at the tops of their voices. I don't know where their rooms were but at 2am onwards it became worse and worse. So much so - at 2.45am I telephoned the Mick in reception to complain. The noise subsided at about 3.30am. My dog normally sleeps through anything but this unsettled him!
Both my wife and myself both felt sorry for the waiting staff that evening - Artur and Lauren who were really working hard and attentive, but were both subject to constant abuse from this group! 
All in all, this group spoilt our nice romantic weekend away and now I will seriously reconsider about returning to the hotel. 
This is a nice hotel, but I do feel that it is tired!

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for all your detailed feedback after your second visit 

I must apologise for the group of 8  and their behaviour on their holiday reunion . 

I agree their behaviour was both a surprise to us really due to their age and unacceptable and offensive . I was around in the late afternoon and early evening and had spoken to them about consideration to other guests , something I rarely need to do here at the Hickstead  but I am aware that they continued to disturb people and that this adversely affected both the staff and a few guests they came into contact with .  

It is sometimes hard to keep everyone happy but we would normally expect a normal behavioural level and if we had known in advance about them they would not have been entertained at the hotel . 

Sorry we did not deal with this delicate situation forcefully enough. 

We would like to see you back at the hotel and to this end please get in touch with me and I will make the arrangements for you 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: Lovely stay
text:
We spent Saturday night here on a bed, breakfast and evening meal package. The staff were very hard working and incredibly friendly. The food was terrific, so much better than I was expecting from this type of fixed package. The breakfast whilst buffet style was of an exceptionally high standard.The whole evening was relaxing and I would highly recommend a stay. The room was clean and comfortable and reception were always available if you had any additional needs.

The only downside is the lack of soundproofing in the rooms - not a particular problem for us as the only noise was next door's snoring and I'm used that! 

All in all, I thought it was great value and look forward to staying again.

ownerResponse.text:
Really pleased to read such positive comments 

Delighted you enjoyed our winter offer , watch out for other seasonal offers as we hope to see you again in the New Year

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

General Manager

title: Great food, but sadly appearance and cleanliness really let's it down
text:
Just returning home from an overnight stay. On arrival we were a bit disappointed with how run down the outside appearance is, a nice looking building but all the woodwork needs attention. Reception staff friendly enough, our room sadly didn't live up to its superior title, again it's all a bit tired looking, our toilet seat was broken, extractor fan didn't work and there were lots of cobwebs.
On a better note, our meal was excellent in the hotel restaurant, the staff were very attentive and friendly and breakfast was also really nice, if the rating was on food alone it would be five stars! 
We expressed our concerns to reception at check out so hopefully they will rectify the issues we had. Just a little sad that our celebration was tainted by a lack of attention to detail.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback . 

Delighted to read that you were looked after ad had a good experience in our restaurant 

My apologies for the bedroom issues which you should not have experienced . 

These are all now rectified now and we hope yo welcome you back soon

Kind Regards

General Manager

title: Sons wedding not at this hotel
text:
It was the smell as soon as I entered...I did not except first room so took second...mould on window and celing in the bay...work had been carried out on the celing above bed  but not finished paper peeling ...shower the shelve to hold shower gel was rusty toilet seat fixing dirty...when i dropped my earing trying to look for it  I found more than a earing...dust under the dresser once stall removed and a old toe nail ...i could not wait to leave how they could call this deluxe room.....breakfast the mushrooms were hard.   I tried to find some where else but at short notice as I was going to my Sons wedding I needed to get ready there.....
Lynn Surrey

ownerResponse.text:
I note your comments  and apologise that we did not achieve the expected standards in this occasion . We have emailed each other and resolved this privately  as I responded to your original feedback on our hotel system before you published on trip advisor  , so a little disappointing to read about it again in such a public forum . Really we like to deal with things at the time of stay as we find this a lot better and it is hard to rectify things like this historically 

Kind Regards

General Mannager

title: Lovely stay, very helpful staff
text:
We stayed for a wedding and were greeted by friendly and welcoming staff.

Our room was large, modern and clean and was ready early so that we could check in.    

Breakfast was very nice, with a wide selection.

With the friendly staff and the hotel not being big it had a very peaceful, friendly atmosphere.

The hotel is situated in a isolated position near to the A23.

ownerResponse.text:
The team were pleased that your enjoyed your visit to the area to attend a wedding . 

We hope to welcome you back soon

title: Over priced
text: We travelled straight from work and arrived at the hotel at 9:05pm, five minutes after the restaurant stopped serving food. This is fair enough but I expressed a wish for a snack with room service, to be told that wasn't possible. I then asked if I could have takeaway, this was permissible but unfortunately didn't arrive until 11:00pm - beware Papa John's. After this less than splendid start to our "relaxing stay" the room was hotter than a recent stay in the tropics! After eating a rather late meal of pizza we finally managed to go to bed ( my side the springs had gone and had to sleep in a dip) people came crashing, laughing, shouting along the hall 1:30am. 2:30am the room shook as a body fell upstairs, now unable to fall back to sleep we then realised how thin the walls were, it felt like the chap next door was snoring next to me. Sadly the decor is tired, chipped away and worn. Breakfast was cold with only a couple of options for vegetarians. When you enter the reception you are hit by a blast of (sewage - toilet air) £244 is an extortionate price for two nights here. Very disappointed!

ownerResponse.text:
I was really disappointed to read your comments and feedback and although I was on all weekend , I am unaware of these issues with yourself and would appreciate the opportunity to discuss them if you would like to get in touch . If there are issues we would always prefer to deal with them at the time but if not too late , I will see what I can do for you . I would also be interested to find out how you booked as this rate is more than our normal charges if you booked directly with the hotel 

In any case I do offer my apologies but would appreciate the opportunity to talk to you directly rather than through this medium  

Kind Regards

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Afternoon Tea
text:
The hotel is well signposted from the main A23 and easy to find. The welcome was warm and genuine from the front desk as soon as we arrived. Excellent organisation meant we were passed on to waiting staff and a swift service continued with the delivery of food and refreshment in short order. The food itself was gorgeous and we could not have wanted for more. A pleasant, quiet setting gave a relaxing time which is just want we wanted.

Quiet tasteful music played in the background and helped us enjoy our time. Although only a short visit it is a place we will gladly return to in order to have a longer stay.

ownerResponse.text:
I was really pleased to read that you enjoyed our home made afternoon tea during your recent visit to the Hickstead 

Thank you for your warm review 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Quiet hotel with good food
text:
We stayed at this hotel for a weekend in October on the Discover Sussex dinner, bed and breakfast offer.  It was excellent value for money. The room was clean and spacious but the bed was a little hard, although some people may like this.

The food was excellent. there was plenty of choice for dinner and breakfast.  Service was outstanding on our first day, although there were only a few tables taken.  On our second night (Saturday) the service wasn't quite so good as the restaurant was nearly full at dinner and again for breakfast. However, the quality of the food made up for this. The house wine was drinkable but could have been better.

We got the train into Brighton for the day and the receptionist was very helpful in giving us directions to the train station. It made it an easy journey and well worth a visit.

I would recommend this hotel for a quiet weekend getaway and would stay here again.

ownerResponse.text:

WE were indeed very busy Saturday night and Sunday morning but it appears to were still well looked after . I also note your comments about the wine list , something that we  are currently updating and expanding and we will have a new one in place in the next two weeks

We hop to see you again for  New Year's Break  

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: One Night Stay
text: We had an executive room but not sure what makes it an executive room!  Perhaps it was the robe and slippers.  The bathroom was tiny with a horrid shower curtain.  The bed wasn't comfy, neither were the pillows.  Honestly a Premier Inn provides more!  The room looked basic/standard I'm sorry to say.  The only positive was it was clean.

title: Afternoon Tea
text: Quite a remote hotel but worth finding.  Visited on a Sunday afternoon for tea which was delicious.  The sandwiches, scones and cakes were all freshly made.   Staff are very pleasant and friendly.  Endless pots of coffee or tea.  Would thoroughly recommend.  Ideal venue for special occasions

title: Just fabulous
text:
My husband and I went for a relaxing 2 days break to The Hickstead Hotel. When we arrived we were very welcomed and remembered from previous stays. The hotel room was lovely - it was large and clean. 
The food - we were welcomed by Debbie each morning who looked after us so well and like all the staff couldn't do enough for us. Breakfast- you can have continental, cereals, fruit, yogurts and a cooked breakfast. 
Evening meals - plenty of food and absolutely delicious - very very good value for money.
There isn't a lot to do on site which is why we went there but you are very close to the downs, to the seafront, airports and shopping centres. 
We chose the Discover Sussex deal - B&B, 3 course meal and a glass of wine for £98 for both of us !!! 
I will definitely recommend The Hickstead Hotel to all my friends.

ownerResponse.text:

title: great location
text:
close to Gatwick (so an option for early flights), close to Brighton and A23, so very convenient for travelling around the area. Of course you need your own car.
Hotel itself was very clean, good sized rooms, we only ate breakfast here but that was very good and a reasonable price. Room price was also very good. Staff very helpful, we checked in really early so they looked after our bags and even took them up to our room when it was ready.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your positive feedback , I am delighted to read that you were looked after

title: Hotel Manager worth his weight in gold!
text:
We attended a wedding at the Hickstead and arrived midday (2 hours prior to check in) reception staff very accommodating and gave us rooms that had been cleaned so we could get luggage away prior to service.
This is a 3* hotel and you get no more/less than expect, rooms are tired but comfortable - two things which would improve immensely are a fan in room I know weird request as we are in the UK! but as all hotel rooms only allow less than a fag size gap when opened I do think you can always add the spare blanket but not able to cool down easily. second thing is windowless bathrooms are the norm but extractor fan is essential to clear steam/odours!
anyway wedding went so smoothly this is thanks to I think his name was 'Giles' (apologies if not) the best manager I have ever met, hope the hotel chain read this as this man needs a payrise - you would go under without him as any minor hotel faults are compensated by the attentive but not grovelly service given by the manager and he leads all staff behind this 10/10 service.
wedding food which would not expect much when mass catering is involved, especially in 3* restaurant but no every course was SO good, extra gravy on tables, vegetables plenty - cooked & hot! my partner is the worst food critic and he said best food he had had so praise indeed from the man who has never praised my cooking in 25 years!
beautiful grounds and lovely outside area off the function room, which is a walled garden so very sheltered. anyway to sum up this is an excellent venue for a function or to eat and with a couple tweaks to rooms not a bad place to stay either.
Well done and many thanks to The Hickstead and staff.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for such a glowing review of our service levels and our treatment of Weddings . Such a personal day which we fully appreciate and it makes all the hard work worthwhile  for all the team 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Deluxe Afternoon Tea
text: We visited today for a Deluxe Afternoon Tea brought for us as a wedding gift, my husband read the reviews before we choose this hotel, having been to a lot of these type of things unfortunately the look of the hotel is slightly for me disappointing although please bear me mind as I say we have been to lots, the interior inside the dining area needs updating and is looking tired, I think it needs brightening and modernising which I can see is in the plan. We could not however fault the food in terms of the actual cakes and scones they were lovely and scones were warm, the cakes delicious! My husband felt the sandwiches could be a little smaller and a bit more choice and maybe presented a little better but again the bread was lovely and fresh and the fillings were nice. It could all maybe be improved a bit with nicer cups and saucers and a nice teapot but again if the hotel are not specialising in this sort of thing, would it be worthwhile for them. I would definitely say it was average with the food scoring high. Staff were lovely and helpful also.

text:

Very nice to sit outside and have a drink surrounded by beautiful fields. A walk before dinner is very nice.

But is it clean? The seats of the chairs in the social areas look dirty (maybe they cannot be more clean than this).  The carpet in my room made me wish I had brought my flip-flops.

title: Felt compelled to review straight away
text:
Visited today for an afternoon tea that was bought for us as a gift earlier this year.

Front of house staff very friendly but hotel/restaurant looked quite tired. 

Tea and food very average: no choice of tea (and we'd expect proper tea not tea bags), no clotted cream (this should be a given, right?!) although scone freshly cooked and very tasty. Sandwiches were a bit lack lustre with fairly tasteless fillings and slightly stale bread. Macaroon seemed stale too. The meringue with strawberries and cream were very nice.

We bagged the rest of the cakes to take home.

Perhaps we have been spoilt by some 'top end' afternoon tea experiences in the past but when you think you could do better at home you know there's an issue...

title: I would say this is a working hotel,
text:
The reason.............. you would only be here because your on the way to or from somewhere else. The facilities are OK but as has been remarked it is in need of some refurbishment in the rooms.  The dinning room is fine and serviceable.
The meals were ok for the traveller but not a place to experience a culinary adventure. Staff were very good and there was plenty of service. Parking very good.
On the two night we were there they had functions and while I did not see the function room there was little or no noise from that area of the hotel.
As a base to explore the east Sussex coast or a place to stay prior to accessing Gatwick it is in a good position.

title: Quiet location
text:
We had to attend a funeral in Brighton, and on the advice of a cousin booked here.

It is located off the busy A23 but sufficiently far away not to be affected by traffic noise.

The reception and restaurant are located in what looked like the old building with rooms in a newer block built on.

The rooms are modern with all the usual facilities with table and chairs. 

We had booked breakfast which on our first morning was self-service but was waitress service on the second. It depends on how many people they have staying we were told.

After our main reason for staying, we returned for an evening meal. This was reasonably priced and offered a good choice of menu.

There is also a service area off the access road to the A23 with a Little Chef in case you don't want to use the hotel's facilities.
