title: Comfy like an old pair of slippers...
text: Stayed here at this nice quiet hotel many times and always had an almost identical and satisfactory experience.  Plenty of car parking.  Nice staff led by highly experienced manager, who always tries his best to make every guest happy and has an ability to choose some excellent staff for his team...every one of them seems to be nice.  The bar is often staffed by the friendly, helpful Bar Manager, Hasan, who has a great sense of humour and can entertain you with some interesting stories.  It is worth ordering a pint of Guinness, just to watch the whizzy machine making it!  Rooms are clean and well equipped with decent TV...no air-con,  so can be hot in the summer.  Beds seem a bit too firm, but lots of people like that.  Some bathrooms are a bit dated, but who cares, they are nice and clean and the showers work really well.  Dinner can be a bit "hit and miss", but the restaurant specials are normally good.  Breakfast is cooked to order and very tasty.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the trouble to post some comments 

It is appreciated and it is always a pleasure to see you revisiting the hotel and to hear that you feel recognised and well looked after 

See you again soon 

Giles 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Afternoon tea
text:
My friend and I had afternoon tea yesterday and were rather disappointed.  Firstly the tea I had was an excellent pot and my friends coffee was equally good. We started off our afternoon tea with sandwiches which were ok but not exciting. We were given 9 fingers of sandwiches, an odd number for 2 people, which consisted of 3 each of egg mayonnaise, ham and mustard and smoked salmon. We both felt that the sandwiches lacked sufficient filling, except the egg mayonnaise. The smoked salmon was very plain and would have been better with some cress or cucumber added. Also there was too much mayonnaise in the egg sandwich.

Onto the cake section - we had two warm scones, mine was hollow which was strange but the jam and clotted cream were nice.  We then had a really delicious small meringue with cream and fresh strawberries followed by an eclair which was rather large.  We did look at the piece of cake on the tiered plate but it was large and had some thick topping which looked very unappetising and neither of us ate any of it, and a large shortcake biscuit, again we didn't eat it.  I did take the shortcake home and my husband ate part of one of them and said that it was so sweet he couldn't finish it.  There were a number of other people having tea and lots of the cake was left.  In our opinion the cake and shortbread were much too large.

Quiet a lot of the cake selection was based on cream so if you don't want a cream overload then it would be worth mentioning before booking afternoon tea here. We didn't feel it was good value for money or a very enjoyable experience.

We ordered more tea and the waiter promptly bought more tea and coffee, took our milk jug away for refilling and forgot to bring it back, not a big deal but attention to detail is important.

ownerResponse.text:
My apologies for the delay in response . I did speak to you on the day as you were departing and having investigated the cake selection that day and the scones all of which are homemade , I agreed with the chef that the days production by one particular chef was not up to our normal standards and we have addressed this and apologise that you did not get our normal standard of offering . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Ideal location for our family meet up
text:
The whole family booked in to stay here, as we had a family wedding locally, and we had stayed here 10 years ago.
We had all checked in Friday evening, and met up for a family meal in the restaurant which the hotel had pre-arranged for us. Then being a lovely warm summers night, we sat outside drinking and talking.
Breakfast is indeed plentiful with pretty much all you could want, cereal's pastries or a full English....my only complaint is that the only eggs are scrambled which if you're not down first thing become dried out. 
The day after the wedding was a nice quiet day, sitting out in the sun again, before meeting up with the newly weds for a Sunday lunch locally.
Check in and out was quite quick and worry free.
Staff are very helpful and friendly, and my request for an iron and ironing board.....plus an ice bucket...we're delivered promptly.
A good base to explore the local area if you don't know it....about  15 mins drive down to Brighton, or a quick taxi to Burgess Hill and get the train.

ownerResponse.text:
I appreciate all the positive comments and glad you had a nice weekend . When we do a Buffet breakfast we do put out scrambled egg but we do or should have offered alternative choices of egg so I apologise that this did not happen 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Strawberry meringue to die for!
text: My daughter and I went to the Hotel for a delicious afternoon tea, a birthday gift from her boyfriend. What a treat! The hotel is in quiet surroundings with views to the South Downs, plenty of car parking space and with helpful and attentive staff. I had a pot of coffee and my daughter a pot of tea. Plenty of fresh milk on the table and we were offered refills if required. The finger sandwiches were lovely. Fresh bread - not too thin and not too thick. But the scones and cakes were superb! The scones were of a robust proportion, warm, and accompanied by an ample helping of clotted cream and jam. There were two eclairs, two dainty macaroons, two generous pieces of lemon cake, two scrummy shortbreads and the two creations of Meringue (light as a feather) topped with delicious slices of strawberry and drizzled with chocolate. Absolute yum factor! We were almost beaten....and couldn't manage a single morsel more for the rest of the day in spite of having a 2.30 sitting. Really nice. Thank you.

ownerResponse.text:
I was delighted to read that you enjoyed our homemade afternoon tea experience and thank you for your glowing review 

Come back and see us again soon 

Kind Regards 

Giles 
Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Perfectly ok average three star hotel
text: The staff are helpful, the decor is fine, it is perfectly good for an overnight stay. There are fields all around - the outlook is not beautiful but it is nice to be surrounded by green. It is a bit of a drive from any sort of destination. We had been to a wedding in the vicinity and needed somewhere to crash out afterwards. I suppose that does raise a small niggle, that after a late night it is a real pain that breakfast finishes so early - at 10.00 at a weekend. It is worth noting that there is nowhere else to go for breakfast nearby - we had to drive into Burgess Hill which is about fifteen or twenty minutes to drive and park. The hotel does have room service but that also finishes at 10.00

title: Pleasant stay and excellent value
text:
Booked a two night special package which was dinner, bed and breakfast while using the hotel as a base for visiting Brighton. Booked a family room over the phone and checked in by friendly staff who were great for the duration of our stay.

Our package gave us a three course meal from the main menu and this was good value and of a good standard. We had to pay for our son's food but this still meant good value. 

The restaurant staff looked after us very well - nothing too much trouble.

The room was comfortable for what we needed. Wifi slow but free. The corridors are very echoey and any conversation in the corridors do find their ways into the adjoining rooms - but this is more due to the guests than the hotel.

Breakfast was fine - on Saturday we gave our orders for hot breakfast but on sunday it was buffet but it was much busier.

We were a family of three - most of the other guests were a bit older than us shall we say ! However, the staff were very welcoming to our 10 year old !

Brighton was only a 15 minute drive away.

Great stay - most enjoyable

ownerResponse.text:
Really pleased to read your lovely comments and the team were pleased to see them 

I hope to see you all again soon on your next visit to the area 

Kind Regards 

Giles

title: Dated
text:
The Hickstead feels like a hotel that was really nice 20 years ago but hasn't had a penny spent on it since then. 

Positives: It's a nice location, quiet enough, the staff are pleasant and parking easy. 

Negatives: The room is extremely hot with no way of cooling it, the window can't be opened fully as its on a restraining lead despite being on the ground floor. The food is plain pub style food and the drink selection limited. The bed isn't the most comfortable, and the furnishings generally have seen better days. The wifi is free, but slow to the point of being unable to load an email let alone stream media. 

Overall, a reasonable place to overnight in, especially in winter, but not for a longer stay and not for the avid internet user.

We have just invested about £20000 on refurbishing our corridors and have had new carpets laid and as part of our on going investment we are now looking at all our bathrooms .  Wifi is being improved all the time and by the end of the year BT have assured us that their fibre optic project will be complete in our area and this will really improve our wifi reception speed . Hopefully you might see fit to try us again

title: Overnight stay in transit
text: We only stayed the one night but from the short stay the positives were: ample parking, good bed, decent size and firmness; powerful shower, good size room for two persons. Negatives were: the doors were heavy and slammed on closing. In a long corridor every closing door would reverberate throughout and as guest were returning to their rooms particularly around 1 - 2am sleeping took a while. The building construction appeared light so not only the doors made disproportionate noise but so would guests who were just chatting as they walked down the corridor. This is obviously a difficult one for any hotel. The room was a bit stuffy but opening the window obviously helped but as it was a ground floor room, leaving it open all night was not really an option since the window opening restriction was just a sprung loaded hook. So in conclusion the hotel in my opinion is average but would I choose this over the Travelodge around the corner? And the answer would be yes.

title: Tired
text: The reception staff were all very pleasant, but there is no disguising the fact that this hotel is past its prime. The décor is tired and in need of a facelift, but the place itself is clean. We had a room on the ground floor, so opening the window was an issue. I would guess that the bed is rather old, and not very comfortable, but a good size. The lights in the room are not very bright so you have to go to the bathroom for applying makeup. The bathroom is clean but the grouting in the shower (above the bath) could do with a go seeing to with lots of bleach. The shower itself is powerful (but clearly quite old) and I'm not sure I really like the use of a shower curtain rather than a screen - pretty sure it's not terrifically hygienic! We didn't use the bar or restaurant facilities, so can't comment on them. Plenty of on-site parking and the grounds look well-kept.

title: Afternoon tea
text:
My husband and I went here for an afternoon tea for two. We paid £17.50 each and anticipated that it was the correct price for an afternoon tea in a modest hotel. 
Well ! The surroundings are fab,with good views of the downs etc. The Hotel itself was a little sad in places and could do with updating. For example the dining room chairs looked if they were from the 1980s and the dining tables were all bare "mock "wood with no cloths and décor really not so pretty...minor points I know but they all add to the ambience. Tea consisted of a pot of tea or coffee (very good tea) each which could be replenished again and again; A plate of 9 finger sandwiches was bought to the table. Rather unsophisticated in that they were very large , not the freshest and each one had just one crust left on ! At the same time as the sandwiches, a tiered cake plate was added to the table. This was the most hilarious thing as it was so wonky and wobbly and chipped around the edges, we had to prop it up on the table using a serviette! On the tiered plate there were 2 fruit scones some jam and cream. the scones were very nice and homemade too...by far the best part of the tea.There was a choice of cakes on the bottom layer. Consisting of 2 bitter chocolate mini eclairs,2 cream meringues and 2 massive "builder like " portions of the weirdest cake ever. It looked and tasted somewhat like carrot cake mixed with ginger cake! On top of each slice was the most disgusting "frosting" and I use that term very loosly...it was slimy, oily and taste free. It almost slipped off the cake top...yuk. Service from a solitary Maître'd was just that...solitary. Don't think that this was my best experience of an afternoon tea, cant imagine they have many returners. In fact as we left we were going to voice our comments to the Maître'd but he vanished into thin air. COULD DO SO SO MUCH BETTER

title: Excellent base for a stay of any length.
text:
We arrived on Friday 6th May having read awful reviews, we did not have a high expectation.  However, the only smell was the wonderful fresh fragrance of new carpet !  There was absolutely no sign of work being done, and the staff told me that the work had been completed ahead of schedule. They also told me that areas undergoing renovation had been cordoned off accordingly. And that disputes notified whilst the parties are still on-site are ALWAYS addressed immediately. I have absolutely no reason to question this.

 We found the staff to be helpful and courteous at all times. I can only assume that people complain AFTER they leave . . . Mmmmm.

Our room (205) was entirely satisfactory, comfortable and well-supplied. And allowed the pure joy of being able to open a hotel window to allow air in !!! It was very quiet, despite there being a wedding party staying and all rooms fully booked. This meant one morning with a buffet breakfast (obviously, to cope with the numbers involved and no delays), but the food was fresh and frequently replenished. The other mornings we had table service with food cooked to order.

The grounds and environs are very pleasing, especially if you are lucky enough to have good weather.  It is a central location for visiting much of the South Coast area, with good road links.

My one criticism would be that I considered the restaurant dinner menu to be very limited in choice, and unimaginative in content. However we did have two dinners there that were okay, but not inspiring or memorable. They did the job, but that was all.

I would certainly recommend this hotel.

ownerResponse.text:
Delighted you enjoyed your stay  despite your reservations . 

Thank you for your honest comments , we appreciate the good and average and will work harder to improve those and continue to offer great customer service 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Unexpectedly good restaurant
text:
Stayed for two nights for business. This is a hotel that seems a little dated but the informative, if unusual, letter I was handed at check-in told me the carpets were being replaced and suggests the proprietors know that the buildings needs a bit of sprucing up. 

The front desk staff were friendly but unable to help provide the toiletries that should be available according to the guest handbook - apparently they haven't had toothpaste for some time now, though the garage I was apologetically directed to had everything I needed. 

The lobby smells a bit like cabbage and I wondered if that was a foreshadowing of dinner. Happily it was not and the restaurant, as mentioned above, is surprisingly good. Food was a little slow to arrive but once it did it was obvious that time had been taken to prepare an excellent standard of cuisine. It is very reasonably priced, too, given the quality. 

The bar manager was particularly engaging, quite rightly berating my colleague for not finishing her dessert while being the kind of barman who knows not to keep all of his bottles of ale in the fridge. 

If I lived nearby, I'd be back to that restaurant often.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your lovely feedback about our restaurant and bar service and the quality of our food , the team will be pleased to see this . Apologies about the toothbrushes , I have now ordered some more. 

We are in the middle of an £80000 refurbishment programme of our corridors and the letter was purely  an apology for any disruption , this may cause . I hope that you like the result when you return . We are constantly investing in the property and whilst of course more could always be done , we are always looking at ways to improve the overall guest experience and I am delighted you were looked after 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Something Missing
text:
I stayed in the hotel for 2 nights, I had booked 4, but a funeral and an early finish of work meant I only needed 2. The hotel offered no compromise or refund of the nights not required. I do understand hotel policy, but customer satisfaction and retention must be important too, there seemed such desperation to obtain my payment for all nights. 

The hotel itself is in need of attention, it reminded me of hotels I stayed in when I was a child in the 70's. The smell of paint was very strong in the corridor to my room, which was clean and big, the bed however, was far too hard and the pillows awful. There was no heating so the room was freezing.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments after your recent stay . 

Please could I ask you to contact myself regarding your late cancellation fees as we would ,not normally charge so rigidly but each circumstance is treated on it's merits and  I am unaware of your situation .

Please accept my apologies for the paint smell , as indicated by the apology notices around the hotel we are  in the middle of an £80000 refurbishment of our corridors and public areas ti improve the hotel and inevitably some residual paint smell stays for a few days . 

Every room has an individual radiator turned on and off at the radiator , similar to a domestic system . As each guest has different needs it may have needed turning on , but my apologies it felt cold to you . 

I look forward to speaking to you in person

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Given voucher for meal and decided to stay overnight
text: Room really nice as able to take dogs so did wonder if would be ok but was really nice. On ground floor near car park which did seem to be populated by loud people who either had no home to go to or didn't want to go in to the building ! Not too good if you have young children as language was rather colourful shall we say ! Not fault of hotel ! Food in evening was really good quality - well presented and served by nicely dressed helpful staff. Breakfast was same - good choices, well presented by very helpful staff.

ownerResponse.text:

Please come and see us again soon with the dogs

title: Wi-fi slower than dial up used to be
text:
The hotel looked good on the website, however it did not match up to its claim. The wi-fi was terrible, taking minutes to load simple pages, over 30 minutes just to download the paper. Ridiculous in 21st century. The room was slightly tired, a cracked sink.

The staff were welcoming and polite. The breakfast was fine and had good service. There was a good selection of cooked items which could be ordered.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments after your stay last week  ,Delighted to read you were looked after and had a good breakfast . we do get occasional issues with our wifi but this week have just upgraded some of our routers and when I tested it on Bank Holiday Sunday you will be pleased to know it was very speedy on phones , laptops and tablets 

Apologies  that you experienced a temporary blip

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Wedding
text:
My wife and I had our wedding here in February. 

I've read some reviews saying the hotel is awful. We had no complaints at all in fact we had a lovely day. 

The staff are very friendly and couldn't do enough to for us. 

The wedding day itself was lovely and everyone who attended said how thoughtful the staff were. Attention to detail was exceptional. 

The food was lovely, hot and no one had to wait, again some people wanted different choices and this was no trouble at all. 

Giles looked after us from the minute we went to look at the venue right the way through to check out. So thank you very much Giles, you and the team made us feel very special. 

Ok so the hotel isn't 5 star but your not paying 5 star prices. The rooms are £80 per night and this includes a monster full English. 

The rooms are clean and comfortable. 

Put it this way on our return from honeymoon we stayed 1 night at Cisswood house in Horsham expecting something very nice, wrong!! £85 for a night and didn't include breakfast this was another £18 joke and it was over cooked and not much at all. 

The rooms were dirty and dated in need of some attention. 

So bottom line, we had a great time hear and no 1 had a complaint about anything and yes I would stay here again. 

Thank you to all the staff who made our wedding day so special.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time and trouble to put up some feedback . It was a pleasure looking after you both and your family and friends , as with all Weddings we pride ourselves on our service and giving you the special personal day . 

The team enjoyed making the day special and thank you for the chocolates you dropped in on your return. WE look forward to seeing you again soon 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Very average afternoon tea in bland surroundings
text:
This would be two and a half and not three if possible.
We won 'Afternoon Tea for two' at our annual Fun Day last July and have finally found a gap in the Hickstead hotel's diary that suits us and them.  I did look on here before we went but always take mixed reviews with a pinch of salt as not everything suits everybody.  However, I have to agree with all those who say the room is bland - brown and tired - bare tables with no tablecloths.  There was no choice of tea and pots brought out with no hot water for top ups, although pot refills were offered.  The food, well, the sandwiches were presented without a choice or garnish, and considering there were two of us, there were an uneven numbers.

The cakes - where do I start!  The scones had been liberally dusted with icing sugar to cover up how brown they were.  I think they were trying to match the decor. No soggy bottoms here. More like crunchy.  I am not sure if anything was homemade. The lemon cake was so dry we came home and had some of the one I had made!  Although the size of the cakes was good, the quality was poor.

The whole experience is what you make it. Perhaps it has been a busy weekend and they have fallen back on freezer/cupboard standbys?  Perhaps they are short of staff?
Whatever the answer I feel the other people in the room who had paid for their tea were badly let down this afternoon and the hotel did itself no good on the 'word of mouth' front.

ownerResponse.text:
Please accept my apologies that you afternoon tea was not as good and experience as it should have been . We get great feedback the vast majority of the time , but from what you describe , it would appear that one of our more junior chefs overbaked some items , something we checked into and discovered 

Everything is made at the hotel and we take pride in offering a nice experience which is appreciated by most people . 

If you would like to come back and have a complimentary tea made properly and to our normal standard , please let me know 

My apologies again 

Giles Paul

General Manager

title: Shocking
text: Room is fine apart from hearing the chap in the next roomi vomiting and swearing! Food well ....... ordered steak and after 45 mins waiting apparently they had none left, nor lamb, nor scallops so ordered linguini decided not to eat the shellfish as it looked emmmm pale won't stay here again

ownerResponse.text:
My apologies once again for your bad experience . As  noted by another guest on trip advisor we lost the main chef that night to cluster headaches and then junior chefs did not cope that well , a one off situation , although regrettable it has been resolved and indeed having spoken to you the following day and apologised and explained I was disappointed to read about it as well

Kind Regards

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: wedding coordination and venue excellent
text: A warm friendly welcome started our stay off on a good footing.We were welcomed as wedding guests as were the rest of the party.Excellent location and plenty of amenities nearby.The wedding co ordinate did an excellent job Well Done.Bedroom lighting really made my stay under par as unable to see clothes in wardrobe and dressing and make up without mirror light very testing.Bedside lighting also inadequate for reading.Hopefully this can be rectified.Thanks for a Happy Wedding

ownerResponse.text:
I am delighted you enjoyed the Wedding as a guest with us and can assure you that lighting is something we are looking into as part of our on going refurbishment programme 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: A Beautiful Wedding
text:
We stayed at the Hickstead to celebrate the wedding of our dear friends and from their feedback to us, we know that you went over and above the call of duty to make their day as perfect as possible. 

Our two day stay was comfortable and pleasant. The food was great and all staff members were helpful and polite. 

The room where the wedding reception was held looked stunning and it was clear that you went to a lot of trouble to make things look as lovely as they did. So, as you can see, the above is all positive.  

I hope the management do actually take these reviews on board as they say they do, as there is a big BUT here......

What possessed you, (on a day when there was a gale so ferocious that is was given a name - Katie) to think it was ok for the room where the wedding ceremony was held to have a broken window that could not be closed? 

It was clear that the metal in the frame was broken. We did try to close it but couldn't get it past about a 3 inch gap. This meant that a) the temperature in the room was freezing (no wonder the bride was shaking) and b) those sat in the vicinity of the window (us), were  absolutely frozen and subject to rainy blasts coming through. 

This seems such a shame. Quite clearly you would have known about it as the people setting the room up would have seen and felt it. Plus, this room was used twice during the day, so the problem could  have been sorted in the meantime. 

To be honest, gaffer tape may not have looked pretty but your guests would have been far more comfortable. 

So  well done for laying on a superb wedding for our friends, and 
FIX THE WINDOW!

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments following the recent Wedding you attended of Barbara and Ian , I am delighted you enjoyed our efforts and service . We spend many hours preparing and trying to give service that makes the special day perfect for all guests 

And now the window . As the manager running the day , let me apologise and explain. 

The ceremony room had been checked and five minutes before getting you all upstairs the Wedding Videographer  went through the window on to the flat roof left the window open in the very windy weather and a gust caught it and buckled it completely , he then appeared apologetically and told me . I then had about five minutes to try and pull it to , which I tried to do . It was well beyond gaffer tape but I did try to move some chairs away from it and really I then just had to leave it open two inches and hope we did not get a squawl . Unluckily we did have a 5 minute downpour severe wind gust . 

We would never leave something like this but had little choice , I did mean to catch up with you on the day but just could not find the right moment whilst looking after the day  and the other 70 guests

You will be pleased to read that it was fixed first thing on the Tuesday after Easter Monday by a professional glazier who commented he had never seen such a distorted frame

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Don't knock it!
text:
As an employee of HPC compressed air systems based in Burgess Hill, this hotel is always prebooked and ready to slip into after I've driven the 5 hrs  (after dealing with the southern drivers ) from the laid back West Country- Plymouth.
As a regular that has to forfeit his time on the road dealing with customers, my time spent here is what I need after a hard day in the classroom undertaking training. 
The staff are always welcoming, and for the morning breakfast stint, there is one member of staff who knows me by my first name...
For those who have given negative reports on this place, don't knock it. It's a 20 min drive to the nearest international airport; so close for those who need an overnight stopover to recharge their batteries, or those who just need to wind down after an intense absorption of air compressor components. 😉

We look forward to seeing you again soon

General Manager

title: Don't stay here if you want a good nights sleep
text:
There is so much wrong with this hotel, that I’ll start with the good stuff as that’s the easy part.

The wifi is free and there is no onerous logging on or remembering passwords, it’s open and it’s free.
The room itself was clean and a good size
There is plenty of parking
The breakfast was nice.

Now here comes the bad…

The walls are extremely thin.  I was in room 112 which had the option to be an adjoining room to the room next door.  The door was obviously locked as this option was not taken.  The guests next door arrived back at gone midnight and I could hear every part of their conversation and every word on the television programme they were watching.  They were not being particularly loud, just having a normal conversation.  I was shocked and rather annoyed having been woken up.

There is insufficient lighting – 4 wall lights and that is it.  Fine for the evening wind down but I was trying to work having got back from my meeting earlier than expected and the light was fading, meaning I was struggling to see.

The mattress was rock hard, and I like a firm mattress but this was beyond firm and like sleeping on a plank of wood.  Several times I woke up in pain where my normal sleeping position was impossible due to how hard the mattress was.

The room was also incredibly hot, even though the radiator was off.  I overheated several times.  Opening the window was an option, however it is near a busy road which could be heard.

The bathroom was not as clean as I would have liked.  Small hairs were still present in the sink and the grouting was in need of a good scrub.  There was also mould on the ceiling above the shower.

When  I was checking out, the room had been prepaid but I had to tell the receptionist that who was trying to get me to pay.  She said ‘what makes you thin it was prepaid?’ as if I was trying it on.  I  had to forward my hotels.com booking details to her to prove it – after everything this was quite ridiculous.  I also wasn't asked if my stay was ok, meaning I couldn't provide this feedback to enable them to make improvements.

It was a business trip, and I didn’t get to choose the hotel but will be feeding back to avoid it in the future.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Louise 

I was really disappointed and concerned to read your comments following your recent business visit . 

I really must apologise for the several reasons which disturbed your sleep . I wish we had  ha,d  the opportunity to discuss the issues whilst you were with us and indeed we could have easily moved you to a quieter room with a softer bed away from other guests and the main road, actually about a mile away  . I would be happy to speak to you directly about the issues if you would contact myself by email on gm.hickstead@classiclodges.co.uk

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: Yummy Cakes and Relaxing Afternoon Tea
text: The staff were very welcoming and friendly. We booked to have an afternoon tea at the Hotel and it was served in a quiet and relaxing room with comfortable chairs, great atmosphere for a nice tea. We were offered three choices of sandwiches salmon, egg mayonnaise and ham and mustard which my partner really enjoyed however I am not really a fish or egg fan but the ham was lovely. We were then offered a selection of home made cakes and a scone with jam and cream. All the cakes were absolutely delicious and their was a nice selection. We were  offered an endless supply of tea and coffee. We also chose to stay the night in the Hotel too. The room was very spacious, clean and met our expectations. The room was very comfortable to stay in for the night and breakfast was included in the price. The only thing I would say is that at breakfast time it was a little busy and some guests had to wait for the buffet to be replenished however it did not take long for the food to come out. The breakfast was your typical full English and the food was lovely but maybe a little bit more choice of hot food would be nice. There was also a cold breakfast option of cereal, fruit, yoghurts and pastries available. The pastry I did have was very yummy but again there was only two left for me to chose from. All in all our Hotel experience was very good and I would defiantly be recommending it to my friends and we will also be staying here again too.

ownerResponse.text:
Delighted to read your comments after you leisure visit to the hotel for afternoon tea and then an overnight stay . 

I am glad you were well looked after and take on board the breakfast comments , something we work hard on to get right for our guests and which we almost achieved with yourselves 

We hope to welcome you back soon 

Kind Regards

General Manager

title: Overpriced and a bit dated
text: This hotel is slightly dated and the room is not that comfortable. I would say it is very overpriced for what you get. The room had the bare essentials but nothing more. The best part of it was the breakfast and the staff were very friendly. Will not be visiting again

title: Wedding delight
text:
My son and his fiancée settled on The Hickstead for a Christmas wedding. As it was between Christmas and New Year, we more or less had the place to ourselves.

A number of us stayed the night before the wedding and they provided an excellent buffet supper for about 30 guests.

Huge car park available - shouldn't be an issue if hotel is fully booked. No vending machines for late night snacks, but there is service station about 5 / 10 minutes walk away.

The rooms were comfortable ( we lucky enough to be on the ground floor very close to the reception) and spacious. Huge bathroom and large towels (often lacking!). Only down side for our room was that the laundry lorry came early one morning and parked outside our window. I notice the Sussex Search and Rescue team seemed to be based here as well.

Members of the party who had family rooms were given inter connecting rooms rather than cramming lots of beds into one room. Nice touch.

Free  WiFi available which didn't seem to suffer with loads of people logging on to the system.

Breakfast was buffet style and plenty of choice from continental style to full English. 

The wedding had been meticulously planned by our new daughter-in-law and overseen by Giles from the hotel. There never seemed anything that fazed him and the level of service from him and the staff was second to none. Full marks all round.

The wedding 'breakfast' (never understood that term) was first class and good alternative veggie and childrens' meals provided. Top notch DJ for the evening entertainment. Evening buffet provided for the guests and a huge pile of sandwiches provided for the late night revellers.

The two best men (we don't anything by half) needed a projector and screen for their speech and this was readily supplied and put in place when needed by the hotel team. The fab Giles again.

Breakfast the next day was again brilliant and no pressure to get us out of the rooms  by the correct time. The team were again helpful with regard to storing presents and dishing out wedding cake to the departing guests.

Cannot recommend strongly enough and will be returning in a few months when I seem to be acting as best man at another ceremony. 

I would say this is neither a budget hotel or luxury hotel but middle of the road, good old fashioned service from a caring and attentive staff. Did I mention Giles?

We had a slight hiccough when an elderly aunt fell over outside but again hotel staff helpful and caring. Thanks goodness for a couple of paramedics in the bridal party. 

(Sorry I can't provide any photos - they seem to be mostly wedding pictures)

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your very comprehensive feedback following the Wedding last week . it was a pleasure for myself and the team to look after you all and we were delighted that it all went well . 

It is very rewarding to us all when our efforts are recognised on such an important occasion 

I look forward to seeing you again in July 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Wouldn't stay here again
text: Nearly all of the staff (cleaners and waiters) were very friendly and the breakfast was the best part of the stay, the full English was really good! The first thing we noticed was the overwhelming strong odour in the passage and room when we went up the stairs. The air smelled like an industrial strength cleaner or some kind of highly scented air spray, a very stale incredibly strong odour which permeated everything. (I really don't know what caused it) We could barely breathe so we slept with the window wide open as the whole room needed a serious airing. The room was a good size but was a bit run down overall, a loose tile on the bathroom floor in front of the sink, stains on the bed and carpet. Could do with general updating. There was NO hot water so it was like doing the "ice bucket challenge" in the shower in the morning. We would have accepted this at a small B&B but at a hotel we expected a hot shower for our money. The pillows were flat like pancakes, like resting your head on a bag of cement powder. Between the smell and the uncomfortable pillow, I had a splitting headache the next morning. When we mentioned the cold shower and pillow to the receptionist her manner and reply left us feeling slightly fobbed off. All anyone want's is a good night's rest, a good breakfast and a hot shower. We got 1 out of 3. We were relieved that we had only booked for 1 night.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to  post a comment 

I was however very dismayed to read about the issues of which I knew nothing and would be pleased if you would call me on 01444 248023 or email me on gm.hickstead@classiclodges.co.uk with contact details to allow me to discuss the issues with you in person , We normally have no issues with hot water , in fact even when full it is in plentiful supply and I was very disappointed to hear that you felt fobbed off by the staff member you dealt with , all are equipped with the skills and authority to sort out any issues , I look forward to talking to you once you get in touch

title: Night away - October 2015
text:
My Partner booked a special treat for us to have a night away at the Hickstead Hotel.  This is about our fifth visit to the hotel and we always feel at home here.
We had booked a Dinner, Bed and Breakfast package inc a large glass of wine package directly with the hotel.  On arrival we were greeted by the receptionist who recognised us from our last visit and although we were early our room was ready.  We spent the afternoon in Brighton before heading back for our dinner booked for 7.30pm.  Giles the General Manager was on hand to show us to our table and again recognised us from the last time.  Our three course meal was excellent and the menus enabled us to pick and choose courses all of excellent quality and very well presented.  As a three star hotel other establishments can learn a great deal from this hotel.  In the morning we plumped for a cooked breakfast which was cooked to order and there were a great selection of cereals, croissants and fruit.  Emily our server was very attentive and an asset to the team.  Thank you Giles and the team for another excellent stay we will be back very soon.

title: Afternoon tea
text: We were given a voucher for afternoon tea. After several attempts at booking a convenient date and dealing with a unfriendly receptionist we finally arrived to have our tea. The receptionist was not very attentive an carried on talking to her colleague making us feel unimportant. She then led us to a table in a stark dining room. We were seated very close to two other couples having the same afternoon tea. Although the food itself was plentiful and looked great the whole experience could have been made better. First of all the tables could have been spread out a little so that you could have a romantic time with your other half without the people on the next table listening to your every word. We did ask to be moved but was told we couldn't because we had to sit where it had been laid for tea... a cup and saucer and a plate! I had visions of a nice white table cloth and a pot of tea with water etc. but the table was bare and cold and although we did get a pot of tea it was too strong to drink as I like weak black tea. The music was also awful. There was pop music playing loudly in the restaurant.  The other couples were offered more drinks but we were left until we had finished before we were asked. The waiters and waitresses were professional but only popped in a couple of times, otherwise we felt abandoned. Whilst the chef had done a good job with the cakes and sandwiches it was let down by the organisation in the restaurant. Would we go again? No.

ownerResponse.text:

title: Overnight stay
text:
My partner and i have been visiting this hotel for over 4 years, and this was around our 6th stay.  This was the first hotel we stayed in after we met in 2011, and it's so lovely, and has such special memories, we try to visit at least once or twice a year.  It's a great location for us as we are frequent visitors to Brighton, and The Hickstead is approx a 20 minute drive away, and although not far from the motorway, is in a peaceful rural area.

The rooms are tastefully decorated, and comfortable, the staff are always curteous and polite, and the food in the Oak Tree restaurant inside the hotel is excellent.  Compliments to the chef again.  I chose a tomato soup, double lamb cutlet followed by dark chocolate mousse with shortbread biscuits!  Absolutely delicious as the food always is here, including a quality full English the next morning too.  Thank you again to everyone at The Hickstead - See you soon!

See you for number 7 soon 

Giles

title: Amazing Wedding!
text:
My sister and (new!) brother in law got married at this hotel on a Friday. Everything was perfect! Giles was, and is, amazing! He is not afraid to get stuck in and help out, and nothing was too much trouble. 
The hotel is in a perfect location, the food was brilliant and the rooms perfect. Dinner was lovely, leak & potato soup to start, chicken with rosti and veg for main and profiteroles for desert, breakfast was also lovely, both hot and cold options available. After the wedding a few of us sat in the bar and they did not shut the bar until we were finished! Think we had the poor bar maid up until 2am!! 
Thank you Giles and the Hickstead Hotel for a wonderful wedding package!!

title: Quiet hotel away from the bustle of Brighton
text: Four of us stayed here last night for a one night break. We were looking for accommodation in Brighton but were put off by the expensive prices! We came across this lovely hotel hidden 12 miles away in the countryside. The rooms were a generous size with a double bed and tea & coffee facilities. We had a three course meal which was beautiful, every meal was made fresh and was really tasty. We also had a free glass of wine within the offer. The breakfast was really nice with a large selection of cereals, cooked breakfast, fruit juices, yoghurt, fruit and croissants. We were also given a pot of tea and coffee each as well as a round of toast. Would definitely recommend this hotel if on a break in Brighton but don't want to pay top end prices for one night.

Our room 226 was on the first floor close to the stairs so during the night people were heard on the wooden stairs. The room it's self was comfortable and had nice little touches like fruit and a paper. In all a good place to stay. Right now comes the yes or no bit...
Would we recommend the hotel.... Yes we would.

title: More tea? That would have been nice.
text: Second tea we've had here, both using. Virgin Vouchers. Really glad we didn't pay. The dining room is like an office canteen, lacking decor or atmosphere. So this time we asked if there was a garden so that we could have tea outside with the dog on a beautiful sunny Sunday afternoon. The garden was actually the car park outside the main entrance with tables on the Tarmac. Apart from the lady in reception the five minutes of service we got was noticeable for the lack of smile or talking. Champagne tea for two is half a bottle which gives you a good couple of glasses but the rest was a left down. One and a half rounds of sandwiches (egg, ham and salmon) a scone, a meragine complete with blonde hair, a sponge neither of us fancied and a soft shortcake biscuit. We were totally ignored once the tea had been dumped, no refills or usual "how is everything?" visit. We gave up after an hour of cold tea and walked the ten foot to our car. One advantage of tea on the Tarmac!  There are so many quaint places for afternoon tea around this part of Sussex. This ain't quaint and won't be getting a further visit.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback following your recent return tea visit . 

I have made the assumption that you received a better service on your first visit or you may not have returned but must apologise that you did not get topped up and checked once you decided to sit outside . I would be pleased if you would email me directly , so I can identify who was on duty and find out why you were not offered our normal level of service . I can be reached on gm.hickstead@classiclodges.co.uk . 

We serve many teas every day and the vast majority have a good experience from a service and food quality perspective but we are concerned when we get this sort of feedback and want to learn the lessons and improve . I look forward to hearing from you

Kind Regards

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Afternoon Tea for 2
text: We were really looking forward to it. Have to be honest, was a little disappointing. Firstly, we were sat in front of patio doors looking out on a car park. These doors were covered in the biggest spider webs I've seen and huge spiders, which was difficult for my partner to relax and enjoy herself, as she he has a real phobia of spiders.  We were offered tea or coffee, no choice of which type of tea or coffee. The sandwiches were nice enough, but it would have been nice to have been asked what our preference was for fillings. The scones were lovely and warm and the selection of cakes were good. Overall, it was the little things that were missing, no table cloth, tea served in a metal teapot, not china. Not being asked where we would like to sit. It was a gloriously sunny day and would have been nice to have sat outside. It was very nice but not the tea for two experience I was expecting.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to write a review and please accept my apologies that you were not offered the choice of teas by our staff or the sandwich choices when you booked with us in the first instance . 

We have had a couple of new waiting personnel on recently and this has identified a training need at our end , which we will address as we do many afternoon teas and generally get excellent feedback . 

We do also offer people to sit outside , though the weather is so changeable at present this can be a bit of a lottery but perhaps we should be more proactive in offering the facility . 

I am glad that you enjoyed your tea but acknowledge that we could and will do better

title: Lovely hotel for Gatwick & Brigton
text: We booked this hotel for an overnight stay whilst dropping our daughter off for university in Brighton. We hadn't stayed here before but wow what a good find. We booked two rooms but I had accidentally booked two doubles win I needed a double and a twin. No problem, rooms changed by staff who even showed us to our rooms. Perfectly located adjacent rooms. Rooms were a good size and tidy with all the comforts needed. We ate in the restaurant that evening with a good choice on the menu. We'd also booked the b & b option and enjoyed a very generous breakfast the following morning. All of the staff were very friendly and noticing we had a child with special needs went out of their way to make our stay really comfortable. We'll definitely be booking again for overnight stays when we travel to Brighton or before flying from Gatwick.

title: Average but nothing special
text:
We were given an afternoon tea voucher for this hotel so as it was our anniversary too decided to book for dinner, bed and breakfast as well. 
We arrived and were shown to our room by a very helpful lady from reception who then spent time helping us decide what to do in the area. 
Later I went to reception again to ask a question and another lady was there then who really gave the impression she couldn't be bothered.
Went into dinner in the evening and was quite frankly unimpressed with the selection on the menu. When the meal came is was nicely cooked and presented (must have decent chefs) but we felt the quality of the ingredients could be better.
The mattress on the bed felt hard and a bit lumpy and even with the window open the room felt hot. There was only a heavy quilt on the bed so sweltered all night which kept us awake, so not a good night! 
Breakfast was good and plenty of choice. We went out for the morning and arrived back for our afternoon tea which was very disappointing for the money. There were a few finger sandwiches, one scone with cream and jam and three other cream cakes. It would of been nice to have some plain cakes on offer instead of so many rich creamy ones. We have had several afternoon teas in various places and this was by far the worst. 
One other comment is the fact the car park has only one disabled space, which was occupied. The car park was very full so I had to park quite a distance away from the main entrance which made it difficult for my husband. When I asked about this at reception I was told that they had only ever had one space, as if that made it ok!
Overall we were not very happy with our stay and certainly would not recommend it to others.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments , I will email you privately to see if I can get a little more feedback from you .

We do value customer feedback and I was disappointed and surprised to receive negative feedback on our afternoon tea which normally gets great reports . 

I have made a note to look at our disabled car parking situation , not an issue that has  cropped up before as we have may spaces near the front door but something we will now look at . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Slightly disappointing
text:
We stayed here for 2 nights as part of a long weekend break. The room itself was nice and had everything we expected, but the bed was very hard, and the bathroom definitely needed some attention – some tiles at the end of the bath were loose, the rail and tray over the bath were rusting, the grouting was going black, and a couple of floor tiles were also loose. The receptionists were welcoming, as was the young lady in the bar and restaurant. We had a very nice meal in the restaurant on Sunday evening, and the breakfasts were also very good with a reasonable, although not extensive, choice. The breakfast staff on Monday morning were great, but the Sunday morning staff left a lot to be desired – there was a lot of wandering about and no clearing of plates until tables had been vacated – I had to physically call one over and hand up my dirty plate in order to get it off the table so that I could enjoy my last coffee without looking at leftovers.  The lounge area had some comfortable chairs but it wasn’t a welcoming area – and it was all very . . . brown. I think the problem is the steep rate that was charged – it raises your expectations of the quality of accommodation that you’re going to get, and my expectations just weren’t met. We spent the following night in a hotel on the outskirts of Ipswich for half the price, in a former manor house that was well looked after, well run, beautifully furnished and had a sense of luxury about it, and also had a fitness centre and pool. 
On a plus, this hotel is well placed for touring the South Downs and visiting other attractions, and the Royal Spice restaurant in nearby Burgess Hill does a most excellent curry.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback . 

We always strive to improve and learn from feedback and so I was disappointed to read your comments about the bedroom facilities , which are maintained on a daily basis . . 

Food quality and service is also a key area and clearing plates is a normal part of a busy Sunday service , so I am really sorry you were missed on this occasion . 

I would be pleased if you would contact myself so I could discuss this with you in more detail as we are always keen to offer value for money , which it appears you did not feel you received and we would like to try and address this with you . We always prefer to deal with these issues at the time of stay , but as this did not happen , we will try to rectify this now for you 

I look forward to hearing from you 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Disappointing
text: Stayed here for one night for work, I opted for this hotel thinking it would be a step up from the travelodge. I was wrong, overall the hotel is very tired, chairs in the restaurant were grubby, the water in the sink took ages to drain, the mattress was old and the scrambled egg I ordered was shocking. I fed this back to the hotel. I would not recommend.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback . As I was on duty during your stay both in he evening and at breakfast time , I would have liked to have had the opportunity to rectify any issues that you had during your stay but the first myself or the team were aware that there was an issue was when you returned the scrambled eggs and left the restaurant, giving them no opportunity to even speak to the chef or get you some fresh egg cooked more to your liking . 

It is our first complaint about scrambled egg cooked by our chef who does about 50 breakfasts every day and who has been with us for 3 plus years , but we are not perfect and eggs are a very  specific taste requirement , so we are always happy to cook not only to order but also to specific requirements 

Our restaurant chairs did need a clean and have now had that done and your sink was made free flowing later that day , once we found it ourselves . 

I fully appreciate your points but always prefer to sort things out directly rather than as a result of a computer review

My apologies that you encountered these issues 

Kind Regards

General Manager

text: Lovely afternoon tea, lots of cake and sandwiches however, did not get our champagne! Guess I should have asked at time but was enjoying the fun experience I forgot. Shame as this was a gift. Staff were very nice.

text: We recently stayed here after attending a friends wedding that was also held at the hotel.  The staff were incredible. title: The Hickstead Hotel
text:
I arrived just before 9 pm. this hotel is in the middle of nowhere. The restaurant was closed and it was only touch n go whether I would be fed. Luckily Chef agreed and his food is excellent as was Head Chefs . Not good though if you are a late arrival.
I found the hotel to have deteriorated visually since my last visit. The bedroom had a strange smell/ window had to be manhandled to close/ bath tap dripped/ room needed renovation/ no heating and thin bedding. Price per night too expensive for what is on offer.
No lift and poor ladies had to manhandle my luggage upstairs- most willingly I may add.
However some redeeming points-
The staff are lovely. The food is superb.  The outside gardens/ views are lovely too. 
chef/ pleasant staff/ Giles is a nice manager
Classic Hotels you need to spend some money here for clients to continue staying.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback , I was pleased to see that you were well looked after and disappointed to read that you felt the facilities were not quite up to scratch as our maintenance team work hard to ensure facilities are as good as possible . As regards the lack of a lift , we have only one upper floor reached by about 16 steps and half our  bedrooms are on the ground floor , so a pity  we were not aware of the issue as we could have moved you . likewise the heating had been switched off as normal for the end of May but could have been put back on if requested . 

Please get in touch with me when you are coming back and I will ensure you are more comfortable 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul

title: Afternoon tea booking
text: I tried to book afternoon tea but was told "we're full as we don't have enough staff".  Well here's an idea - get some more so you can actually provide a service!  This was on a Sunday morning for that afternoon, so not exactly last minute.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments following your phone call yesterday morning . 

I was sorry to read your comments and can assure you that staffing was not an issue but more the fact that all our space had already been booked for yesterday . Staffing of course and getting the right amount is always an issue so we can provide the proper level of service to all our customers . 

I appreciate your disappointment but we do not want to double book tables or rush people as we are short of space and we hope to welcome you back in the near future for an enjoyable experience 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Lovely location and very welcoming
text: We had a lovely relaxing stay last weekend, the staff without exception were friendly and welcoming. Our room was lovely, fresh and clean, the bed linen was spotless and very comfortable. The breakfasts are very good and again the service is excellent. We had some very helpful advice on planning our trip to Brighton on the Saturday and even maps were provided. Good location, we visited Brighton and Hever Castle.

ownerResponse.text:
I was really pleased that you had a lovely weekend and were well looked after .Delighted also that you enjoyed your day visit to Brighton 

We hope to see you back soon 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: Fantastic afternoon tea !!!
text: Must say how never had such a scrumptious tea ! This quaint ,cosy welcoming hotel is a gem !! A 3 tier cake stand full of homemade cakes all my favourites and so fresh !! Tasty sandwiches with a large variety of fillings with crusts cut off to perfection ! Then scones all homemade with endless cream !! What made it so special was the warm welcoming and friendly staff  especially our waitress (ellie) couldn't do enough for us ! They don't rush you and even have board games available to play while enjoying the lovely country views !! Definitely recommend this hotel !! So enjoyable booked for Mother's Day !!!

ownerResponse.text:
Delighted you enjoyed your tea and were well looked after . 

Do come back and see us before Mother's Day 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Friendly with great service as always
text: We had the pleasure of an extended visit of over a week while work was done on our house. As usual we were made to feel very welcome and greeted like old friends. As we had an extended stay we had an opportunity to sample the food at the restaurant. We knew it was good but it seems to have got even better! The quality is superb and we relished every meal we had here. A big Thank You to everyone - Reception, Night Porters, Cleaners, Chefs, Restaurant staff and of course Hassan! Thank you Giles for a brilliant Hotel which we can always rely upon!

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback after your distress visit caused by your house flood problems . I hope that you are making some progress and I am pleased that we had the chance to look after you again  and the team will be delighted to see that their efforts are appreciated
Kind Regards

Giles 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Welcoming receptionist and good breakfast
text: The receptionist was always pleasant and even managed to get me some green tea for our room. The beds were comfy, perhaps the room was a little too warm at times. Facilities were ok but nicely clean. Breakfast was nicely done. Little things like qood quality cooking of the scrambled eggs, tasty mushrooms and grilled tomatoes. The croissants were delicious and crisp. I enjoyed a good size pot of green tea, from which there was a selection of tea-bags. The gentleman serving tried his best to check that everything was good. All in all, very welcoming.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your warm response to your recent visit 

I was delighted to read that you had an enjoyable stay and look forward to welcoming you back again later in the summer . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Beautiful surroundings;comfortable room
text:
Stayed on the Saturday night before the Brighton marathon(my husband was running).Pleased with room and hotel itself.Easy journey into Brighton. Very helpful staff before stay when asking questions regarding breakfast availability due to checking out early.
Would love to stay again

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback. 
Delighted to  read you enjoyed your stay and hope your husband raised lots of money. 

I was pleased to read that you appreciated the efforts we made to cater for the diet of the marathon runners for dinner and breakfast and the early porridge breakfast offered on sunday taken up by about 40 people attending the marathon .

We would love to see you back on a more leisurely footing 

Kind Regards

Giles Paul  
General Manager

title: Great alternative to expensive Brighton hotels!
text: Nice, comfortable hotel. Extremely polite and helpful staff. Although there was a small hiccup with our breakfast (the orders got mixed up, and we ended waiting for ages), the waitress was very apologetic, and we ended up getting our food at 50% of the price. Plus  food was delicious (both cooked breakfast and an evening meal). Free and very quick wifi. And all this for a very reasonable price. Great base for exploring Brighton and the South Downs without breaking a bank.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for the lovely comments . I was aware of the mix up at breakfast and tried to catch you over the weekend to apologise personally . The chef was very upset that he had got his days mixed up and closed down some of the ovens thinking it was a weekday , which ultimately caused your delay but I am delighted you were dealt with efficiently and enjoyed your stay  overall 
Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Nice atmosphere, but in need of refurbishment
text: I stayed one night, having read other reviews n this hotel prior to my stay. I agree with others, that the hotel badly needs upgrading. The whole place is about 10 years out of date and has not been well maintained. I observed rotten window frames, poor decorations, broken tiles in bathroom, etc. it is a shame because the hotel is in a good location. I found the staff to be very friendly and efficient and it looked like they had a number of regular guests staying who were looked after well by the staff. I had a meal in the bar and was disapponted with the food - nothing special. Breakfast was ok. Compared with other hotels I have stayed at in the area, this does not provide the best value for money. I would only stay there again if my usual hotels were not available, but would consider it again if refurbished/upgraded.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your response  and comments following your recent visit 

I  was  extremely pleased to see that you received good customer service from our friendly team and  for your comments about the treatment of our other regulars . 

I was however concerned about your comments regarding our hotel maintenance . 

WE take this seriously and whilst we have not had a major bedroom refurbishment for a few years we are constantly doing things within the property and the rooms are maintained on a daily basis . Perhaps you could let me know which room you were in , so I can address  the specific points to ensure other guests get a better experience 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Hotel and wedding venue
text:
Attended a wedding here, what a great service they do. Very polished and professional.
I could not fault the food, the tomato soup was the best I have had, the breakfast had everything we wanted. 
The check in and out process was easy. 
The staff were helpful and pleasant.
My only niggle was the noise at the back of the hotel when the DJ was leaving, but that only lasted around 15mins.
Beds have a very hard mattress

ownerResponse.text:
Weddings are so personal and so important and special a once in a lifetime day and we recognise this and really try to deliver. It is therefore really pleasing and gratifying for myself and the team to read such nice feedback 

Thank you 

Giles Paul General Manager

title: Tired and in Middle of nowhere
text: Start with the positives, staff are friendly, courteous and give good service; rooms are fairly spacious and clean, nice breakfast. Negatives, whilst clean and kept as nice as can be expected, it is old a bit tired with no spa facilities (some arrangement to use facility down road at cost). It seems to be off away from Haywards Heath in middle of nowhere.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your thoughts and the time  taken to recognise the nice friendly service you received . 

I note your comforts about wear and tear and cam assure you we are constantly maintaining our bedrooms and investing in different parts of the building . 

We have  not had a spa facility or pool for over 7 years , so was a little confused as to the comment about it , but we are located  in rural Sussex  actually very close to Burgess Hill and Victoria Park Industrial Estate  = 6 minutes , the A23 to Brighton - 2 minutes but at the same time nestled below the downs peaceful and with a lovely panorama   . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Pleasant but average
text:
Friends room had defective heating ,it took an hour to get another room even though he hotel was not full. Dinner was just average. Staff very pleasant and attempted to be helpful.
 This was the 5th Classic lodge we have stayed at and ranks 4/5!

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your feedback after your stay with your friends.

I do agree that you friends were regrettably kept waiting for a little too long relaxing having a cup of tea  whilst we tried to ease the radiator valve  and I did apologise to them but it should have been resolved more speedily . 

I am glad to read that you had good , friendly service and a little disappointed that you felt dinner was average as we normally get excellent feedback but without more specific feedback a little difficult to comment . Please feel free to contact myself to discuss this further 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Well run hotel with good customer service
text: We stayed for 1 night in order to visit Brighton which was a 20 minute drive away. The girl on reception was very welcoming and check in quick and easy. Our superior room was warm and well decorated . We had a bottle of Prosecco chilling in bucket and bottles of water and fruit bowl on the table . We had a very good meal in the restaurant with excellent bottle of Rioja . Service was efficient and friendly . Breakfast again was very good with a good choice of cereals fruit youghurts and fresh croissants plus a full English breakfast. An enjoyable stay . Would recommend

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you  for your lovely comments after this weekend . I am delighted that you enjoyed your first visit and were well looked after .

We  ;look forward to seeing you again when visiting the area or maybe another classic lodge . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: Weekend Break
text:
Having stayed many times at The Hickstead i'm afraid to say that the Wedding that was taking place this weekend unfortunately seemed to take over the Hotel.
Wedding guests were everywhere!!!! We had a lovely meal as usual and hoping for a drink in the bar afterwards, but  had to retreat to our room (4 guests) at 9.15pm as there was literally nowhere to sit. Sorry to say, spoilt our stay and we felt that non Wedding guests were not too important. Have stayed before when a Wedding has been taking place with no problem.
Saying that The Hickstead is still a super little Hotel with great offers.

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Paul 

Thank you for your comments good and bad following your latest visit . 

I am really sorry to hear that you felt invaded by wedding guests .We do try hard to manage the business as we are very conscious of individual guests as well the Wedding guests and they have their own party room and bar to alleviate this and normally after around 1930 the lounge becomes quieter to allow diners to use the facilities peacefully .

This weekend there were 19 children at the Wedding which I am sure may have been a factor in the invasive feeling and I can assure you we will continue to look at ways of improving this situation and ultimately the experience you have . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: Nice day for a white wedding
text: Stayed here for a wedding. Not the kind of place I'd fly solo and I tend to prefer places that have  a lot of character to them. The rooms weren't feats of grand design but they were comfortable, spacious and good value for money. Nothing special, but nothing wrong with them either. The staff however were a delight, they made the wedding and the stay absolutely fantastic, the food was good and the service was flexible and unfailing kind always going the extra mile. Definitely recommend them for functions parties and weddings.

ownerResponse.text:

This will really please the team , so thank you again 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Bad Smell of Sewerage in Reception!
text: I have stayed here many times, on several occasions there has been a smell of drains/sewerage in the reception area. This time the smell hit you as you walked in and could be smelt in communal areas. One staff member shrugged their shoulders, the other said they would "spray the air". Some serious drainage redial work required. I will be looking to stay elsewhere until this is sorted.

text: We were looking to get away for the night and chose the Hickstead as we have been several times before. On arrival we were welcomed by the head receptionist who recognised us. The food and service were excellent as always but we noticed a few new faces and had been informed Jenny had left. Still a very warm welcome by the newbys and we had a very enjoyable evening. We will be back in the spring for sure.

title: Can't cook poached eggs!
text: I stay here regularly and the chefs seem to be completely incapable of cooking poached eggs. Even when I specifically ask for them to be soft, they come out with the yolks almost completely solid. One of the easiest things in the world to cook, but they can't seem to get it right. I want runny yolks!

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments about Poached Eggs , 

We have taken this up with our Breakfast Chef who is perfecting the art , so that we can improve in an area we thought we were doing alright . We are also looking a dispensing breakfast more quickly once it has been cooked as poached eggs particularly do not hold well on a hot pass and do become overcooked very easily . 

When you are next staying , please speak to Deborah or myself , so we can deal with your issue personally as we would prefer to on a face to face individual basis , rather than trying to identify you through tripadvisor

title: Let there be light. Please.
text: Excellent, helpful staff, great food, comfortable beds. When we arrived (December 21) the heating was off in both our adjoining rooms, and there was no travel cot, but the heating was turned on immediately we pointed it out, and a travel cot found (actually, two, as the first could not be erected properly). Our only complaint is that the rooms (at least both of ours) are too dark. There is no overhead illumination, only wall uplighters. This meant the only way we could find stuff in our suitcase was by using our mobile phones as torches!

ownerResponse.text:

title: 60th birthday party
text:
Only stayed for one night but room was very spacious and surprisingly quiet considering a DJ was playing down below. Queen sized bed was very comfortable. Breakfast was pretty standard although the sausages looked undercooked and I sent mine back to be cooked a bit more.
All in all was good value for the money and would not hesitate to stay again.

Thank you for your feedback following the 60th party celebrations and your overnight stay .

text: We stayed here in December 2014, the second time this year. On both occasions we found the food to be excellent and well above the rating of the hotel and plenty of it. text: I took my four employees, helpers and partners for a Christmas Dinner.  This is the second year we have done this and I found it as usual excellent. title: Sack the DJ
text:
Went here for a Christmas party at work last week and found parts of the stay very disappointing! The hotel looked tired and in need of repair, the bar area was small and not very festive.

The rooms were very cold and could not get the heating to work!

The food for the Christmas party was excellent, and service was good!
 
The room where the event was being held was cold and had the air conditioning on in December!

The DJ was out of date, rude, and he made inappropriate comments that were racist, and sexist, sack the DJ, put the heating on and some Christmas cheer and things would be much improved.

ownerResponse.text:
Following my attempt to contact you separately by email  to discuss your comments particularly regarding the DJ and the lack of response back to me , I was sorry to read your thoughts . My DJ is concerned and upset  that anyone has interpreted his words in this way  

I contacted other parties who ate on the same night, all of whom enjoyed the food facilities and entertainment and having served around 1200 people over this period with only positive comments apart from yourself I need to find out in more detail what the issues were  . I would be really pleased if you would change your mind and contact me directly to discuss your issues in more detail with me , so we can improve what we offer and ensure we do not offend anyone else .  

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Emma and Lee's wedding
text:
My daughter and her husband had their wedding on the 5th of December, what an exceptional wedding it was. The staff were friendly helpful and made the day perfect

The venue was brilliant, and incredibly pretty, made only better by how smooth and hassle free the whole day was. I really couldn't of hoped for a better result.

title: Emma & Lee's Wedding

title: My Niece's wedding
text: Attended a Family wedding and stayed 2 nights. As I have travelled a lot and stayed at 5 star hotels was not expecting to much. HOW WRONG WAS I. The staff were wonderful everything for the wedding went like clockwork and the food was amazing. Every member of staff greeted you with a smile and nothing was to much trouble. I would recommend any one to arrange a function here.  I honestly can not find anything that was not good about this stay. And I will be back for sure

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for such lovely comments following your attendance at your niece's Wedding .
