Kind Regards

Giles Paul

title: Needs lots of TLC
text: Stayed Friday and Saturday night here for my sons wedding. I booked this some 16 months ago.I had planned to arrive mid afternoon and eat in the restaurant on the Friday evening. Just a few days before my arrival I was informed that I could no longer use the restaurant as they had a wedding and a Christmas party that evening.I did ask whether we could have room service and was advised that they may be able to do sandwiches but we would have to order as we booked in but it could take hours! I opted to go to a local restaurant first, which delayed our arrival.On arrival we booked in, was given a key but no offer of any help to the room ( I am a disabled person). The room, 105 was reasonable in size but really smelt so first thing was to open the window. When it came to closing it at midnight it became problematic. The window would not close. Eventually the night porter managed to close it, but we were unable to open it for the rest of the stay.The en-suite bathroom is very tired, (as is all the hotel). The toilet seat was so loose, just hanging on. Someone else's hair on the toilet and in the bath!! The tiles around the toilet were loose & broken and the grout non existent! The bedding was clean but bed hard and the noise from the corridor went on most the night. Looking forward to a shower turned out to be a disappointment. There are no disabled rooms in the hotel and all the baths have a shower over, which is great if you are able bodied. Having been lifted into the bath I then found that the shower had only lukewarm water that quickly turned to cold! We reported this to the reception and was told they would sort it for us. Breakfast was a good choice, a buffet help yourself and the staff attentive but limited for vegetarians. The wedding breakfast was lovely and the room was made to look amazing.So up Sunday morning looking forward to a hot shower, once again having to be lifted into the bath to find we had just freezing cold water!! NOT impressed. I spoke with the manager Giles, who informed that the thermostat had gone and was down to the age. Couldn't get it fixed till Monday. Yet no one had bothered to let us know or offer us another room to shower in.I have seen on TA that the week before someone else had stayed in the same room,105 and had experienced the same problem with the shower. Whilst they reflected the problems in my bill, only paid for one night, I still feel cheated and feel I wasted my money. A final word....about time this hotel put in at least one room with a shower in it to be disable friendly. Would I stay here again....NO.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments which disappointed and surprised me , especially as I spent a good 30 minutes with you discussing your shower issue and offering to move you as we could not fix the issue until Monday .During this time none of your other thoughts , comments and issues were even raised on a one to one basis with myself , which is a little disappointing as perhaps some of them could have been resolved at the time . 

We do however take all comments and complaints seriously  and we have had lovely feedback in general from the Bride and Groom about the Wedding . The shower was fixed with a new thermostat on Monday and the other issues will all be looked into 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Our wedding
text: We held our wedding at the hickstead hotel and it was beautiful,  the staff are fantastic. I can't fault the staff or hotel as they have all been very helpful in making our wedding day as special as it was. Unfortunately I did hear that orders got mixed up at the wedding dinner, and the beds were hard. Apart from that a lovely place to stay. Would definitely be visiting again :-)

title: Afternoon tea
text: This was a gift for our wedding anniversary, the staff and the food were very nice with  small selection of sandwiches and cakes ,the tea was hot. We were the first to arrive and there wasn't much of an atmosphere and the room was a little chilly. We had finished in 45mins ,much the same as others who had arrived.

title: Could do better
text:
We stayed at the Hickstead on a Saturday night. Plenty of room in the car park. I was not impressed by reception, the welcome was not warm, the girl was flustered by the arrival of guests to a wedding. Our room was not ready until late in the afternoon. The room was tired, the bed and the pillows were very hard. There was plenty of hot water in the bath taps but it was not reaching the shower which we could not get to heat up. The cleanliness of our bathroom (room 105) was not up to my standards!  On the other hand, there were good tea & coffee-making facilities. 

The main problem was noise. There was a wedding and reception at the hotel on the day we stayed, with many children. A group of these were running up and down the corridors on the ground floor and the floor above, banging doors and screaming until late evening. It was difficult to sleep with this noise. I did complain to the receptionist, but action taken, if there was any, was not effective. Because of the number of people moving around there was a lot of rubbish and debris along the corridors that gave a messy appearance to the hotel lobby, bad and corridors.

Breakfast was good although the only eggs on offer were scrambled. Diet or sugar-free lemonade/Sprite or 7up was not available in the bar. The orders for the main course and dessert served at the wedding reception was all mixed up with many people complaining that they got the wrong food.  I feel the hotel could have done better all round

title: Very good food and service
text: Rooms well appointed - lovely to have tea/coffee tray that actually had a teapot. Food very good. Autumn deal very good value. Building itself rather functional - dining room would be improved with tablecloths.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your reason feedback , delighted that you enjoyed your stay 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Memorable Tea.
text:
We were given a present of a voucher for Afternoon Tea at this hotel so duly phoned and booked a date.
Never having been to this hotel before we were pleasantly surprised to discover it in fine open countryside in spite of being so near to Gatwick airport. The main building is a lovely old country house that has been carefully brought up to date with good taste and quality fittings.
The Tea was served in the dining room that has excellent views of the gardens surrounding the hotel.
The Tea itself was more like a meal with an excellent selection of sandwiches, beautiful warm homemade scones and a mouth watering selection of delicious cakes; the size of the tea was too much for our modest appetites so we asked the waiter if we could take the surplus home and they willingly wrapped the items and put them in a bag for us.
The service was very efficient and the staff were all friendly and helpful and made sure we had an enjoyable experience at The Hickstead.
We shall certainly return and we also know what to give special people as a present - a Tea Voucher !

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your extensive words following your visit for afternoon tea . 

Really pleased to read you had a nice relaxed experience with good food and friendly service . 

I hope you enjoyed the doggy bag at home later in the evening 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: We loved the warm welcome!
text: The overwhelming comment for this hotel is a very big thank you to the staff for their overwhelming welcome and their friendliness.  It certainly makes this hotel special and I hope the management take time to praise their staff for this essential ingredient.  The bedroom was clean and spacious and the bed was extremely comfortable (and big).  The evening meal was fantastic and the quality extremely high so complements to the chefs... We had seabass, mackerel pate, goats cheese and the fish and chips... Brillilliant!  Breakfast was standard but very nicely served and cooked.  Often, it is the little things which make a difference and The Hickstead understands these and includes them.  I would very much recommend a stay at this delightful establishment.  Thank you Hickstead.

title: Fair to Middling
text: We booked a double room here as we were visiting relatives and then going into London for the day the following day, so it was handy and a good price.  There was a private function going on when we arrived, which rang a few alarm bells but we'd booked so we took the chance.  We couldn't get to the bar as although the private party had their own function room, they had taken possession of the bar area too, and although you were paying guests, they made you feel as if you were intruding.  The hotel staff did nothing to try and accommodate us even though they could see we were a put put out.  If we had wanted to eat in the dining room, we could have been accommodated but we didn't want to, we just wanted a drink, so we went without.  The receptionist who booked us in was quite ungracious to start with as we had to summon her via phone from elsewhere, but she managed to crack a tepid smile and asked a few scripted questions.  The room when we got up to it was fine.  It was a good size with a table and two hard arm chairs, but the lighting was so gloomy it was untrue.  There were tea and coffee making facilities but no bottled water.  We could have ordered some drinks from room service but objected to paying £2.50 extra just because we couldn't actually get to the bar!  The room looked clean, but you couldn't tell as it was so dark!  The window didn't open fully as it had a safety device fitted and we couldn't be bothered to try and summon help from reception from little miss helpful.  The bathroom was tired.  Grout in floor tiles missing, the chrome fittings all rusty and the shower curtain skimmed the edge of the bath.  The shower was efficient but you constantly had a large rusty chrome hand rail poking in your side.  The water also ran a bit hot and cold when the room next door turned their shower on.  The bed was clean but very hard, but extra pillows were stashed in storage bags in the wardrobe.  Towels were the usual grey, skinny, hard offerings and only one large one and a hand towel.  Good job we didn't need the complimentary toiletries as they weren't of an exceptionally high standard and had been there a while judging by the covering of dust.  The walls are paper thin and you could hear every word said in the adjacent rooms even though other guests weren't talking particularly loudly.  About 9.30 we were disturbed by the children belonging to the private party using the corridors as a race track.  There was a group of them thundering up and down the corridors screaming and shouting and banging the fire escape stair doors.  No one was supervising them or seemed to care.  We rang reception, no one answered, so my husband went out and asked them to be quiet, and they scarpered.  We could hear the music from the private function and to be fair the guests were not awfully considerate when returning to their rooms in the early hours, but the hotel can't be held responsible for that.   We went down to breakfast and the ladies in the the dining room were very helpful and pleasant.  We were shown to a table for two and helped ourselves to the plentiful buffet.  One minor query though, cooked breakfast without fried eggs, not even an offer to cook them fresh, just scrambled.  The rest was very nice though.  Then the private function group arrived from the night before and they proceeded to seat them all around us!  Once again we were made to feel we were intruding  so made a hasty exit.  It was fine for one night, but I certainly wouldn't return or recommend it to anyone who wanted anything more than just somewhere to lay their heads for the night.  And then only if they had ear plugs.

title: Unimpressed
text:
When I arrived,  there was no recored of my booking as it had ben cancelled by the Central reservations ten minutes after me making it, for some reason. No matter, they had a room spare so I took that.  The room was separated from the next with a door, and the people in the twin room next to me were talking loudly and animatedly and watching TV, perhaps with other people present (I know it was a twin as the receptionist insisted there were only two people in there 'because it was a twin').

Again no matter, I moved to another room which was was separated by an actual wall from the neighbouring rooms. 

I could smell smoke in the bathrooms of both rooms, not just a trace, as if someone was actually in there smoking.  At first I thought it was people in the next room, but the receptionist assured me it came from outside. I realised it must come in through some kind of ventilation system right into the bathrooms from people standing outside,  which to my mind is more of a problem than one rogue guest smoking in their room.

Dinner was overpriced but not awful, I thought for a £13 salad there would be some substance and/or style, but they were stingy with items that were not lettuce and it was rather generic and insubstantial. 

I filled in the breakfast card to order room service and one of the items I ticked was 'fresh fruit platter' - this turned out to be a bowl of tinned mandarin and grapefruit segments, and prunes. I don't think this can claim to be fresh fruit platter by even the most generous definition. 

The worst thing, and probably what drives me to leave the review in the first place, and mention all the other things, is the fact that even rooms separated from their neighbours with real floors and walls are poorly soundproofed. I was kept awake by one of my neighbours snoring, I can hear a phone vibrating in the next room, I can hear every footstep on the room above me.

I don't understand why hotels claiming to hold themselves to a high standard, such as this one, can put such little emphasis on quality of the primary service they are supposed to provide - somewhere to sleep. Subjecting the customer to all the intimate nocturnal snorting and groaning of their fellow guests, and at the same time depriving them of their own privacy by separating then with such thin walls, makes the experience no better than staying in a cheap hostel. For all its airs and graces, I think this hotel has its priorities wrong.

The reception staff are pleasant and polite, which is important,  so I give it two stars.

title: Mealtime Lottery
text:
Stayed here for business. The room is big, bed comfortable and does what a hotel bedroom should do.
I had dinner last night, and chose Chicken Caesar salad. The waitress asked if I would like the anchovies, and I thankfully said no. I hate anchovies.
The Caesar salad was brought out with anchovies. Clearly chef believes that a Caesar salad should have anchovies. Anyway, I picked them out of the salad, and finished my meal.
Next morning, came down to breakfast.
Ordered my cooked breakfast, eggs, bacon, sausage, mushrooms, beans and black pudding. Only when it was brought out to me, chef had obviously decided that a cooked breakfast wasn't complete without fried bread, and I couldn't have black pudding.
I asked to swap the crispy fried bread for a piece of black pudding and was served a slice of barely warm, and suspiciously undercooked black pudding. If only it had been of the same crispiness as the fried bread..
So mealtimes at the Hickstead are a bit of a lottery.

title: Was Dinner worth the waiting for
text:
I booked a room for a one night stay at the Hicksted Hotel. 
As I walked in reception told me before I put my bags down that the restaurant was vary busy tonight and there would not be a table before 8pm. No problem I said as it was 7.15pm at that time.
Well the rooms are ok and clean, the public areas are a little tired and a bit late 80s and the grounds not loved,but for £89.00 B&B that's ok in this part of the world.

When I came down into the bar at 8.15 I herd the restaurant manger tell the barmen not to let any more diners in as they can not cope tonight.
The restaurant had only 24 covers/diners in it, considering it has 50 covers in it so only just over half full I can't image what the service would be like if they had all the tables full.

So I decide to have a beer and come back at 9.pm When I came back I was met with we are vary busy and it will be vary slow service tonight from the manger.
Well in the end I got a vary over cooked piece of pork and the bread served as I finished my main dish and the water never came.
The manger then offered the tables all round me free Port to keep the peace as the service had been a vary poor but forgot to offer any to my table who had had to wait since 8.15 for dinner.
 
Was it worth the waiting I think not and we still have the fun of  breakfast to look forward to.

ownerResponse.text:
Please accept my apologies for the overall service you received in our dining area during your visit . We had an unexpectedly busy night which does happen and this was not controled or handled very well by our supervisory staff as you have detailed . This is not a normal night where we regualrly do 30 plus covers but as we are a corporate hotel midweek it is hard sometimes to gauge how busy you are going to be and staff accordingly . Room was spacious and everything worked - only constructive criticism was that the lighting was not really adequate for the whole room.
Restaurant was above usual chain hotel standard, and the food was well prepared with a varied menu, which had different specials every day.
All the staff were helpful, cheerful and welcoming.
It is well situated for a wide variety of leisure trips in this area of Sussex.
We will recommend this hotel to our friends and family, and we will certainly stay here again.

title: Lovely Hotel
text: Stayed  with my daughter in a very large twin bedded room (room215). We stayed here because the location is only half a mile from Hickstead show ground. The beds were comfortable, the room spotless and very quiet being at the end of the building.  The staff were friendly and helpful. We had an evening meal at the hotel.  The main course was excellent, but the dessert did not come up to the same standard.  This is my only criticism of this otherwise lovely hotel.  Would most definitely stay again..

title: A sussex gem
text: we stayed here very much by chance , we were going to stay near by but told it was full when got there . But what a superb place .We stayed on a special offer of room evening dinner with glass of wine and breakfast . The receptionist was so friendly as too were the other staff, room was spotless very attractive sunny position . Food was fantastic 3 course dinner brilliant and the breakfast was a true full english . WELL DONE to everyone involved

title: Nice hotel with stunning views
text:
Driving up the long driveway to a large carpark, a very warm and friendly welcome from the receptionist. Told me anything I need to know. 
The room was nice and spacious, clean.  A very large and comfortable bed, slept well in it. 
Shower was good, powerful and hot 
Wifi was good though out the hotel. Good breakfast lot's to offer, very friendly staff. 
Just a couple of bad points, 1st you can hear every room doorbeing shut, late in the evening and early morning and 2nd the evening meal was nice don't get me wrong but a bit on the pricey side for what you get. 
But all in all a superb hotel would stay again.

title: Grim
text:
Stayed one night whilst on business.  Room 'tired' looking and smelled stale, needed a good dust.  Shower curtain inadequate so bathroom floor dangerous after using shower.  
Room service food inedible.  Breakfast adequate.
Staff pleasant and apologetic, and insisted I not pay for food, although I would have been happy to pay for breakfast.
Adequate as an hotel but in no way does it live up to its advertisments.  Better to just convert to a care home and be done with it....  
Travelodge down the road better value and cleaner

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments following ypur recent visit to The Hickstead . 

Having met you at Breakfast to discuss the issue of a disappointing dinner , where your tuna was undercooked through an error of the chef reading the order incorrectly , I was a little disappointed to dicover that you had other issues which were not mentoned face to face at all , but were just aired on trip advisor , giving me no opportunity to investigate any of them . 

We pride ourselves on the customer service and presenting a clean hotel - noted consistently by others on trip advisor , so I was surprised to read your comments . 

I would welcome ther opportunity to talk with you again and to show you that we can do a good job of meeting your expectations and would ask that you contact me directly next time you are in the area on business .

Giles Paul General Manager

title: Pleasant Stay
text: Stayed at this hotel for a couple of nights. Staff were very helpful and the breakfasts were very good. Like other comments which have been made the Hotel is looking very tired and needs painting. The room was comfortable enough and we had tea making facilities and we had clean sheets and towels on both nights. I am sure they would get more five star ratings if they were to refurbish the corridors and couple that with a coat of paint. We did enjoy our stay and would recommend it as it is in a beautiful area and convenient for Brighton.

title: Pleasant stay
text:
My wife and I use Classic Lodges because they are reasonably priced in good locations
I would class this as a good average Hotel...Staff very helpful in all departments  no complaints with room very tidy  but stairs and corridor looking very tired and in need of a repaint
Restaurant was nice and comfortable   the food OK bit more choice would have better also lack of choice in the wine department  no new world wines  and the quality average taste but nothing to complain about  we enjoyed our stay.

For our anniversary we didn't want over pretentious 5* (if we had, we'd have gone to one!) - we just wanted a pleasant country environment and good food! Really really worth a stay!

text: This hotel is in good proximity for Burgess Hill & Haywards Heath. Not a lot to say about it really, nothing to write home about & nothing to moan about either. So was very good value, buffet breakfast included was good too.

title: Nice enough!
text:
Booked up 1 night for our 18th wedding anniversary and did mention this on the booking form, but no offer for special requests(wine/champagne in room/chocolates/flowers etc)even though I did ask. Very friendly staff and the restaurant were very obliging when I found my dinner wasn't quite up to scratch(veg cold), but breakfast was not such a great experience. Ordered toast, tea and coffee and had to wait a considerable time for it to arrive and people ordering after us seemed to get served before!! Did find a nasty place in the dining room floor where my foot went down, think that could do with being repaired. 
Room was clean but could do with a bit of updating, bathroom wasn't the cleanest I have seen, I really do hate hairs in bath and the fan didn't work properly. The bed was fine, clean and very comfy but the heat of the room was unbearable overnight, even with the window open! Suggest maybe lighter duvets.
The area for getting into Brighton was ideal not too far but far enough out to think you are in the countryside.

title: A wonderful stay
text: I came here with my boyfriend as we were celebrating for our anniversary in Brighton and read reviews on this hotel. We caught the bus on the way in (as didn't drive) and as we are 18 normally our service at hotels have been a little distant, awkward and rude. However I can happily say that hickstead hotel made our weekend a fantastic one. The staff were so lovely to us and to all other visitors. Hannah in particular at the desk was lovely and gave us a map on brighton and helped us out with where to go. Room was clean and tidy, toiletries and coffee and tea making avaliable, some reviews have said it looks dated, well from a younger point of view I didn't see that at all! The bed was ridiculously comfy and that bath was wonderful as it filled up very quickly with a good depth. There was a useful book on our desk answering any questions and a large tv was a great addition. The atmosphere of the hotel was great from the wonderful staff and I would like to thank hickstead hotel for a lovely weekend. Will definitely be coming back when I can drive!

text: Stayed here for just 1 night on a package which included dinner, bed and breakfast plus a bottle of Prosecco.  We'd spent the day in Brighton and it's only about a 20-25 minute drive to the Hickstead Hotel.  Very quiet even with a wedding reception in full swing - they didn't disturb us.  The room was slightly dated but very clean and comfortable.  Dinner was great - food gorgeous and hot and the service from Jenny was excellent.  Breakfast next morning also great - when several young male members of the wedding party came down the restaurant manager brought out a jug of water for them as they were feeling the effects of the night before!  Nice touch I thought.  All the staff were helpful and are an asset to this hotel - would certainly recommend it and stay there again if in the area.

title: Our Third Visit
text:
This was Robert and my third visit to the Hickstead.  Arriving the weekend of the 22 March.  It is always great to come back to somewhere where they remember you and are so pleased to see you back.
We had been given an afternoon tea gift from a member of the family and we decided to make a weekend of it and stay at the Hickstead.
Robert my partner booked us in for Saturday night and when we went down to dinner we were met by Jenny, the Restaurant supervisior like an old friend! Her professionalism and personality is really infectious and she really does make you feel so very special.
I must compliment the Chef on an excellent dinner saturday night.  The food was fantastic and I was offered extra veg as I don't eat potatoes. The lemon possett was to die for! We returned on Sunday afternoon after a trip into Brighton to have our afternoon tea as there was a wedding on during Saturday, but we didint mind and the afternoon tea was delicious!  Thank goodness we didnt have lunch and had walked a fair distance before we arrived to sample a fantastic afternoon tea and to see Jenny again.
We will be back and thank you for providing such fantastic customer service.  The gentleman at Breakfast was also very professional and very attentive and the buffet breakfast is of a very high standard and plenty of it!   Jenny's daughter on reception remembered us too.
See you very soon and thank you for a very enjoyable weekend.  Mark

ownerResponse.text:

Your comments in particular have been passed on to Jenny amongst others . 

Looking forward to seeing you again soon 

Kind Regards

General Manager

title: Return Visit
text:
A return visit on the occasion of my wife's' birthday, and another very pleasant stay.  We booked - as before - upon receiving the winter break leaflet in the post.  This was for Dinner, bed and breakfast with a complimentary bottle of prosecco.
We were made aware when booking that there would be a wedding taking place on our chosen date.  We were allocated room 118 - which I note has been referred to in a previous review.  This room, along with room 117, have an interconnecting door.  I note from the comments of the previous reviewer that it could be possible to 'peep through' the keyhole, and that the reviewer did just that!  Interesting that one would want to do that, but each to their own I guess, not quite sure the reviewers motive here?  Still - I note that the keyhole has a cover over it.
Very similar to other rooms we have stayed in at this hotel - my only one gripe would be that the interconnecting door does mean you can hear the occupants of the other room - other rooms we have had in this hotel are very well insulated from noise.
As usual, the food was to a high standard, and it was nice again to be recognised and welcomed by Jenny in the restaurant.  It is this attentiveness that sees us returning - a real credit to the hotel.  With the majority of guests at the hotel attending the wedding, there were  just three tables in use in the restaurant when we dined - nice, quiet and intimate!
We did not get disturbed at all by the wedding that was in progress, although the occupants of room 117 decided upon their return from the wedding to have a chat at 3am using some wonderful colorful metaphors!  Still, judging from the way they spoke, I would not have classed them as overly intelligent, and courtesy to others may not be their priority.....
Due to the large onslaught of wedding guests expected for breakfast, there was a buffet laid on as opposed to breakfast being cooked upon request.  This was no issue, and the food was all very nice, piping hot and a great selection for all tastes.  We headed to the restaurant quite early, so it was very quiet.
So all in all another pleasant stay, and I really do not see the issue with the keyhole in room 118.
We are mindful that we were introduced to this hotel as part of a wedding party, so have the benefit of seeing both sides of the story when it comes to the individual guest/wedding guest debate!
I expect we will be back again in 2015.

title: Afternoon tea
text: Came to the Hickstead Hotel for afternoon tea. Food and service was excellent. One of the members of our party got lost as the sat nav sent her to the wrong location, and the waitress who was just going off duty offered to drive to her so that she could follow her back to the hotel. I was so impressed with this. Very friendly and helpful staff, thank you!

title: A pleasant experience
text:
This is a pleasant and peaceful hotel set in its own extensive grounds well away from road noise but excellently positioned for quick access to Brighton etc via the fast A27. Parking was free and abundant and the site was very well lit on our late night return. The hotel is basically in two parts; the original house and the more modern and corporate looking accommodation building which is more reminiscent of a Travellodge. However, that’s where the similarity ends as this hotel is very well run by smart, professional, polite and sociable front-of-house staff who are a credit to the management. 

Whilst some of the public (landing and corridor) areas in the newer building are rather tired and in need of a little refurbishment, our room was fine and on a par with any other ‘chain’ hotel. The  'house' is very smart and tastefully presented throughout, with a spacious and sunny dining/breakfast room. Service was impeccable throughout our stay and the full English breakfast was exceptionally good. At the risk of sounding controversial, I have to say it was a refreshing pleasure to be served by smiling and communicative British staff with no language or cultural issues. Our two night stay at the Hickstead was peaceful and enjoyable and we’d have no hesitation in recommending it to others and there’s a good chance we will return here during our next visit to West Sussex. All in all vey satisfied.

title: Fantastic friendly service
text: We stayed for one night on the valentine package. Although the hotel is dated the food and service more than made up for this. Fantastic hot shower to. Thanks for the lovely biscuits which were a nice touch. Staff seemed to genuinely want to give a good service. Breakfast was well cooked and had all you would want.

title: Valentines night special
text: We arrived to be welcomed very warmly, our room was very comfortable and very clean.  We went down to start our stay with a lovely gin and tonic in the lounge very nice, then relaxed changed for dinner we had lovely canapes and a glass of champagne we were then showed to our table in the very nicely set up dining room, we were asked on sitting down if we wanted water, but no wine list offered I think that is one thing that might be addressed as our starter arrived and we had to ask for the wine list also other tables had been offered bread but we had to ask, just little things but could easily be addressed the food and the staff were excellent and we thoroughly enjoyed.  We then had a few drinks in the lounge, then off to bed.  Good nights sleep very comfortable bed.  Breakfast the next morning was excellent and overall a very nice stay.

title: Fantastic cream tea with excellent service
text:
We went back for another cream tea this week and loved it. The portions are very generous - a substantially sized scone, cream eclair, meringue, cookie, slab of lemon drizzle each plus cream, jam, sandwiches and tea or coffee. They were happy to provide vegetarian sandwiches and kindly checked which fillings I would like before making them.
My companion was very happy as they made him some extra sarnies and coffee served with a smile as he liked them so much.
Had to take leftovers home to enjoy later.
A real hidden gem - go and try it.

title: Friendly and Comfortable!
text: Came on a Valentine's night special. Had a great Four course meal. Excellent food and excellent service just like you get at reception  or at the bar. Rooms are very comfortable and quiet an excellent retreat after a good evening's meal! This is the second time we have come here so it must be good!

title: Hungry
text:
We arrived at the hotel at around 8 15pm on valentines day for a romantic night away. We were called at mid day to be told there were no tables left in their bistro, but when we asked were told room service was available still (perfect)! We settled down in our room had a look at the menu and decided we would have a quick bath then order some food. The bathroom was okay but their is rust on the wall and the soap holders which is pretty grim also chips in the bath. 

We got out the bath and called room service to order, we were at this point told we couldn't order any hot food as it was past 9pm.. I looked at my watch 9.02pm. 

As someone paying for a service I would expect better than this, especially as the restaurant was full and there are no other options to dine out at this time. 

The only other thing that slightly annoyed us was that the bed was on wheels. Not great for a "romantic" night away, but this actually became quite amusing.

title: a ggod experience
text:
my wife and I really enjoyed an overnight stay
although it was rather quiet

we had a good meal but were the only poeple in the dining room
the service was excellent

the breakfast was also good

a shame more people didn't find it!

title: New Year's Eve celebration
text: Our party of 7 had a very enjoyable and memorable celebration with five course meal and excellent waiter service throughout our stay.  The resident DJ was very personable and gelled well with the guests.  The quiz played at the end of dinner and prior to the dancing starting was pitched well, and his delivery of it was fun for all.  Food was excellent and presented well and speedily.  We found all the staff really pleasant and more than willing to help.  We would definitely recommend this venue for any such celebration.  Found breakfast a little chaotic due to lack of seating as all guests had decided to descend at the same time, but again, the staff  dealt with this situation professionally.  Our bill on departure was wrong with items charged that we hadn't had, however reception rectified without question.

title: Good stay for the holidays
text: Very friendly and helpful staff combined with e good rate over the holidays made this stay just shy of four stars.  They just need to renew the beds, which were squeaky and not so comfortable and update the bathroom, which in need of sone TLC.

title: Christmas celebration
text: We were with a party of 50+ for a Christmas celebration dinner and booked in for one night so we could drink without having to worry about driving. The staff were extremely friendly and helpful and the food was some of the best we've had at similarly large dinners. The bed was very comfortable although I personally found the room rather too warm and the atmosphere very dry. From a critical point of view, an overhead light would have been helpful in the bedroom as well as the provision of bathrobes and the towels themselves were rather on the rough side. However, these slight niggles would not stop us booking at The Hickstead or any of the group's hotels in the future. Overall it was a very pleasant experience. Thank you

text: We spent one night here (room 211) and found it to be excellent, despite one or two critical reports on Tripadvisor regarding The Hickstead, I went ahead and booked. The hotel staff were charming and helpful, the building was pristine, the bed the most comfortable hotel bed we have slept in this year, nice bath, a decent sized flat screen TV -  and the full English breakfast was plentiful and freshly cooked. We would definately book in here again.

title: Delighted
text: We spent one night at the Hickstead Hotel and have to report it`s excellence. I was slightly concerned regarding some of the critical comments of The Hickstead on Trip Advisor but went ahead and booked anyway. Delightful staff, pristine throughout, probably the most comfortable bed of all hotels we have stayed in for a year, plus a first class freshly cooked full English breakfast. The hotel was in surrounding countryside so no disturbance from traffic noises. We would most definately book this hotel again.

title: Couple staying the night.
text:
My husband and myself were staying for just the one night to visit our son. Our room was fine, beds were comfortable enough as well as clean. 
We were greeted kindly by all members of staff, although it did seem a little mechanical- aside from one of the young waitresses at breakfast (I believe her name was Emily) sincerely asked us how we were and where we had travelled from; she also went out of her way to serve us specific items due to allergies etc. The young man working alongside her was also very attentative, and Jenny who served us in the evening was also lovely, with a smile on her face. 
The food was lovely and desert was particularly tasteful and presented very well! (We ordered the banana split and the treacle tart). For breakfast there was a hot Full English buffet topped up regularly with fresh food. A variety of juices were also topped up regularly, and there was also a large selection of cereals and fruits. (I would however have liked there to have been Mango juice on offer, rather than two jugs of orange.)
Location is fine, although it would be reccommended to bring your car along, as there isn't very good access to public transport, and there isn't much of anything within walking distance.
Would definitely recommend this hotel to others, but don't expect too much from it as it is only advertising for 3 stars.

ownerResponse.text:

We very much appreciate the time taken to do this and were delighted to read that you were looked after for your overnight visit . 

Please come and see us again if you are in the area again 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: ***6 STAR SERVICE***
text:
8 of us stayed here recently, occupying 4 rooms, prior to and following a family funeral in Brighton. The Hickstead is by all accounts a fairly average hotel. It is clean and well appointed, but the rooms are just starting to look a little used. All of the usual ameneties are available for a property of this type, there is tea and coffee in the room, a bar and a reasonable resturant.

What really sets the Hickstead head and shoulders above other similar hotels are the staff. Every single member of staff from the maids to the general manager were just perfect in every respect. Our party included a 2 year old and an 80 year old who both demanded special care and attention, the staff were always happy to help and do almost anything to enable us to enjoy what was actually quite a sad affair.

To cap it all, I managed to leave a suit in my room and the hotel staff posted it back to me in Nottingham. I can't imagine a Travelodge doing that?

I would reccomment the Hickstead to anyone stopping in the Burgess Hill area.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to respond at such a difficult time for you all . I am glad the team made you feel welcome and that you felt looked after . This is something we pride ourselves on 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Average stay
text:
We stayed as a part of a group booking. We paid £155.00 for two nights stay bed and breakfast. On the face of it, not bad. On the Saturday night we joined a group dinner meal that was £32.00 per head. The meal was not good value, with little or no choice and very average, so imagine how great a deal we thought we had, when today we meat a couple who had stayed for one night (Saturday) and paid £109 for room, dinner bed and breakfast. Considering we were quoted £89 as a 'special rate for the one night bed and breakfast, that means we paid £40.00 over the top for a pretty average dinner. That said the overall package was ok and I would say average. We stayed in room 202, when we arrived the room was cold, but a quick call to reception and the receptionist arrived with tools In hand to 'sort' the faulty thermostat out on the radiator, which she did, but you would have thought that this should have already been checked. The door slammed shut each time and we noticed that the door damper was dripping oil onto the carpet and had been for some time! This was reported. The rooms and the hotel are a little tired and the view from the rear of the hotel (our room) looks out onto a dump of a yard! Not a room with a view! So ask for a room facing the front of the hotel if booking.  Staff were very friendly and willing to help, so no issues there. There is an unpleasant smell in the general reception area.  Good location for visiting the area and loads of parking and it is a out of the way and quiet, except the fire alarm went off at 1.0am this morning for what reason we don't know.
Breakfast, Buffet style. Why? Why not freshly cooked, the hotel should be able to cope. I just don't like dried out and stewed food,  and uncooked mushrooms, let alone dried black pudding and bacon. Well Saturday's breakfast was not as good as Sunday's breakfast. Definitely down to the chef! Sunday's was much better.  My advise to the Hotel is to do away with the buffet breakfast, at least the cooked breakfast. Part cook the bacon and sausages and finish cooking to order, would be so much better.
Beds are hard. Bathroom was ok and shower good, but the maintenance guys need to take more attention to detail. When replacing screws on the shower handle, don't use plain steel, the rust makes down the tiles make the bath tiled area look old and tired. Not a good impression.
Would I stay here again? Probably not if I was on a personal weekend away, but for a group booking, it was probably ok. would be a reasonable wedding venue.

title: buissiness trip
text: BE WARNED!!!!!!!!!!!!!! Do not stay in room 117 or 118. The interconnecting door has an old fashioned key hole with no key placed in it.....The concern here is that you can be seen by the occupant/s in the adjoining room. I was able to see a gentleman standing in his room using his mobile phone. My concern here was that if I could see him....then he could obviously see me! I complained and was moved to another room. I was also offered 5 pounds off my bill for my trouble.The manager, Mr Paul, informed me that in a six year period (since the rooms were revamped) they have never recieced any complaints of this nature...... (very concerning) as this poses the question why not? I demanded that these rooms were taking off sale until this issue had been addressed. Mr Paul confirmed that they would not sell the rooms (other than to families) and that work would commence immediately (I am not sure I believe this statement especially as according to Mr Paul he had recieved no previous complaints). Mr Paul and his team have a duty of care to protect and maintain their guests privacy and well being. Im my opinion they have totally failed to do so. This was my second visit to the hotel but after this incident I will never stay at this hotel again and I strongly recommend that before you do that you seek confirmation that this issue has been addressed. I feel my privacy has been totally violated. Also the bill was paid in full as I felt that Mr Paul and his team believed that I was only complaining because I was looking for a discount!!!! talk about add insult to injury.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments following your recent visit 

As I discussed with you when you raised the issue , it is something which had not come up as a problem in the previous 6 years that I have been at the hotel , but indeed it is a serious issue and once pointed out it was resolved that same day as I had agreed I would do with you . 

We take comments and feedback seriously and we value comments observation and criticism so we can learn from it and improve . 

I was a little disappointed that you felt you needed to go on trip advisor when I met and gave you my personal assurances at the time , particularly as this was your second visit and you told me you had had a pleasant stay on the first occasion , but please be assured that we took your comments seriously and acted upon them . Indeed I rang you and left a message when you were out to confirm the same . 

No offence was intended by the receptionist who moved you straight away to deal with the immediate problem ,though I do understand there was no need to offer a discount as offering you an alternative room dealt with the immediate issue . 

I do hope that you might reconsider coming back to the hotel and I am very happy to discuss this again with you and will try to contact you again by telephone . 

Kind Regards

title: Not the best value for money
text:
We stayed one night while visiting friends nearby; we arrived in the middle of a wedding reception but our room was on the other side of the hotel and we couldn't hear the main party. All was good until some of the wedding guets decided to continue partying in the room beneath ours at 1.30. There was music blaring, loud drunken voices and lots of door banging. We called the night porter and by 2.30 they had quietened down enough for us to eventually get back to sleep. Obviously the hotel can't help having antisocial guests but not getting a good night's sleep puts us off having more weekends away. 
Seeing that another reviewer had a bathroom where the ventilation fan wouldn't switch off, we had one that wouldn't switch on.
On the plus side, the room was dark - no green or red LEDS glowing from smoke alarms or aircon machines, no minibar fridge to hum, no traffic noise, a fairly comfortable bed, spacious room and very friendly staff. 
We didn't get up for breakfast as we decided to sleep in after the bad night, so can't comment on the food.

title: Virgin Experience voucher
text:
Stayed 1 night on a Virgin voucher for bed breakfast and evening meal. Unlike other reviewers I was not expecting gourmet food or 5 star service, but I was very impressed with what we had. Yes the napkins were paper and there was no bread offered, but with a 3 course dinner I didn't need bread. 
The hotel was clean and well presented, with chocolates, water , robes and slippers in the room. The bathroom is a little dated with a shower curtain that attacks you as you shower, but it was clean and everything worked. 
We did have some problems with the heating, with nothing during the evening but it came on full blast during the night. I think this could be a changing season thing.
The staff were professional and pleasant. I thought the food was well presented and very tasty. The menu wasn't extensive, but there was enough choice. The waiting staff were attentive without being too fussy, ( there were only 5 tables occupied). 
Breakfast was a buffet with plenty of choice of hot food or cereals, croissants etc.
The location is good, 3 minutes off the A23, but no noise.

title: One night
text:
We went for one night as we'd been invited to a friends wedding, the reception being held in the hotel.
Reasonably priced hotel. The service was excellent from the receptionists to bar and waiting staff. The wedding celebration was a hoot, dancing and people chatting loudly. However when in our room, very near to the disco area, there was little or no noise, the fire doors suppressed all noise, and more to the point they shut quietly. No banging of corridor doors in the small hours, unlike so many other hotels we've stayed in. What a treat.
The room was newly refurbished, clean and well appointed. We asked for more tea etc (English tea drinkers !!) and it was all was delivered in 5 minutes. This in a hotel with some 150 wedding guests and hotel residents. Impressive. Definitely recommend this place as a quiet stopover while exploring Sussex.

title: Not as advertised!
text:
My wife and I booked a night in this hotel because one of our children had given us a Virgin Experience "Dining Treat for Two" voucher as a gift. From the list provided this hotel was nearest to our home and in the "blurb" it promised, "an intimate dining experience" and the experience of, "simply relax for an evening of pure indulgence". So...we expected a well cooked and professionally presented meal. We  hoped for perhaps even a gourmet meal- well  you would wouldn't you?
WRONG! First of all I must say that the staff were friendly and helpful throughout but that's as far as it went. Our table was graced with one sad and dusty silk flower with the cheapest paper napkins. There was a side plate with knife - never used because no bread was forthcoming in any shape or form. The meal itself was best described as an average to poor pub meal. 90% of the food was the commercial pre-prepared kind. My wife's meal was sent back since it was a dried up overcooked disaster.
Our room was big and clean, the bathroom had tiles lifting and a ventilation fan that never switched off - keeping my wife awake all night. At breakfast my wife's poached egg was a solid yolk surrounded by grey scum and...oh yes they forgot the bacon! The coffee was absolutely tasteless and my wife's tea left a great deal to the imagination. What a pity. Ok if you are a tired commercial traveller just wanting a nights stopover but certainly a great disappointment to us.

title: Great Peaceful Location & Friendly Efficient Staff
text:
With Brighton Hotels fully booked, I chose the Hickstead House Hotel.  It is just 20 minutes from Brighton on the A23 and very easy to find with clear 'brown' hotel signposts to guide you in.
Lauren on reception was charming and very helpful - when I booked I was told there was a large wedding party on the Saturday evening but with rooms almost fully booked I thought it was worth the risk. She again showed concern on booking in that we knew about the wedding party and advised that the fire doors would exclude any sound. 
 I need not have worried.  The disco was loud but the fire doors completely blanked out any noise and the wedding guests were careful not to disturb other hotel guests - thank you.
Our rooms (123 and 220) were spacious, very clean and peaceful, and the ensuite bathroom had an excellent powerful shower.  It is a 3-star hotel and so that is what you get although this hotel is better than any other 3-star in Brighton and far better value.
Despite the wedding party filling the bar, we were able to order late at night 22:50 some very tasty sandwiches - I had smoke salmon which was really generously filled and the egg mayonnaise was also very abundant and all came with some very well cooked chips and fresh salad.  The delightful waitress was only too pleased to serve us so late and despite being there beyond her going home time seemed genuinely pleased to help us.
Perhaps the highlight was the breakfast; the restaurant manager coped brilliantly (constantly surveying the diners to ensure our needs were met - unusual in my experience, so very well done -  and despite the packed room the service was quick and efficient. Coffee and toast appeared very promptly and I noticed that the buffet food was topped up regularly - they don't put too much out at a time in order to have it kept piping hot.  The sausages were good quality and well cooked, the back bacon was also well cooked (I hate undercooked bacon), there were fresh grilled tomoatoes, loads of mushrooms, black pudding and hot baked beans, and we ordered poached eggs which were perfectly cooked too; the scrambled egg on the buffet didn't look too inviting. There were fresh warm croissants, a choice of juices, yoghurts and fresh fruit.  The bowls of jams and marmalades looked homemade although I doubt they were and there was the largest pot of Marmite I have ever seen - well done the chefs and restaurants staff.
This was a really great value hotel in a lovely rural location with a huge car park.  The staff were always smiling and attentive - you can't ask for more than that.  We will be going back.  You will need a car or a £25 taxi ride into Brighton.

title: Lovely setting, lovely staff, lovely food.
text:
The Hickstead is a lovely three star hotel set in lovely surroundings.  

The main building where the bar,lounge,reception and dinning room are is well presented and we were warmly greeted by the receptionist.  We were booked in for a two night stay with breakfast and dinner.

The hotel rooms is the only area I could say lets the side down.  Although they are clean and tidy (and recently refurbished), the rooms on a whole do feel a little tired - our room (a deluxe double) - had water stains on the ceiling and paint peeling in the bathroom.  I felt that if they had just spent that little extra, this room really would've lived up to its name.

That has to be my only criticism, but hopefully the owners may read my review and act upon it.

The food we had was lovely and the service was prompt.

All in all, our stay was very comfortable and I would go back in the future to get away from things!

text: We had a very pleasant one night stay at The Hickstead.  Staff were very helpful and friendly.  Our room - 111 - was large and well-equipped. However - because it was a Sunday - the breakfast was buffet-style and not as hot as I would have liked it (not helped by cold plates).  Still tasty though - especially the fried bread. title: Do you like cold showers?
text:
I had fully intended to write a positive review until I woke up in the morning. I woke up to a terrible view of a scrap yard when i requested a good view and to cold water in the shower. There water was room temperature warm at best. A huge fail for standard expectations. There was a long line at the check out desk on departure. I was once a hotel manager myself so when I walked out I left the key on the desk and told the clerk that I was checking out. I did this because they took my card details on arrival and even charged 1p to it. I was then told to take my key and get in line. I waited for 20 minutes until I was able to check out. When I worked at a hotel you could leave your key and we would charge the bill to the card and send the receipt if requested. 

I booked the room based on breakfast being included but it wasn't stated that it was a buffet breakfast. This should be stated.

A real positive of the stay was that it had a good WiFi connected and the staff were very polite. I think I could do a better job of managing it though.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to write on trip advisor. 

I am very sorry that you had to endure a luke warm shower . We have normally very poerful and hot showers which you can see from other trip advisor feedback and having not checked out your shower it would appear that the cartridge containing the thermostat was beginning to wear out , something unfortunately whihc can happen without warning . I can assure you it is not the standard we would normally offer . 

As regards the delay at checkout , as you stated about working in a hotel previously , the practise of no delay checkout is now very difficult due to changes in the law regarding credit cards and confidentiality , which means we are unable to hold the three digit security numbers to process charges in the absence of guests . This effectively means that although we take your number on arrival - the 0.01 p which was taken and refunded in the same action in order for us to load your card on to our system , we cannot process anything without the customer being present or the hotel calling you afterwards . 

I am sorry that you arrived to checkout at the peak time on a Sunday and therefore had to wait a short time , but in a 52 bedroomed hotel , peaks such as thios are very rare and it is the first time in 5 years that it has been an issue in my experience . 

Breakfast is normally a plated offering but on a Sunday with 100 people in the hotel mostly coming down in the space of one hour from 09.00 - 10.00 I am sure you can appreciate it is almost impossible to get a plated food out to everyone on an individual basis .

I am delighted the staff looked after you and hope that despite your observations you may return on some future occasion . 

If you wish to discuss the matter further please feel free to contact me at the hotel . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: An excellent stay
text: Needed to be in the area overnight and the Premier was full which was a jolly good thing as I had a wonderful stay at the Hickstead Hotel.  From the moment I walked in to when I left I had fabulous service.  The friendly receptionist quickly signed me in and even came to my room to 'sort my TV out'!  I had arranged for a couple of friends who live locally to join me for a drink and Hassan, behind the bar, looked after us beautifully.  I ordered room service just before 9pm (they helpfully reminded me that 9pm was last orders) of sirloin steak, mushrooms, tomato and offered me a selection of vegetables instead of the fries.  The 'au poivre' sauce was in a separate jug.  It arrived promptly and was exceptionally good.  The steak was a good thickness and cooked perfectly and all the vegetables were delicious.  It was so good I was compelled to leave a thank you note on the tray for the chef!  The room was a very good size (Room 126), immaculately clean and well appointed.  If I was being very picky my slight niggles (which is what prevents a 5* review) are that the lighting in the room was generally dim (although I have noticed that hotels do this quite often), the bathroom floor tiling/grouting needs some attention and the very comfortable mattress was creaky on one side (quickly sorted by sleeping on the other side!).  The breakfast was superb; served by a couple of lovely and friendly staff.  The usual cereal and fruit and juice selection but it was a refreshing change not to have to cook your own toast and to have a large range of cooked breakfasts offered.  All brought with a smile and perfectly cooked.  Thank you Hickstead Hotel for a very comfortable stay.  Highly recommended.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for taking the time to post such a detailed and thorough critique of your stay with us at The Hickstead Hotel . I am delighted that you were not disappointed with your second choice of hotel and I hope when you return it will be your first choice . 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul
General Manager

title: Wedding March 2013
text:
Me and my partner had our wedding day at The Hickstead and it really did live up to the hype, it was better than we could of dreamed it would be.
From our very first meeting with Giles the general manager who also had the job of our wedding co-ordinator right through to the morning after our wedding, all the staff were very friendly and helpful, putting all the finishing touches to our day and nothing was too much to ask of them, they even made some of the wedding guests sandwiches at 2.30am!!
The bedrooms are a generous size, comfortable beds and a beautiful bathroom.. they block all the sound out and are very peaceful.
The food was out of this world, they cater for all requirements including little mouths and fussy eaters. The breakfast is amazing, you will not go home hungry!!
The staff were on hand throughout the day and got to know us as a family which added a really personal touch to our day. They were there from the crack of dawn helping the grooms party with their button holes right through to the early hours of the morning, helping the grooms party find their rooms.
We would highly reccomend The Hickstead as a wedding venue, all of our guests couldnt compliment it enough and we didnt have one complaint.
My Nan lost her engagement ring during the day and the hotel staff found it and contatced us the following day to say they would keep it safe for us to collect.

We are going back in the summer for afternoon tea in the garden... and i already cant wait!!

title: Disappointed.
text:
We were looking forward to our stay here after reading the good reviews - but we left feeling that everyone else was talking about another hotel!

First of all, the hotel is very badly signed - there is no signage off the surrounding lanes (well small one, but very hard to see, especially in the dark!)'

The rooms and some of the public area are looking a little tired and dated, our first room had no heating at all and we had to be moved to another one.  The window did not fit properly in the second room so we were aware of traffic noise most of the night.  The bed was the hardest one I have ever slept in, as were the pillows, and the duvet only just fitted the bed so we were playing "duvet wars" all night.  I have stayed in many, many hotels around the world and it was definitely the most uncomfortable bed in a hotel room.

The saving grace was the very good cooked breakfast and the pleasant hotel staff, but it certainly was not the stay we were expecting.  The Premier Inn at Burgess Hill is a much better option for this area.

title: fantastic wedding venue
text:
after reading reading a review about how disappointed a bride was with her wedding here i just had to write this and disagree.
we got married here on the 22nd of december and can only say that it was everything we wanted and more.we live about an hour and a half away from the hotel so we could have chosen a nearer location but after meeting the hotel manager giles at a wedding fair we were convinced that this was the venue for us.

title: Back Again
text:
Our third return visit since originally attending a wedding in 2010 as Best Man.  On this occasion we returned with the bride and groom.

Efficient check in, despite arriving at the same time as other guests who were arriving en-masse for a Diamond Anniversary event.

After getting settled in our rooms, we enjoyed some time relaxing in the lounge playing games supplied by the hotel.  It was very quiet and made for a pleasant afternoon.

Dinner was to the high standard we have become used to, and we enjoyed the complimentary Proseco with the meal (we were on an inclusive package).

There were only two items on the menu that I could see that incurred a supplement when using the package, neither of which I considered expensive.  3 course dinner with complimetary bottle of Proseco, bed & breakfast for under £100 per room can't be sniffed at!

Staff again were friendly, attentive and efficient - and it was nice to be remembered again by Jenny and Hannah.  We look forward to returning again.

ownerResponse.text:

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul

title: Disappointed bride.
text:
As an Events Manager myself, I thought long & hard about writing this review (I got married here in September 2012) but recently heard further bad feedback from my guests, it's just not going away!
Firstly I have to say that we had a wonderful day. The hotel is ideally situated for a wedding and we had fireworks in the  grounds - which were amazing! Dawn, the wedding planner did a great job, from start to finish and the food was great.
However, I would say that the hotel failed on organisation overall. 
I found the Restaurant Manager to be patronising, pushy & unorganised - in fact I asked several times for her to be kept away from my wedding. When I was being led to my ceremony, one of the guests was talking to my mum and brother about her bad attitude, the last thing I want to hear right then!
During the meal, I had ordered a bottle of rose for the top table, when the wine was served this not materialise until all the other wine had  been served and my mum asked for it. (Myself and my mum didnt have wine until everyone else did.)
There was a huge delay in between every course, mainly due to the fact that while we were eating there were no staff in the room. When my guests started complaining, I got up and complained to the Restaurant Manager. No bride should have to be complaining on her special day!
The restaurant staff were wearing T shirts and cardigans, but the next day, came in for breakfast in shirts and waistcoats.
We had ordered 1/2 bottle of wine per person, but only 1 glass per person came out, until I asked where it was.
In the evening, I had asked for my cake to be put on the buffet (it was meant to be put on the centre of the tables during the day, but we had  run out of time due to slow service). When I went to the toilet, I saw my beautiful cake, sitting in the service room. When I asked the manager where my cake was, she told me it was on the buffet - until I pointed out to her that I could see it!

I thought that this was it, until we had dinner with some friends last week.
There was another wedding party in house, that were getting married elsewhere. My friends said that during the ceremony, all they could hear was the reversing sound of the coach that was picking the other guests up! Surely they should have been told not to make noise during the time of our ceremony??
They also told me that our cupcakes were put in the bar (where we were sitting) after the reception and the other wedding came back and started helping themselves.

I am an events manager, so have a keener eye than most, but I am so disappointed that my day wasnt the organised day that I would have wanted it to be. I have had a response from the hotel, but no amount of free weekends away will compensate for the fact that everytime someone asks me about my wedding, I say 'we had a lovely day, but...'
One of my friends has since commented that she would never get married in a hotel after my wedding and everytime someone talks about it - it's in a negative light!

ownerResponse.text:
I was disappointed to read your comments on trip advisor and whilst it would appear that we can do nothing for you to alleviate your feelings  , I would like to reiterate that we take disappointment and lack of service very seriously .
 I understand that you may not wish to take up my offer of a  weekend away , this was not made to try  and replace your Wedding day but along with an invite to talk to me or have a meeting , it was part of an apology . I fully understand that we cannot replace your day but was a little upset you chose to go on trip advisor rather than respond to my personal letter by email or telephone . 

In any case , please accept my apologies once again that your day from an organisational point of view did not meet your expectations 

Kind Regards

title: Very accommodating, a solid night's sleep and a great breakfast
text:
We, my husband and I, stayed here for one night when hubby decided he wanted to run the Brighton half marathon (which is 20 minute drive away). We tried for ages to find somewhere actually in Brighton but a) he'd left it a bit late to organise and everywhere was full and b) everywhere that did have rooms available required a two night stay on a weekend. Unfortunately we had a family committment on the Friday so could only stay for one night. We originally had a B&B booked in Bishopstone but they were difficult (in fact slightly rude) when I asked if we could have a late check out (hubby not finishing his race until 10.30am at the earliest and needing a shower, and possibly a short rest). 

When I phoned The Hickstead to check if we could have a late check out the answer was "of course", is there a charge for that? "only if you're staying until the evening, but until 1pm we won't charge you". Brilliant. Can we have an early breakfast for hubby? Of course! Do you have gluten free bread (me being somewhat of a 'wheatard') - of course! Well, for £89 for both of us including breakfast, I'm sold. 

Finding the hotel is easy, come off the A23 onto the A2300 and head towards Bolney, there's signs for The Hickstead Hotel almost straight away. Follow the signs and you're there, down a country road with bunnies hopping off the side. The 'drive way' feels like you're entering a farm, there's a horse in a field, ducks on a pond, an angling lake and lots of fields and trees. Coming from London I started to relax immediately. The hotel building looks a little like a converted barn crossed with an office block, but it's welcoming and the surroundings are peaceful with lots of birdsong. The main car park is a good size, with an overflow car park round the back of the building. 

Check in was quick and uncomplicated, we got our bags and went up to our room. We had a standard double - which normally would be fairly small, but the size was really good. There's plenty of room to walk around without getting in each other's way or tripping over bags or clothes. The bed is a large double (hubby wasn't kicking me in the night!) and super comfy. The duvet is so soft and warm, and combined with extra pillows gave me a super night's sleep. We had a little table and chairs by the window, at which I was able to do a little work on my laptop using the free wi-fi in the hotel (no password, just log on, nice and simple). The TV is Freeview and also has Sky Sports, so we were able to watch a little sport and a film. Standard teas/coffees in the room, plus good mirrors, trouser press, wardrobe, plugs, drawers etc. Curtains are proper black out curtains which is great. Good bathroom - huge soft towels (my favourite kind), large lit mirror, body wash/soap/shower cap etc provided, super-strong shower with easy to adjust temperature, although the bath is teeny tiny (about as wide as the length of my foot). The only other downside I can think of with the room is the lighting, there's only four small wall lamps, it makes the room a little darker than I'd like. I would prefer to have one or two large ceiling lights. 

Our room was on the first floor, overlooking the overflow car park and what looks like a builders yard. Fortunately, it was the weekend so we weren't disturbed by anything going on in the yard, but I imagine during the week there may be some noise coming from there. The only other outside noise we heard was the geese and ducks wandering around the back of the building and having a go at each other, the bird song, the occasional tractor and the main road. Inside the hotel the sound proofing is good, although you can hear loud TVs in other rooms and the cleaners hoovering, but it's not too bad and I've certainly had worse. 

Breakfast is served 8am-10am on a weekend, which is a little earlier than I would have liked, but that's probably because I was so enjoying sleeping in that bed! Choice of breakfast is the usual juices, cereals, yoghurt, pastries and a good quality cooked breakfast, with gluten free bread and rooibus (red bush) tea. I do like being accommodated properly, it makes all the difference. The staff were helpful and friendly at all times, not once have I had to wait, despite the hotel being almost full and everything I needed/wanted has been provided without difficulty or issue. Plus, we stayed at the hotel until 1pm and had no problems checking out at that later time. 

Overall we've been very happy with our stay, if we ever need to stay in this area again we'll definitely consider coming back.

title: Lovely staff but hotel needs an upgrade to be 'country house'
text:
My husband and I stayed overnight with the £99 D,B&B offer (including bottle of wine) which was great value.  Surprisingly (for first weekend in Jan) there was a wedding on and we had to fight our way through the wedding line-up to get to the reception desk - not a huge deal but a little planning could have avoided this.  The receptionist was polite and welcoming.  Our room was a reasonable size and clean but decor is more on a par with a travel lodge.  We had a drink before dinner in the bar area with a very friendly barman but, again, the whole bar area could do with some focus - maybe putting in a wood burning stove or similar to give a country-house feel (as the hotel is clearly aiming for).  At the moment, there is just a large TV which made it feel slightly more like a waiting room.  Lighting was too bright as well, didn't create any intimacy which a good hotel bar should have.

There was an excellent choice of menu at dinner and the food was reasonable, however the dining room really needed an upgrade.  Not necessarily a huge spend needed but, for example, making sure that all the tables have cloths on rather than the dated placemats we had on ours. They also needed soft lighting, to draw the curtains (dark and cold outside) and light the candles to create a better atmosphere. The staff again were great which lifts this above similar hotels.

We can't argue with the value of this hotel and overall it was a good stay, however someone needs to cast a critical eye over this hotel (who knows what a real country-house hotel looks like) and make some improvements to decor.  Again, this wouldn't require a big investment but just some little touches such as flowers on the reception desk, changes to lighting, better crockery and cutlery in the dining room.

title: new years eve gala delight
text: we stayed in room 117 for the gala new years eve dinner and disco. We arrived early and was allowed to check in, the receptionist was very friendly and helpful. The room was nice, had all we required, extra pillows etc, it was very clean. The bar man was really outgoing and helpful, infact all of the staff went out of their way to ensure everything was ok. The dinner was excellent, I really could not find fault with anything. Lamb was cooked to perfection. The wine choice was ok and reasonably priced for a hotel. Cheese and port was great, but a massive portion of cheese really was enough for 2. The DJ was great and did a quizz after dinner to get everybody mixing and joining in. I was a little surprised that not all guests had made an effort with their dress, I expected it to be a black tie event and was a little shocked to see some gents without ties and jackets. This evening was not overbooked and could have catered for more guests, there were probably under 40 people. I originally tried to book some 6 weeks ago and was told that they were not catering for 2's (i was really surprised to hear this) I contacted Dawn again by email about 3 weeks ago and again was told that no couples tables were being sold, as they wanted larger groups. We managed to book on friday - I was surprised to see several 2's there, so I can only guess that didn't sell as many places as they expected. I would certainly go again next year, we usually go to a hotel in brighton, but they were hosting a wedding this year instead! The breakfast was excellent and so was the service. This is a hotel that employes staff that really care about their jobs, good old customer services goes a long way! We would definaltely come back and would recommend this place to our friends and family. The only reason I did not give 5 stars is because we had trouble booking initially. Great place keep doing what your doing The Hickstead as it is working well!

title: Everything you could wish for.
text:
We chose the Hickstead because it is part of the Classic Lodges chain and we had enjoyed our stay at The Old Swan in Harrogate. The Hickstead was rated highly by previous guests and was well located for the sort of break we were looking for (Sheffield Park, Brighton and other attractions are very close by). We initially booked three nights B & B through an internet booking agency. We were telephoned by a member of staff enquiring whether we wished to reserve a table in the restaurant for dinner. I asked if their Autumn Break deal was available as this provides exceptional value and included dinner and a bottle of wine. Happy, it was.
After a friendly welcome at reception we found the accommodation was very comfortable with a large, well furnished bedroom fitted with plenty of storage space. The bathroom was modern and well fitted including bath with power shower over.
In the attractively laid out restaurant the food was plentiful and of a high standard. There was a good choice and the service was friendly and attentive. Breakfasts were generous and cooked to order - one of the best cooked breakfasts we have enjoyed in any hotel. Again, the experience was enhanced by the charming service.
Overall, the delightful staff were the key to making our break most memorable. We highly recommend this hotel and hope to return.

title: Nice hotel, great staff
text: My wife and I have just returned from a weekend in the Hickstead Hotel, and we really enjoyed our stay there. As other reviewers have mentioned, every single member of the staff was friendly and helpful - it's a bit of a cliché but they really made us feel like valued guests. Our bedroom was large, clean and comfortable, well decorated and equipped. We ate dinner in the restaurant and there was a wide choice on the menu, the food was good quality, with generous servings. We had chosen the 2 night "garden break" which included dinner on the first night, and tickets to the nearby Borde Hill Gardens, and was excellent value for money. Overall, there's really nothing to complain about, and we would definitely recommend the Hickstead Hotel.

title: Very good hotel, with excellent staff
text:
The hotel itself was very good. The room was large and clean, and at a very good rate for the area. The only criticism I'd make was the temperature. With a maximum daytime temperature of 15C, the temperature in our room was over 23C, which was a bit uncomfortable during the night. The window could only open a small amount (understandable, being on the ground floor), and there was no air conditioning. I'm not sure I'd want to stay here in very hot weather.

The food was good, and surprisingly reasonably priced for a hotel.

But what made it for me were the excellent staff. They were all friendly, but professional, and quickly responded to all questions and requests.
