title: comfortable, friendly and good value
text:
We stayed at the hickstead for 1 night as we were attending a nearby family wedding. Check in was from 3pm but the wedding was at 2.30pm. I telephoned the day before to see if it was possible to check in earlier at 1.30ish and was told that it was possible but not definite. After an argument with satnav (ok - operator error) we actually arrived even earlier at 12.45. The lady on reception was very helpful and although our room wasn't ready, she changed us into another room and we checked in. 
The room was comfortable and clean. Certainly way better than a travelodge (which I had read on a previous review), with plenty of room and the normal facilities.
When we returned from our reception at Old Pangdean Barns, there was a wedding reception or party going on in the function room. Of course this is expected at a hotel and we found it of no trouble to us. The bar was still open to all guests.
After a good nights sleep the breakfast was excellent, with a choice of cereals, croissants, cooked, tea, coffee, juices, etc.... 
Comfortable, friendly & good value.

text: Unfortunately whilst the food and staff were fine we wouldn't stay here again, I rang this hotel prior to booking to check vegetarian options were available in the restaurant and I gave the dates I would be looking to stay, at no point was I advised a full on wedding and reception were also taking place, we could not use the bar all night, the barman suggested we sit at the end of the garden to avoid the wedding guests, then, despite requesting one of their loveliest rooms for my friends birthday, we were allocated a room overlooking the car park which was picking up wedding guests until the very early hours, who naturally were loudly celebrating their friends nuptials. I had seen previous reviews about wedding parties and stupidly thought I would've been informed, so you have been warned. this is clearly a wedding venue that squeezes additional guests in to fill the rooms. title: Good hotel with great value package
text:
Two nights mid-week at this hotel at the beginning of August using their "Discover Sussex" package - amazingly good value at £99 for three course dinner for two, bottle of house wine and double room and full English breakfasts. The meals were well prepared, with plenty of range and generous portions - unlike some places that have a restricted choice (Premier Inn's package comes to mind), the full restaurant menu was available to chose from, although there were one or two items within each course that attracted a supplement if you chose them. To be honest, the quantity of food was more than we could manage by the second evening! The staff were really good, and as the food is freshly prepared they coped admirably with a couple of special requests from us (and we heard others being treated equally as well). Congratulations to the restaurant manager who is clearly passionate about customer service!

I was a bit worried about the location as it appears to be close to the A23; but it is actually set back enough for the road noise to be a distant buzz rather than a raucous roar. With the windows closed in the East facing rooms it could not be heard, although with the windows open it was faintly audible.

The reason I've only given this hotel four out of five is that we felt the room wasn't quite perfect. All the hotel's bedrooms are accommodated in a modern block (see picture by "thekings4" taken May 2012) whilst the reception, restaurant, bar and meeting rooms are in the older house; the bedrooms would be familiar in size, style and layout to anyone who has stayed in the modern Premier Inn or Travelodge chains. The rooms are either ground or first floor and they are very generously proportioned. We asked for a quiet room and were given room 210, first floor, East facing at the end of the corridor adjacent to the South facing premium rate rooms. The room has much darker décor than the room shown in pictures on their website and other traveller's photos here - and combined with the absence of overhead lighting and the use of energy saving lamps in the wall lights this made for a dingy room. There was a small table and two chairs in the bay window; the table lamp which was on the table might have helped with the overall ambience but there wasn't a socket close enough to plug it in to! The king-size bed was equipped with a 10.5 tog duvet, which for us is a winter weight one, and the arrival of summer temperatures and the lack of air conditioning made for an uncomfortably hot night. The second night we slept with the bay window open, but were woken by geese at the adjacent fishery around dawn and then (but only having been wakened) became aware of the background hum of traffic noise. Of course, once the peak of summer has passed, temperature will no longer be an issue and other travellers might not be as affected as we were on our stay. One final criticism is that there was no obvious clock anywhere. Many hotels now-a-days have clock-alarms with i-Pad/i-Phone docking stations, and hopefully this hotel will follow suit soon.

Having said that, on the positive side there was plenty of storage to unpack and hang clothes on, the free Wi-Fi worked a treat, the flat screen TV was good, the bathroom was adequately sized, the shower worked effectively and there was a good range of toiletries supplied.

Overall, the customer service was excellent, the food fabulous, the location good and the package rate fantastic value - we would use it again, though probably not in peak summer months and we might be more choosy about the room allocated (unless room 210 hasn't yet been redecorated).

PS - If you've read RoundedGriffin's review, it focusses on a swimming pool that I don't think exists at this hotel anymore - I didn't see one and it's not mentioned on the hotel's website.

title: Incredible customer service
text: Whether it was at reception or in the restaurant, both at dinner and at breakfast, the customer service at this hotel was excellent. The hotel was clean although it could probably do with a coat of paint soon, but at this time all businesses are having to tighten belts. title: Very good value mid range hotel.
text:
We got a deal in a comfy smart deluxe room on a Saturday with dinner including bottle of wine  and breakfast for £129. Slippers, dressing gowns included. The staff are one of The Hicksteads key assets. Without fail all were charming and genuinely keen to please. 
The location is down a quite lane with fields, ducks, geese and large numbers of rabbits all enjoying what the area had to offer. Close to the A23 but very little traffic noise in the grounds and none at all in the room. 
The dinner  had a good choice and most items were included in the deal. Only one starter and a mains required any form of supplemet. As well as the main menu there was a specails menu with another good selection and we mixed and matched happily. The wine was just a chardonnay, merlot or rose. We fancied none of these so went for a Pinot Grogio and agreed to pay any extra costs. There weren't any.
The room was smart and a good size. Bathroom clean and modern with an excellent shower and super fast bath-don't leave it for more than a few seconds! One slight quibble-no conditioner and us ladies like that. Bed very comfy.
There was a wedding and that could have been noisy but heavy doors were shut and this cut all noise off.
Breakfast the next morning was delicious. All hands were to the pump and it was good to see the manager mucking in with all the other staff who were agian unfailingly polite and helpful. Training is good at this hotel. Jolly good bacon by the way!

All in all a lovely short weekend break in a lovely part of the world. The hotel is very good at what it does and its staff add that bit extra. It's not luxury nor does it pretend to be but it thoroughly desreves its high TA ratings.

ownerResponse.text:
Really delighted that you had a good Hickstead Experience . We really pride ourselves on trying to look after guests in a relaxed friendly manner and I was pleased to be spotted in Breakfast !! 

Quite normal particularly on a Weekend morning , it is after all a team effort and I am glad you experienced this in a positive way . 

We look forward to seeing you again soon on another of our super deals 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Afternoon tea
text:
Four of us went to the Hickstead for afternoon tea on Sunday afternoon. We were made very comfortable in easy chairs by a bay window. Two of our party were vegetarians. The tea was served first and was supplied with plenty of hot water for top ups. We asked the waitress twice for extra tea and coffee and this was served with no extra charge.
The vegetarian tea stand was served seperately from the normal one with fresh cakes. We were given egg mayonaise, fresh ham, and smoked salmon sandwiches and the vegetarian option was cheese and pickle, cheese salad, and egg mayonaise sandwiches. There were warm fruit scones with jam and cream (not clotted), chocolate elair, strawberry merange, lemon drizzle cake and shortbread.
We were there nearly two hours and were not hurried. All in all, very enjoyable.

title: quiet peaceful hotel
text:
Was told about Classic Lodges website froma friend of ours and booked one of their special offers at this Hotel. from the moment we got there we were made welcome and met by friendly staff. Being disabled and in a wheelciar we had booked an evening meal here to save going out looking for somwhere to get in  and all thought there was step up into the restaurant this was no problem for the staff who had a ramp in place before we arrived for our meal. the food was excellent and you wasnt rushed with the meal even with the restaurtant being busy with a large party of guests. all this made for a great and peaceful weekend for me and my wife.

If there is a negative which i hate to add after such a great time, and i only add this for any other disabled guests who use this Hotel.  And it is that in the bathroom you are unable to get under the sink to wash as it is all build in, there is also an hand rail in front of the sink and the only way i could wash was sideways on which is not easy. if you dont need a wheelchair and disabled then i dont feel this is a problem for you. Apart from this all other acess is great and like i said above the staff are more then happy to help you in anyway they can.

title: quiet weekend?
text:
Just got back from a weekend here.

The plus points - very clean and smart everywhere.  Excellent service, breakfast and room.  Staff all very smartly-dressed, clean and polite.  The surrounding fields were pleasant, with geese and rabbits outside.  Plenty of car-parking spaces.  Well-stocked tea tray in the room.  Powerful shower.  Nice coplimentary toiletries.

The down-side - The drinks were expensive.  2 pints of beer and 2 Smirnoff Ice, £20!  We didn't eat dinner here, as the prices were steep for what the meals were.  There was a wedding on yesterday.  Fine, until the evening, when it would have been impossible to sit in the bar anyway.  The party went on until 2am today, and from when we went to bed at 11, until 2am, there were wedding guests backwards and forwards along the corridors, visiting each others' rooms, knocking on each others' doors, kids running about, people shouting, etc.  NOT relaxing.  This was a weekend away that our son had booked as a gift for us.  DON'T go when there is a function on!

ownerResponse.text:
Dear Lynne 

Thank you for taking the trouble to review the hotel following your visit last weekend and I am delighted that you received excellent service and enjoyed the location and  hope that you wil visit us again in the future . 

I note your comments about drinks and dinner ,though buying a three course dinner for around £25.00 per head is really not expensive in the south of England and our drinks are priced similarly to local pubs . We do also offer deals with dinner included which offer best value . 

As regards Weddings , I would like to apologise that you were  disturbed . We are acutely aware that it can cause issue between large parties and individuals and we go to great pains to try and allocate individual guests away from the groups , difficult this weekend when quite exceptionally we had 80 out of 95 guests staying with the Wedding . Normally it is half this or less . 

I do also appreciate that in the very short time between the daytime Wedding function and their evening party , the bar can get very busy , but when I myself left at around 21.30 hrs the bar was almost empty having been busy for about three quarters of an hour . 

We would be delighted to see you back again and if you can find the time  , please give me a call and I will check when we will be able to guarantee you a quieter weekend and see waht I can do for you 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: Nice, clean and picturesque
text:
Stayed at the Hickstead Hotel for a wedding and found it to be very sufficient for our needs. The staff were friendly and the location was lovely. It was convenient to take a taxi wherever needed, some places were within walking distance, if you're up for a scenic hike. Recommend the afternoon tea, it was very nice! Compared to other hotels seen in the area, this was truly a great choice.
Some of the details, like carpeting and paint in public areas of the hotel could be touched up (carpet viewed behind the reception desk) and ceiling in the suite bathroom (also, shower curtain was irritatingly clingy when water was running) but other than that, it was peaceful, quaint and pretty. Beds were comfortable and the suite had lots of room.
Continental breakfast in our room was a great option for a small fee. We didn't try the restaurant because of the wedding festivities but the smell was delicious.
All in all, a brilliant choice and would stay again!

title: Functional & perfectly fine
text:
This place is not somewhere I’d go out of my way to travel for pleasure, but that doesn’t mean I wouldn’t stay here again.

For business this place is perfectly acceptable and the local competition is either a Travelodge or a pub. The rate I paid was very fair for a UK hotel today.

The rooms however are a little dated and the bathrooms look a little tired; and I have to agree with a previous report about poor lighting in the room. Although this wasn’t a problem for me, as I spent very little time in my room. But when I did the bed was comfortable and the room quiet. 

I can’t comment on breakfast, but the dinner I had was OK. Decent whitebait and an average steak, but a poor dessert. The dining room and bar area is clean and function, but (unlike the bedrooms) heavily lit.

The staff are very good though. Polite, helpful, attentive and a credit to the owner.

I’d stay again if in the area.

title: Celebration Evening
text:
Another party evening, this time at the Hickstead Hotel, and not too far from home for a change.

Hotels seems a reasonable place - staff are very good and attentive. Food and entertainment were first class, although the room was not very full (about 30 persons I guess in total).

Rooms are basic but OK - adequate for the price. Only niggle was that the TV remote didn't work, but that was nothing.

The only downside is a rather strange aroma to the place. Strongest in the reception area, one of my companions said it reminded them of an old people's home - not what I would have said, but there is something there.

Location is excellent - just off the A23 means it has good links to Brighton and the coast, as well as Gatwick.

All in all, a good working hotel - not glamourous but they look after the guests and the things that count are done well.

title: Make an excuse to stay here
text:
This is a fantastic hotel - and you should find an excuse to stay here.

In order of growing 'impressiveness':-

1. The restaurant and bar are pleasant and well furnished. There seems to be a few locals that dine in the restaurant as well as the guests - which is always a good sign. Both had a good buzz without being crowded.
2. The rooms looked freshly decorated (in modern greys and browns) and were of a good size. Beds were comfortable and there was free wi-fi that worked well. Everything was spotless.
3. The food in the restaurant was absolutely fantastic. Although I ate there the same night as a large wedding reception was being held in the hotel I was served very quickly (less than 50 minutes from sitting down to finishing a 2 course meal) and the food quality was superb.
4. The staff are astonishingly good here - probably the best level of service I have experienced (an that includes a large number of 4 and 5 star hotels). They provide a very very professional service but they also think about you as a guest. Here are a few examples; 
a. when I booked in they asked me if wanted dinner. When I said that I couldn't as I was working until 02:00 in the morning she asked me what time I was going to sleep to in the morning so she could make sure housekeeping didn't wake me up.
b. After dinner on the second night the waitress reminded me of the times for breakfast. When I said that I would be away working too early to get breakfast - she went out of her way to try and arrange something very early for me.
c. During dinner an elderly couple arrived for dinner. They could not read the menu as they did not have any reading glasses, so the waitress gave them her reading glasses for 5 minutes to read the menu.

All in all a lovely place to stay - with truly world class staff. Unfortunately for me the work I was doing in this area is now complete - so I am looking for an excuse to go back and stay here. 

One minor gripe - Trouser Presses! Trouser presses remind me of some second rate has-been hotel that is grasping for ratings with the English Tourist Board. Does anyone ever use these ridiculous things? The Hickstead doesn't need to grasp for ratings - it already is top class. Please sell them on eBay and use the cash to by an iron and ironing board to stick in the wardrobe.

title: The Easy Getaway...
text:
Situated just off the A23, it was near where we wanted to be. Far enough away from the main roads to be quiet enough to hear the quacking ducks in the nearby pond. Rooms were a decent size; we had the double + single family room with a rear facing aspect. No aircon but the window was sufficient and quiet enough. Looks like a popular special occasion venue, weddings etc. but we were not disturbed by the Wedding in full flight on the Saturday we stayed. There might be a slight concern over proximity to water/open windows for bug life during the summer but as we stayed in autumn, not an issue.
Only issue was with the TV signal dropping out every now and again - but who stays at a hotel to watch the TV? Countered by the excellent free Wi-Fi throughout the hotel (or at least where we went). Overall the hotel is a little dated compared to some 'chain' hotels but was clean and well managed. Breakfast was adequate and makes a change to get waiter service these days (instead of the more common buffet for hot food).
Good value for money. We paid £79 for B&B for the 2 of us booked online. Would stay again.

title: Brilliant weekend
text:
We stayed at The Hickstead for 2 nights (Sat-Sun) in August. We got a great deal through Classic Lodges which got us a free three course dinner in the on-site restaurant and a choice of either cream tea or a bottle of wine. We chose the cream tea. Our second night was just bed and breakfast but that was also a fairly reasonable price as we booked in advance. 
The room was very nice and comfortable. Our bed was very firm, which is not to everyone’s taste but I would rather have that than too soft, and it is better for your back. I slept pretty well. The bathroom was clean and had a great shower. The toiletries were lovely too. The hotel decor is fairly modern and most of the rooms are in a modern extension not the older main building, but we didn’t have a problem with that. The restaurant was really nice and the service was of a very high standard. I suffer from a food allergy and the lady who was serving us was extremely helpful in making sure my food was safe for me to eat. Although I thought my main course was only okay (I had a burger), you could tell the food was fresh and good quality. My pudding (apple pie) was delicious.  Breakfast was also really good. It was plentiful and very tasty. The surrounding countryside is pretty, yet you are a short drive from the main road which is convenient. I’m not sure whether there are places to go close by which you could walk to, we went everywhere by car. Brighton is about half an hour away. 
I would definitely recommend The Hickstead for a comfortable and relaxing stay.

ownerResponse.text:
Thank you for your comments . I am delighted you had a briiliant weekend with us and we look forward to seeing you return in the near future 

Kind Regards 

Giles Paul 
General Manager

title: A chain hotel that thinks it is a family concern
text:
This hotel benefits from its proximity to two places. Hickstead, where many equestrian events take place and Burgess Hill, where commercial pilots train. The relationship between London Gatwick pilot training and the hotel is very good and much of their business is derived from training pilots. I understand their courses last for 40 days; that sounds like good business to me but obviously the hotel had to earn their reputation.
The hotel stands on grounds in the middle of fields without any particular attraction to them a few hundred yards from a main road where traffic is fairly noisy during the day, though not disturbing at night. It is a small hotel, with a restaurant with very good chefs and you were greeted on entry with politeness and helpfulness.
There was no beer on tap when I arrived, and there was no Guinness so I had to make do with a gin and tonic my partner had some bottled beer. For a large gin and tonic, a bottle of beer and two packets of peanuts we paid £12.60 so the prices are pretty standard for mid-level hotels in my experience. I was glad they kept the background music down in the lounge, it is after all called background music not foreground music.
Our room was large enough the shower was delightful and the mattress very comfortable.
We were on special offer £99. double DBB and we did not have to stick to a 'special' menu so we could take our choice from anything on the main bistro menu. That was a big plus. We also had a bottle of very decent wine.
In short, I find this hotel unpretentious, friendly, and does the job. Deborah, our waitress for the breakfast, had been working for the place for seven years and had a deep love and affection for it. Her mother was in charge of room service so it is a family thing. Yes, I would stay there again.
PS they are already booked out for Hickstead next year.

title: Could not be better
text:
Thank you so much to all the wonderful staff here, we stayed a few nights when our new granddaughter was born at Haywards Heath hospital. 
The kindness and service was exceptional (I own my own Travel Agency so I have visited many hotels from hostels to 7* over the years).
It's a good quality 3* in terms of rooms/decor and amenities, but in terms of customer service it's 5* and above.
We felt welcome at all times, the cleaners were among the best that I have ever experienced.
Will we return - yes 100% we will next time we visit our family.
Thank you so much.

title: Quite simply too expensive for what it is!
text:
Overall I would give this two stars and only just that! I will explain:

Character : 0/5. It looks and feels like a Nuffield hospital, but a but more shabby. 

Rooms : 2/5. Decent size, but no coffee table to sit at, just a single chair in the corner and a plastic shower curtain aaaaarrrgh !!!!!!  There is absolutely no excuse fir curtains in any bathrooms, especially ones at £130 a night!

View and grounds : 1/5 ignore the 7 acres, as they only use about 1 which is made up of carpark and a few bushes. The view from my (and many other) windows was of an overflow carpark, a few trees and an I austral unit! See the photo for proof! 

Food and restaurant : 2/5. Even though others have rated this aspect, I have to disagree. Whilst the service and staff were very good, the quality was poor. The atmosphere in the restaurant in the evening was very "bright lights and 80's " Come on Hickstead you can improve that with a bit if thought and effort !!

Location: 4/5. To be fair this is all that is has going for it at present, along with nice supportive staff. Great access to Brighton (15 mins) and easy to find. 

Overall, we were very disappointed with our trip and felt that if it had not been with the Tesco vouchers we would have been absolutely gutted. With them was bad enough!  The hickstead need to be realistic, it is a very small hotel with no character and a dated interior. Not what I want to spend £130 a night on!

ownerResponse.text:

We can of course always improve and indeed aim to do so and I will certainly consider the comments of the shower curtains, which i can appreciate may feel a little outdated. 

Food quality , I have to say is not ever an issue , see many positive comments on hitched.com and late rooms reviews and our own website, but I would be happy to discuss this further with you if you would contact my email with you telephone number .

text: Bit late writing this review!  We got married at the Hickstead in February this year and cannot fault the staff or venue. The staff were unflappable and dealt with leaking babies nappies and vomiting children without fuss and so unobtrusively that those who hadn't seen the "accident" were totally unaware it had occurred.  A small party arrived the night before and we had a fantastic meal in the restaurant.  Rooms were a good size, clean and very pleasant.  Wedding Breakfast was excellent.  Most guests stayed overnight and we all enjoyed an excellent breakfast.  Highly recommend the venue for a place to stay or a wedding.  We will definitely return.

text:

title: Better than expected
text: It's worth noting that this hotel is not in Bolney, and if you take the Bolney exit off the A23 you will get lost! You need to go two junctions further south on the A23 then take the Burgess Hill exit. Once you find it the place is OK, very quiet countryside location, friendly attentive staff and reasonable food. The room was clean, and excellent water pressure in the shower.

title: Nice hotel but lacks character
text: Stayed Thurs/Fri night with my partner using Tesco club card. We felt slightly disappointed on arrival as the Hotel didn't match up to the way it was portrayed on the website. We were expecting the rooms to be within the house itself and not in a purpose built modern annex and expected more to have been made of the "7 acres" on which it is situated. However we thoroughly enjoyed our stay. The rooms were large, clean and well equiped although characterless with little view. The staff were very friendly and helpful. The food in the Bistro was excellent! and the breakfast was very good too. The hotel is well situated for many local attractions within easy reach.

title: Nice Place to Stay
text:
Conveniently located to explore South Downs and Brighton.

We stayed at The Hickstead on the Tesco Clubcard Deals scheme and were not disappointed. Firstly the Classic Lodges web site displays the price of a leisure break clearly and this makes it easy to order your vouchers ( unlike some other hotel chains where the price you pay on the clubcard deals scheme is far more than the price on their web sites).

The rooms at the hotel are fairly characterless but this did not matter to us because:
1. The rooms are a decent size
2. The bed was extremely comfortable
3. The en-suite had a decent bath and nice toiletries
4. Liked the standard items such as tea/coffee machine, hairdryer and trouser press
5. Especially liked free WiFi & Sky Sports on the TV ( although no mini bar )

We were initially worried about the noise from a wedding party but actually never heard a thing all night and always had a feeling that the guests were courteous to each other.

We ate at the hotel one evening and were pleasantly surprised at the quality ( Sea Bass was well cooked and imaginatively presented ) and wished we had eaten there the second night ( don't recommend the cheese and biscuits option though as the cheese wasn't particularly inspiring). Breakfasts were very good with a buffet style range of cereals and fruits and then full English - Delicious.

As for the local area, we enjoyed Borde Hill Gardens ( azaleas and rhododendrons were a delight in May ) and the walks at The Jack and Jill Windmills and Devils Dyke with sweeping views of the Downs.

Overall The Hickstead was a nice place to stay and I would definitely stay there again

title: very relaxing weekend
text: Stayed friday and saturday night nice room , very pleasant and friendly staff . We ate in the Bistro restaurant both nights and really enjoyed the food. We were glad we chose to stay outside Brighton as it was a lovely quiet location. Would definately return.

title: 3 Star hotel 5 STAR SERVICE
text: My wife and I had a brilliant stay. Patricia Cole on reception was outstanding. Room was spotless. Valentine night restaurant was full but the service was still great. Cannot believe this is only a 3 star hotel. I have stayed at much more expensive hotels that were not as good. I will be returning and will recommend this hotel to my friends. Do not confuse this hotel with the Travellodge up the road.

title: Quality evening meals & friendly receptionists
text:
We would like to give positive feedback to the hotel after enjoying a weekend break there, primarily to visit Brighton.
We stayed in one of the two executive superior rooms, which came complete with new decor, bottles of still & sparkling spring water, hygienically-sealed bathrobes and a plate of sliced strawberries, marshmallows & dusted maltesers. Only slight grumble was the view. The bed was very comfortable and we slept soundly on both nights. 
We can honestly say that the staff were all professional: the receptionists and the dining room manager were good; the breakfast service did lack a little consistency around the room. 
The evening meals, prepared at the weekend by the sous-chef, were of a very high quality indeed. The slightly plain descriptions on the menu and the very reasonable prices gave no hint of the delights that awaited you. Everybody seemed pleased with their meals - a very rare experience in any restaurant. The cooked breakfast was generally good and the continental one adequate.
Our stay coincided with The London-Brighton Veteran Car Rally and I would recommend anyone participating in or spectating at this event to give this hotel a try. Don't be put off that it is not in Brighton itself as it is well-located and has free parking. And you will probably get a much better night's sleep than many of my fellow reviewers have done when staying in Brighton!
A good value 3* hotel with friendly staff and great evening meals.

title: We stayed............ for 30 miniutes!
text:
I'd booked the Hickstead for a short Easter weekend break, hoping for a nice dinner on Sunday night, comfortable stay, use of the pool etc on Monday morning, and a lunch before we left for home. No-one greeted us at reception - too busy serving drinks in the bar. Eventually, we were booked in, by a member of staff who couldn't have been more cheery - whilst she was telling us about the amenities - restaurant - closed, and the pool - closed...."oh that's been closed for a while - it's a general maintenance problem!" Then why, we asked, are we here? We'd ordered some bar food [burgers, etc] as that was all there was on offer. We thought about the £80 we'd be spending, booked a cab, and caught a late train home. As we waited the promised 10mins for our cab, Cheery Receptionist asked if we wanted our food! 
I checked the hotel website a few days later and saw they'd sneakily removed the reference to the 'pool'. Still nothing saying the restaurant was "always closed on Sunday evenings!", as the cheery one assured us.
Back home, the welcome was sincere, food delicious, there was no smell of damp, and we went to our local lido the following morning.

title: Quiet
text:
Very Piecefull hotel, cleen rooms, comfortable.

I slept in till 10:50, and made the 11:00 checkout, even with a room at the busy end of the corridorr, overlooking the car park, I was not woken by any noise - you can't say that about many hotels.

I did have the burger on room service, and I eat a lot of burgers, this one was really really bad.

title: Really nice hotel with friendly staff
text:
Stayed for two nights when visiting Brighton in Oct 07.  My daughters also stayed in a twin room for one night.

Given the poor reviews on trip advisor, I was a bit worried about what to expect.  The reviews are totally out of date and the hotel highly recommended.  Really nice friendly reception staff, the room rate was competitive for that area but offers better value.  

The room was very clean and large for a double, nice bathroom with everything you could want.  If you are a very light sleeper, ask for a room upstairs, but otherwise it was very quiet during the night.

Breakfast was the best I’ve had in a long while, huge selection and very helpful staff.  If you like a good cooked breakfast, then this is the place for you.  I do about 40 - 60 hotel nights per year around the world, this was one of the best breakfasts I’ve had for many years.

There is the added advantage of the swimming pool and sauna.  The gym is a mini one, so don’t expect to burn off your breakfast.

Would stay again and recommend for families and business trips as both are catered for very well.

title: very shabby
text: clean rooms, but hotel overall "shabby", and £11.00 for 4 cups of tea!!

title: fantastic
text:
A beautiful hotel for a wonderful weekend away. 
The staff and the building have a very homley feeling, 
comfortable and friendly, excellent cuisine and service, allround a wonderful hotel experience.
I am looking forward to going back soon.

title: Meet Basil here
text:
I strongly recommend the Hickstead Hotel to fans of Fawlty Towers who would like to see what it is like to stay in a tumble down building managed by people who have not a clue about how far they are from the standards freely available from competitors.  

Photographs on various web sites show a nice rural aspect with a wood fence, and describes the hotel as a great base to explore the local countryside.  The hotel is in fact right by the busy A23, and looks out on industrial-looking buildings.

Web sites also describe the hotel as having an indoor pool and a mini gym.  It's hard for two people to swim in the pool at the same time, and the mini gym consists of three or four machines in a room the size of a couple of lavatories.

There is battered paintwork and threadbare carpet everywhere.  To be fair, there is a refurbishment programme under way; but the curtains in the newly done-up rooms don't match the carpets.

The best feature is the food.  It can take a while to arrive (half an hour to be brought any coffee at breakfast), but it is good when it appears.

title: Total bargain! A stay at the Hickstead & a day at Brighton races
text:
We went to the hotel as part of a package day with Brighton Racecourse, which is 15 minutes away.

It was the most incredible value, £55 per person for overnight accomodation, breakfast and a day at the races.

I wasn't expecting the hotel to be very good for the price we paid, but it was excellent! The staff were friendly and helpful and the room a good size. The decor was a little bit dated but clean, but I understand from the staff that a refurbishment is about to start.

There was a good choice for breakfast and the hotel was in a lovely quiet spot.

I have told so many people about it, our stay was such good value!

Shame that our luck at the races wasn't as good as with the hotel!!

title: Brilliant value, excellent staff
text: The beautiful setting is to be expected for this part of the world, but what makes this hotel exceptional is the "Can-do" attitude and friendly helpfulness displayed by every staff member.  The rooms are clean, comfortable and well appointed, the quiet location engenders a restful ambience, yet it is quite close to the London-Brighton motorway.  Easily the best value for many miles around, as a frequent business traveller in need of a little serenity, I have made this hotel a regular stopping point.
